1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: Ha-Meem. (Alphabets of the Arabic Language – Allah, and to whomever he reveals, know their precise meanings.)

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: Ha-Mim. [These letters are one of the miracles of the Quran, and none but Allah (Alone) knows their meanings].

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: The revelation of this Book is from God the all-mighty and all-knowing,

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: The revelation of the Book is from Allah, the Most Honourable, the All Knowing.

2.author: Arberry
2.text: The sending down of the Book is from God the All-mighty, the All-knowing,

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: The revelation of the Book is from Allah, the Mighty, the Knower. Forgiver of sin, and Accepter of repentance, severe in chastisement, Lord of power. There is no god but He; Unto Him is the journeying.

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: The revelation of the Book (this Quran) is from Allah the All-Mighty, the All-Knower.

2.author: Itani
2.text: The sending down of the Scripture is from God the Almighty, the Omniscient.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: This Book is a revelation from Allah, the All-Mighty, the All-Knowing;

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: The revelation of the Book is from Allah, the Almighty, the All-Knower.

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: The revelation of the Scripture is from Allah, the Mighty, the Knower,

2.author: Qarai
2.text: The [gradual] sending down of the Book is from Allah, the All-mighty, the All-knowing,

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: The sending down of the Book is from Allah, the Almighty, the Knower.

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: The revelation of the Book is from Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Knowing.

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: This Book is a revelation from God, the Majestic and All-knowing

2.author: Shakir
2.text: The revelation of the Book is from Allah, the Mighty, the Knowing,

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: This Book is revealed by God, the Almighty, the All Knowing.

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: The revelation of this Book is from Allah, Exalted in Power, Full of Knowledge,-

2.author: Transliteration
2.text: Tanzeelu alkitabi mina Allahi alAAazeezi alAAaleemi

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: Forgiver of trespasses, acceptor of repentance, severe of retribution, lord of power. There is no god but He. Towards Him is your destination.

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: The Forgiver of sin, and the Most Acceptor of Repentance, the Severe in Punishing, the Greatly Rewarding; there is no God except Him; towards Him only is the return.

3.author: Arberry
3.text: Forgiver of sins, Accepter of penitence, Terrible in retribution, the Bountiful; there is no god but He, and unto Him is the homecoming.

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: Forgiver of sin, and Acceptor of repentance severe in chastisement, Lord of Power. There is no god but He; unto Him is the journeying.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: The Forgiver of sin, the Acceptor of repentance, the Severe in punishment, the Bestower (of favours), La ilaha illa Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He), to Him is the final return.

3.author: Itani
3.text: Forgiver of sins, Accepter of repentance, Severe in punishment, Bountiful in bounty. There is no god but He. To Him is the ultimate return.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: the Forgiver of sins, the Accepter of repentance, the Stern in retribution, the Bountiful. There is no god but He. To Him are all destined to return.

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: The Forgiver of sin, the Acceptor of repentance, the Severe in punishment, the Bestower. La ilaha illa Huwa, to Him is the final return.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: The Forgiver of sin, the Accepter of repentance, the Stern in punishment, the Bountiful. There is no Allah save Him. Unto Him is the journeying.

3.author: Qarai
3.text: forgiver of sins and acceptor of repentance, severe in retribution, [yet] all-bountiful; there is no god except Him, [and] toward Him is the destination.

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: The Forgiver of sins and the Acceptor of repentance. Stern in retribution, the Bountiful, there is no god except He and to Him is the arrival.

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: The forgiver of sin, acceptor of repentance, severe in punishment, owner of abundance. There is no deity except Him; to Him is the destination.

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: who forgives sins, who accepts repentance, whose punishment is severe, and whose bounty is universal. He is the only Lord and to Him all things proceed.

3.author: Shakir
3.text: The Forgiver of the faults and the Acceptor of repentance, Severe to punish, Lord of bounty; there is no god but He; to Him is the eventual coming.

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: The Forgiver of sin and the Accepter of repentance, who is severe in punishment and Infinite in His Bounty. There is no God but Him. All shall return to Him.

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: Who forgiveth sin, accepteth repentance, is strict in punishment, and hath a long reach (in all things). there is no god but He: to Him is the final goal.

3.author: Transliteration
3.text: Ghafiri alththanbi waqabili alttawbi shadeedi alAAiqabi thee alttawli la ilaha illa huwa ilayhi almaseeru

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: Only the unbelievers dispute the revelations of God. So do not let their activities in the land deceive you.

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: None except the disbelievers dispute the signs of Allah, therefore do not let their free movements in the land deceive you.

4.author: Arberry
4.text: None but the unbelievers dispute concerning the signs of God; so let not their going to and fro in the land delude thee.

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: None dispute concerning the revelations of Allah save those who disbelieve, so let not beguile thee their going about in the cities.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: None disputes in the Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) of Allah but those who disbelieve. So let not their ability of going about here and there through the land (for their purposes) deceive you [O Muhammad SAW, their ultimate end will be the Fire of Hell]!

4.author: Itani
4.text: None argues against God’s revelations except those who disbelieve. So do not be impressed by their activities in the land.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: None but the unbelievers dispute regarding the Signs of Allah. So let not their strutting about in the land delude you.

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: None disputes in the Ayat of Allah but those who disbelieve. So let not their ability of going about here and there through the land (for their purposes) deceive you!

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: None argue concerning the revelations of Allah save those who disbelieve, so let not their turn of fortune in the land deceive thee (O Muhammad).

4.author: Qarai
4.text: No one disputes the signs of Allah except the faithless. So do not be misled by their bustle in the towns.

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: None but the unbelievers dispute concerning the verses of Allah. So do not be deluded by their going to and fro in the land.

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: No one disputes concerning the signs of Allah except those who disbelieve, so be not deceived by their [uninhibited] movement throughout the land.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: No one disputes the revelations of the Lord except the disbelievers. Let not their activities in the land deceive you.

4.author: Shakir
4.text: None dispute concerning the communications of Allah but those who disbelieve, therefore let not their going to and fro in the cities deceive you.

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: Only those who deny the truth dispute God's signs. Do not let their activity in the land deceive you.

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: None can dispute about the Signs of Allah but the Unbelievers. Let not, then, their strutting about through the land deceive thee!

4.author: Transliteration
4.text: Ma yujadilu fee ayati Allahi illa allatheena kafaroo fala yaghrurka taqallubuhum fee albiladi

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: The people of Noah had denied before them, and many factions after them. Every nation has intrigued against its apostle and afflicted him, and argued with false arguments to condemn the truth. Then I seized them. How was then My retribution!

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: Before them, the people of Nooh and the groups after them had denied; and every nation intended to apprehend its Noble Messenger, and they fought along with falsehood to avert the truth, so I seized them; so how did My punishment turn out?

5.author: Arberry
5.text: The people of Noah before them also cried lies, and the parties after them; every nation purposed against their Messenger to seize him, and disputed with falsehood that they might rebut thereby the truth. Then I seized them; and how was My retribution!

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: The people of Nuh and the confederates after them belied their apostles before these, and each disbelieving community advanced toward their apostle that they may lay hold of him and disputed with vain speech that they may confute the truth thereby. Wherefore I laid hold of them; and how terrible was My chastisement!

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: The people of Nuh (Noah) and the confederates after them denied (their Messengers) before these, and every (disbelieving) nation plotted against their Messenger to seize him, and disputed by means of falsehood to refute therewith the truth. So I seized them (with punishment), and how (terrible) was My punishment!

5.author: Itani
5.text: Before them the people of Noah rejected the truth, as did the confederates after them. Every community plotted against their messenger, to capture him. And they argued with falsehood, to defeat with it the truth. But I seized them. What a punishment it was!

5.author: Maududi
5.text: Before them the people of Noah also gave the lie (to Messengers), and so did many parties after them. Each nation sallied forth against its Messenger to seize him, and they disputed with false arguments seeking therewith to repudiate the Truth. Then I seized them; and behold, how woeful was My retribution!

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: The people of Nuh and the groups after them denied before these; and every nation plotted against their Messenger to seize him, and disputed by means of falsehood to refute therewith the truth. So I seized them, and how was My punishment!

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: The folk of Noah and the factions after them denied (their messengers) before these, and every nation purposed to seize their messenger and argued falsely, (thinking) thereby to refute the Truth. Then I seized them, and how (awful) was My punishment.

5.author: Qarai
5.text: The people of Noah denied before them and the [heathen] factions [who came] after them. Every nation attempted to lay hands on their apostle, and disputed erroneously to refute the truth. Then I seized them; so how was My retribution?!

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: The nation of Noah before them belied, and so did the parties after them. Every nation strove against their Messenger to seize him, disputing with false arguments to refute the truth. Then I seized them, how was My punishment!

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: The people of Noah denied before them and the [disbelieving] factions after them, and every nation intended [a plot] for their messenger to seize him, and they disputed by [using] falsehood to [attempt to] invalidate thereby the truth. So I seized them, and how [terrible] was My penalty.

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: The people of Noah who lived before and the Confederate tribes who lived after them rejected Our revelations. Every nation schemed against its Messengers to seize them and disputed against them to defeat the truth. But torment struck them and how terrible was their retribution!

5.author: Shakir
5.text: The people of Nuh and the parties after them rejected (prophets) before them, and every nation purposed against their apostle to destroy him, and they disputed by means of the falsehood that they might thereby render null the truth, therefore I destroyed them; how was then My retribution!

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: The people of Noah and later factions also rejected the truth and every community plotted against the messenger sent to them, aiming to lay hands on him, and they contended [against his message] with fallacious arguments, so that they might defeat the truth, therefore I seized them. How terrible was My punishment.

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: But (there were people) before them, who denied (the Signs),- the People of Noah, and the Confederates (of Evil) after them; and every People plotted against their prophet, to seize him, and disputed by means of vanities, therewith to condemn the Truth; but it was I that seized them! and how (terrible) was My Requital!

5.author: Transliteration
5.text: Kaththabat qablahum qawmu noohin waalahzabu min baAAdihim wahammat kullu ommatin birasoolihim liyakhuthoohu wajadaloo bialbatili liyudhidoo bihi alhaqqa faakhathtuhum fakayfa kana AAiqabi

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: In this way the sentence of your Lord against the infidels that they would be the inmates of Hell, was justified.

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: And this is how the Word of your Lord has proved true upon the disbelievers that they are people of the hell.

6.author: Arberry
6.text: Even so the Word of thy Lord was realised against the unbelievers, that they are the inhabitants of the Fire.

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: And thus hath the Word of thy Lord been justified on those who disbelieve: that they shall be the fellows of the Fire.

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: Thus has the Word of your Lord been justified against those who disbelieved, that they will be the dwellers of the Fire.

6.author: Itani
6.text: Thus the sentence of your Lord became realized against those who disbelieve, that they are to be inmates of the Fire.

6.author: Maududi
6.text: Thus has the decree of your Lord become due against the unbelievers. They are destined for the Fire.

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: Thus has the Word of your Lord been justified against those who disbelieved, that they will be the dwellers of the Fire.

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: Thus was the word of thy Lord concerning those who disbelieve fulfilled: That they are owners of the Fire.

6.author: Qarai
6.text: That is how the word of your Lord became due concerning the faithless, that they shall be inmates of the Fire.

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: And so it is that the Word of your Lord shall be realized against the unbelievers they are the inhabitants of the Fire.

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: And thus has the word of your Lord come into effect upon those who disbelieved that they are companions of the Fire.

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: The word of your Lord that the disbelievers will be the dwellers of hell fire has already been decreed.

6.author: Shakir
6.text: And thus did the word of your Lord prove true against those who disbelieved that they are the inmates of the fire.

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: Thus has the word of your Lord come true against the deniers; they shall be the inmates of the Fire.

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: Thus was the Decree of thy Lord proved true against the Unbelievers; that truly they are Companions of the Fire!

6.author: Transliteration
6.text: Wakathalika haqqat kalimatu rabbika AAala allatheena kafaroo annahum ashabu alnnari

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: The bearers of the Throne, and those around it, sing the praises of their Lord and believe in Him, and seek forgiveness for those who believe: "O our Lord, Your mercy and knowledge embrace everything; so forgive those who turn to You in repentance and follow Your path; and preserve them from the torment of Hell.

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: Those* who bear the Throne, and those who are around it, say the Purity of their Lord while praising Him, and they believe in Him and seek forgiveness for the believers; “O Our Lord! Your mercy and knowledge encompass all things, therefore forgive those who repented and followed Your path, and save them from the punishment of hell.” (* The angels.)

7.author: Arberry
7.text: Those who bear the Throne, and those round about it proclaim the praise of their Lord, and believe in Him, and they ask forgiveness for those who believe: 'Our Lord, Thou embracest every thing in mercy and knowledge; therefore forgive those who have repented, and follow Thy way, and guard them against the chastisement of Hell.

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: Those who bear the Throne and those who are round about it, hallow the praise of their Lord and believe in Him and ask forgiveness for those who believe, saying: our Lord! Thou comprehendest everything in mercy and knowledge, wherefore forgive these who repent and follow Thine path, and protect them from the torment of the Flaming Fire.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: Those (angels) who bear the Throne (of Allah) and those around it glorify the praises of their Lord, and believe in Him, and ask forgiveness for those who believe (in the Oneness of Allah) (saying): "Our Lord! You comprehend all things in mercy and knowledge, so forgive those who repent and follow Your Way, and save them from the torment of the blazing Fire!

7.author: Itani
7.text: Those who carry the Throne, and those around it, glorify their Lord with praise, and believe in Him, and ask for forgiveness for those who believe: “Our Lord, You have encompassed everything in mercy and knowledge; so forgive those who repent and follow Your path, and protect them from the agony of the Blaze.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: The angels that bear the Throne and those that are around to extol your Lord's glory with His praise, they believe in Him, and ask forgiveness for the believers, saying: “Our Lord! You encompass everything with Your Mercy and Knowledge. So forgive those that repent and follow Your Path, and guard them against the chastisement of Hell.

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: Those (angels) who bear the Throne (of Allah) and those around it glorify the praises of their Lord, and believe in Him, and ask forgiveness for those who believe (saying): "Our Lord! You comprehend all things in mercy and knowledge, so forgive those who repent and follow Your way, and save them from the torment of the blazing Fire!

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: Those who bear the Throne, and all who are round about it, hymn the praises of their Lord and believe in Him and ask forgiveness for those who believe (saying): Our Lord! Thou comprehendest all things in mercy and knowledge, therefor forgive those who repent and follow Thy way. Ward off from them the punishment of hell.

7.author: Qarai
7.text: Those who bear the Throne, and those who are around it, celebrate the praise of their Lord and have faith in Him, and they plead for forgiveness for the faithful: ‘Our Lord! You embrace all things in mercy and knowledge. So forgive those who repent and follow Your way and save them from the punishment of hell.

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: Those who bear the Throne and those around it exalt with the praise of their Lord and believe in Him. They ask forgiveness for the believers (saying): 'Our Lord, You embrace all things in mercy and knowledge. Forgive those who repent and follow Your Way. Shield them against the punishment of Hell.

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: Those [angels] who carry the Throne and those around it exalt [Allah] with praise of their Lord and believe in Him and ask forgiveness for those who have believed, [saying], "Our Lord, You have encompassed all things in mercy and knowledge, so forgive those who have repented and followed Your way and protect them from the punishment of Hellfire.

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: The bearers of the Throne glorify their Lord with His praise. They believe in Him and ask Him to forgive the believers. They say, "Our Lord, Your mercy and knowledge encompass all things. Forgive those who turn to You in repentance and follow Your path. Lord, save them from the torment of hell.

7.author: Shakir
7.text: Those who bear the power and those around Him celebrate the praise of their Lord and believe in Him and ask protection for those who believe: Our Lord! Thou embracest all things in mercy and knowledge, therefore grant protection to those who turn (to Thee) and follow Thy way, and save them from the punishment of the hell:

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: Those who bear the Throne, and those who are around it, glorify their Lord with His praise, and believe in Him. They ask forgiveness for those who believe, saying, "Our Lord, You embrace all things in mercy and knowledge. Forgive those who turn to You and follow Your path. Save them from the punishment of Hell

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: Those who sustain the Throne (of Allah) and those around it Sing Glory and Praise to their Lord; believe in Him; and implore Forgiveness for those who believe: "Our Lord! Thy Reach is over all things, in Mercy and Knowledge. Forgive, then, those who turn in Repentance, and follow Thy Path; and preserve them from the Penalty of the Blazing Fire!

7.author: Transliteration
7.text: Allatheena yahmiloona alAAarsha waman hawlahu yusabbihoona bihamdi rabbihim wayuminoona bihi wayastaghfiroona lillatheena amanoo rabbana wasiAAta kulla shayin rahmatan waAAilman faighfir lillatheena taboo waittabaAAoo sabeelaka waqihim AAathaba aljaheemi

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: Admit them, O Lord, to the garden of Eden which You promised them, and those of their fathers, spouses and progeny who are upright. You are truly all-mighty and all-wise.

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: “O Our Lord! And admit them into the Gardens of everlasting stay which you have promised them, and those who are virtuous among their forefathers and their wives and their offspring; indeed You only are the Most Honourable, the Wise.”

8.author: Arberry
8.text: Our Lord, and admit them to the Gardens of Eden that Thou hast promised them and those who were righteous of their fathers, and their wives, and their seed; surely Thou art the All-mighty, the All-wise.

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: Our Lord! make them enter the Everlasting Gardens Which Thou hast promised them, and also such of their fathers and their spouses and their offspring as verify Thou: Thou art the Mighty, the Wise.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: "Our Lord! And make them enter the 'Adn (Eden) Paradise (everlasting Gardens) which you have promised them, and to the righteous among their fathers, their wives, and their offspring! Verily, You are the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

8.author: Itani
8.text: And admit them, Our Lord, into the Gardens of Eternity, which You have promised them, and the righteous among their parents, and their spouses, and their offspring. You are indeed the Almighty, the Most Wise.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: Our Lord, admit them to the everlasting Gardens You have promised them and those of their fathers and spouses and progeny that were righteous. Surely You alone are Most Mighty, Most Wise;

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: Our Lord! And make them enter the `Adn (Eternal) Gardens which you have promised them -- and to the righteous among their fathers, their wives, and their offspring! Verily, You are the Almighty, the All-Wise.

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: Our Lord! And make them enter the Gardens of Eden which thou hast promised them, with such of their fathers and their wives and their descendants as do right. Lo! Thou, only Thou, art the Mighty, the Wise.

8.author: Qarai
8.text: Our Lord! Admit them into the Gardens of Eden, which You have promised them, along with whoever is righteous among their forebears, their spouses and their descendants. Indeed You are the All-mighty, the All-wise.

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: Admit them, Our Lord, to the Gardens of Eden which You have promised them, together with those who were righteous among their fathers, their wives, and their offspring. You are the Almighty, the Wise,

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: Our Lord, and admit them to gardens of perpetual residence which You have promised them and whoever was righteous among their fathers, their spouses and their offspring. Indeed, it is You who is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: Lord, admit them and their fathers, spouses, and offspring who have reformed themselves to the gardens of Eden which You have promised them. You are Majestic and All-wise.

8.author: Shakir
8.text: Our Lord! and make them enter the gardens of perpetuity which Thou hast promised to them and those who do good of their fathers and their wives and their offspring, surely Thou are the Mighty, the Wise.

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: and admit them, Lord, to the Eternal Garden You have promised to them, together with their righteous ancestors, spouses, and offspring: You alone are the Almighty; the All Wise.

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: "And grant, our Lord! that they enter the Gardens of Eternity, which Thou hast promised to them, and to the righteous among their fathers, their wives, and their posterity! For Thou art (He), the Exalted in Might, Full of Wisdom.

8.author: Transliteration
8.text: Rabbana waadkhilhum jannati AAadnin allatee waAAadtahum waman salaha min abaihim waazwajihim wathurriyyatihim innaka anta alAAazeezu alhakeemu

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: Protect them from evil; and whosoever You preserve from evil on that Day shall have surely received Your mercy. This will be the great triumph."

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: “And save them from the evil consequences of sins; and whomever You save from the evil consequences of sins on that Day – so You have indeed had mercy upon him; and this only is the greatest success.”

9.author: Arberry
9.text: And guard them against evil deeds; whomsoever Thou guardest against evil deeds on that day, on him Thou hast had mercy; and that is indeed the mighty triumph.

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: And protect them from evils. And whosoever Thou shalt protect from evils on that Day, him Thou hast of a surety taken into mercy and that: it is an achievement mighty.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: "And save them from (the punishment, because of what they did of) the sins, and whomsoever You save from (the punishment, because of what they did of) the sins (i.e. excuse them) that Day, him verily, You have taken into mercy." And that is the supreme success.

9.author: Itani
9.text: And shield them from the evil deeds. Whomever You shield from the evil deeds, on that Day, You have had mercy on him. That is the supreme achievement.”

9.author: Maududi
9.text: and guard them against all ills. He whom You guard against ills on that Day, to him You have surely been Most Merciful. That is the great triumph.”

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: And save them from the sins, and whomsoever You save from the sins that Day, him verily, You have taken into mercy." And that is the supreme success.

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: And ward off from them ill-deeds; and he from whom Thou wardest off ill-deeds that day, him verily hast Thou taken into mercy. That is the supreme triumph.

9.author: Qarai
9.text: Save them from the ills [of the Day of Retribution]; and whomever You save from the ills that day, You will have had mercy upon him, and that is the great triumph.’

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: and guard them against all evil deeds. Whosoever You guarded against evil deeds on that Day, surely, You have had mercy upon them, and that is indeed the mighty triumph'

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: And protect them from the evil consequences [of their deeds]. And he whom You protect from evil consequences that Day - You will have given him mercy. And that is the great attainment."

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: Lord, keep them away from evil deeds. Whomever You have saved from evil on the Day of Judgment has certainly been granted Your mercy and this is the greatest triumph.

9.author: Shakir
9.text: And keep them from evil deeds, and whom Thou keepest from evil deeds this day, indeed Thou hast mercy on him, and that is the mighty achievement.

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: Protect them from all evil deeds: those You protect from [the punishment for] evil deeds will receive Your mercy -- that is the supreme success."

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: "And preserve them from (all) ills; and any whom Thou dost preserve from ills that Day,- on them wilt Thou have bestowed Mercy indeed: and that will be truly (for them) the highest Achievement".

9.author: Transliteration
9.text: Waqihimu alssayyiati waman taqi alssayyiati yawmaithin faqad rahimtahu wathalika huwa alfawzu alAAatheemu

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: Those who disbelieve will certainly be told: "God's displeasure was greater than your disgust of your selves when you were called to belief and refused to believe."

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: The disbelievers will indeed be called out to – “Indeed Allah’s disgust with you is greater than your own abhorrence of yourselves, whereas you used to deny when you were called towards the faith!”

10.author: Arberry
10.text: It shall be proclaimed to the unbelievers, 'Surely God's hatred is greater than your hatred one of another, when you were called unto belief, and disbelieved.'

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: Verily those who disbelieve-they will be cried Unto: surely Allah's abhorrence was greater than Is your abhorrence toward yourselves when ye were called Unto the belief, and ye disbelieved.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: Those who disbelieve will be addressed (at the time of entering into the Fire): "Allah's aversion was greater towards you (in the worldly life when you used to reject the Faith) than your aversion towards one another (now in the Fire of Hell, as you are now enemies to one another), when you were called to the Faith but you used to refuse."

10.author: Itani
10.text: Those who disbelieved will be addressed, “The loathing of God is greater than your loathing of yourselves—for you were invited to the faith, but you refused.”

10.author: Maududi
10.text: It will be announced to the unbelievers (on the Day of Resurrection): “Surely Allah's abhorrence of you when you were called to believe but you disbelieved was greater than is your abhorrence of yourselves today.”

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: Those who disbelieve will be addressed: "Indeed, Allah's aversion was greater towards you than your aversion toward yourselves, when you were called to the Faith but you used to refuse."

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: Lo! (on that day) those who disbelieve are informed by proclamation: Verily Allah's abhorrence is more terrible than your abhorrence one of another, when ye were called unto the faith but did refuse.

10.author: Qarai
10.text: It will be proclaimed to the faithless: ‘Surely, Allah’s outrage [towards you] is greater than your outrage towards yourselves, as you were invited to faith, but you disbelieved.’

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: It shall be proclaimed to the unbelievers: 'Surely, the hatred of Allah for you is greater than your hatred of yourselves. You were called to belief but disbelieved.'

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: Indeed, those who disbelieve will be addressed, "The hatred of Allah for you was [even] greater than your hatred of yourselves [this Day in Hell] when you were invited to faith, but you refused."

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: The disbelievers will be told, "God's hatred towards you is much greater than your hatred of your own selves. You were called to the faith but you disbelieved".

10.author: Shakir
10.text: Surely those who disbelieve shall be cried out to: Certainly Allah's hatred (of you) when you were called upon to the faith and you rejected, is much greater than your hatred of yourselves.

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: Those who deny the truth will be told, "God's abhorrence of you is greater than your hatred of yourselves. You were called to the faith but you denied it."

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: The Unbelievers will be addressed: "Greater was the aversion of Allah to you than (is) your aversion to yourselves, seeing that ye were called to the Faith and ye used to refuse."

10.author: Transliteration
10.text: Inna allatheena kafaroo yunadawna lamaqtu Allahi akbaru min maqtikum anfusakum ith tudAAawna ila aleemani fatakfuroona

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: They say: "O Lord, twice You made us die, and twice You made us live. We admit our sins. Is there still a way out for us?"

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: They will say, “O Our Lord! Twice You have given us death and twice You have given us life* – we now confess to our sins – so is there a way out of the fire?” (From nothingness to life in this world, to death and then Resurrection.)

11.author: Arberry
11.text: They shall say, 'Our Lord, Thou hast caused us to die two deaths and Thou hast given us twice to live; now we confess our sins. Is there any way to go forth?'

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: They will say: our Lord! Thou hast made us die twice, and Thou hast made us live twice now We confess our sins: there no getting out any way?

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: They will say: "Our Lord! You have made us to die twice (i.e. we were dead in the loins of our fathers and dead after our deaths in this world), and You have given us life twice (i.e. life when we were born and life when we are Resurrected)! Now we confess our sins, then is there any way to get out (of the Fire)?"

11.author: Itani
11.text: They will say, “Our Lord, you made us die twice, and twice you gave us life. Now we acknowledge our sins. Is there any way out?”

11.author: Maududi
11.text: They will say: “Our Lord, twice have You caused us to die and twice have You given us life. We have now confessed our sins. Is there, then, any way out?”

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: They will say: "Our Lord! You have made us to die twice, and You have given us life twice! Now we confess our sins, then is there any way to get out"

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: They say: Our Lord! Twice hast Thou made us die, and twice hast Thou made us live. Now we confess our sins. Is there any way to go out?

11.author: Qarai
11.text: They will say, ‘Our Lord! Twice did You make us die, and twice did You give us life. We admit our sins. Is there any way out [from this plight]?’

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: They shall say: 'Our Lord, twice You have made us die, and twice You have given us life, now, we confess our sins. Is there any way we can be brought out of this'

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: They will say, "Our Lord, You made us lifeless twice and gave us life twice, and we have confessed our sins. So is there to an exit any way?"

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: They say, "Lord, You have caused us to die twice and You have brought us back to life twice. We have confessed our sins, so is there any way out of this (hell)?"

11.author: Shakir
11.text: They shall say: Our Lord! twice didst Thou make us subject to death, and twice hast Thou given us life, so we do confess our faults; is there then a way to get out?

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: They will say, "Our Lord! Twice You have made us die, and twice You have given us life! Now we have confessed our sins: is there any way out [of this]?"

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: They will say: "Our Lord! twice hast Thou made us without life, and twice hast Thou given us Life! Now have we recognised our sins: Is there any way out (of this)?"

11.author: Transliteration
11.text: Qaloo rabbana amattana ithnatayni waahyaytana ithnatayni faiAAtarafna bithunoobina fahal ila khuroojin min sabeelin

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: This has come upon you because when God alone was invoked you disbelieved; but when partners were associated with Him, you believed. But judgement belongs to God, the all-high and supreme.

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: “This has occurred because when Allah the One was prayed to, you used to disbelieve; and when a partner was ascribed with Him, you used to believe; so the command is only for Allah, the Supreme, the Great.”

12.author: Arberry
12.text: That is because, when God was called to alone, you disbelieved; but if others are associated with Him, then you believe. Judgment belongs to God, the All-high, the All-great.

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: That is because when Allah alone was called upon ye disbelieved, and when some one was associated with Him ye believed. So the judgment is of Allah, the Exalted, the Great.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: (It will be said): "This is because, when Allah Alone was invoked (in worship, etc.) you disbelieved, but when partners were joined to Him, you believed! So the judgement is only with Allah, the Most High, the Most Great!"

12.author: Itani
12.text: That is because when God alone was called upon, you disbelieved; but when others were associated with Him, you believed. Judgment rests with God the Sublime, the Majestic.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: (They will be told): “(The cause of your present state is that) when Allah alone was invoked, you disbelieved; and when others instead of Him were invoked, you believed. Today all judgement lies with Allah, the Most High, the All-Great.”

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: (It will be said): "This is because, when Allah Alone was invoked, you disbelieved; but when partners were joined to Him, you believed! So the judgement is only with Allah, the Most High, the Most Great!"

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: (It is said unto them): This is (your plight) because, when Allah only was invoked, ye disbelieved, but when some partner was ascribed to Him ye were believing. But the command belongeth only to Allah, the Sublime, the Majestic.

12.author: Qarai
12.text: [They will be told,] ‘This [plight of yours] is because, when Allah was invoked alone, you would disbelieve, but if partners were ascribed to Him you would believe. So the judgment belongs to Allah, the All-exalted, the All-great.’

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: (They shall be answered): 'That is because you disbelieved when Allah alone was supplicated to, but when others were associated with Him you believed.' Judgement belongs to Allah, the Most High, the Great.

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: [They will be told], "That is because, when Allah was called upon alone, you disbelieved; but if others were associated with Him, you believed. So the judgement is with Allah, the Most High, the Grand."

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: They will be answered, "Your suffering is only because you disbelieved when One God was mentioned. When other things were considered equal to Him, you believed in them. Judgment belongs to God, the Most High, the Most Great.

12.author: Shakir
12.text: That is because when Allah alone was called upon, you disbelieved, and when associates were given to Him, you believed; so judgment belongs to Allah, the High, the Great.

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: [They will be told], "This is because when God alone was invoked you denied the truth, yet when others were associated with Him you believed in them." Judgement rests with God, the Most High, the Most Great.

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: (The answer will be:) "This is because, when Allah was invoked as the Only (object of worship), ye did reject Faith, but when partners were joined to Him, ye believed! the Command is with Allah, Most High, Most Great!"

12.author: Transliteration
12.text: Thalikum biannahu itha duAAiya Allahu wahdahu kafartum wain yushrak bihi tuminoo faalhukmu lillahi alAAaliyyi alkabeeri

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: It is He who shows you His signs, and sends you food from the heavens. Yet none takes a warning except him who turns to Him.

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: It is He Who shows you His signs, and sends down sustenance from the sky for you; and none accept guidance except those who incline (towards Him).

13.author: Arberry
13.text: It is He who shows you His signs and sends down to you out of heaven provision; yet none remembers but he who repents.

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: He it is who sheweth you His signs and sendeth down for you from the heaven provision. And none receiveth admonition save him who turneth in penitence.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: It is He, Who shows you His Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) and sends down (rain with which grows) provision for you from the sky. And none remembers but those who turn (to Allah) in obedience and in repentance (by begging His Pardon and by worshipping and obeying Him Alone and none else).

13.author: Itani
13.text: It is He who shows you His wonders, and sends down sustenance from the sky for you. But none pays heed except the repentant.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: He it is Who shows you His Signs and sends down provision for you from the sky. Yet none takes heed except he who constantly turns to Allah.

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: It is He Who shows you His Ayat and sends down provision for you from the sky. And none remembers but those who turn in repentance.

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: He it is Who showeth you His portents, and sendeth down for you provision from the sky. None payeth heed save him who turneth (unto Him) repentant.

13.author: Qarai
13.text: It is He who shows you His signs and sends down provision for you from the sky. Yet no one takes admonition except those who return penitently [to Allah].

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: It is He who shows to you His signs and sends down your provision from the sky. Yet no one remembers except he who is penitent.

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: It is He who shows you His signs and sends down to you from the sky, provision. But none will remember except he who turns back [in repentance].

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: It is He who has shown you the evidence of His existence and has sent you sustenance from the sky, yet only those who turn to God in repentance take heed.

13.author: Shakir
13.text: He it is Who shows you His signs and sends down for you sustenance from heaven, and none minds but he who turns (to Him) again and again.

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: It is He who shows you His signs, and sends down provision for you from heaven; but none pays heed except the repentant.

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: He it is Who showeth you his Signs, and sendeth down sustenance for you from the sky: but only those receive admonition who turn (to Allah).

13.author: Transliteration
13.text: Huwa allathee yureekum ayatihi wayunazzilu lakum mina alssamai rizqan wama yatathakkaru illa man yuneebu

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: So call on God with exclusive obedience, howsoever the unbelievers may dislike it.

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: Therefore worship Allah, as His sincere bondmen even if the disbelievers get annoyed.

14.author: Arberry
14.text: So call unto God, making your religion His sincerely, though the unbelievers be averse.

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: Wherefore call Unto Allah, making religion for Him pure, though the infidels be averse.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: So, call you (O Muhammad SAW and the believers) upon (or invoke) Allah making (your) worship pure for Him (Alone) (by worshipping none but Him and by doing religious deeds sincerely for Allah's sake only and not to show-off and not to set up rivals with Him in worship). However much the disbelievers (in the Oneness of Allah) may hate (it).

14.author: Itani
14.text: So call upon God, with sincere devotion to Him, even though the disbelievers resent it.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: So call upon Allah, consecrating all your devotion to Him, howsoever much the unbelievers may dislike it.

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: So, call you upon Allah making religion sincerely for Him, however much the disbelievers may hate.

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: Therefor (O believers) pray unto Allah, making religion pure for Him (only), however much the disbelievers be averse -

14.author: Qarai
14.text: So supplicate Allah, putting exclusive faith in Him, though the faithless should be averse.

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: Supplicate then to Allah making your religion sincerely His, even though the unbelievers oppose it.

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: So invoke Allah, [being] sincere to Him in religion, although the disbelievers dislike it.

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: Worship God and be devoted to His religion even though the disbelievers dislike this.

14.author: Shakir
14.text: Therefore call upon Allah, being sincere to Him in obedience, though the unbelievers are averse:

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: Therefore call upon God, making faith pure for Him, averse as the deniers of the truth may be to it:

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: Call ye, then, upon Allah with sincere devotion to Him, even though the Unbelievers may detest it.

14.author: Transliteration
14.text: FaodAAoo Allaha mukhliseena lahu alddeena walaw kariha alkafiroona

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: Most exalted of position, Lord of power, He directs inspiration by His command to any of His creatures as He will, to warn (men) of the Day of Meeting,

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: Bestower of High Ranks, Owner of the Throne; it is He Who instils the spirit of faith into the one He wills among His bondmen, in order that he may warn of the Day of Meeting. –

15.author: Arberry
15.text: Exalter of ranks is He, Possessor of the Throne, casting the Spirit of His bidding upon whomever He will of His servants, that he may warn them of the Day of Encounter,

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: He is Lofty in degrees, Lord of the Throne. He casteth the spirit of His command upon whomsoever He will of His bondmen, that he may warn people of the Day of Meeting.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: (He is Allah) Owner of High Ranks and Degrees, the Owner of the Throne. He sends the Inspiration by His Command to any of His slaves He wills, that he (the person who receives inspiration) may warn (men) of the Day of Mutual Meeting (i.e. The Day of Resurrection).

15.author: Itani
15.text: Exalted in rank, Owner of the Throne. He conveys the Spirit, by His command, upon whomever He wills of His servants, to warn of the Day of Encounter.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: Exalted in Rank, Lord of the Throne: He causes the spirit to descend on whomsoever of His servants He pleases so as to warn them of the Day of Encounter;

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: (He is Allah) Owner of high ranks and degrees, the Owner of the Throne. He sends the revelation by His command to any of His servants He wills, that he may warn of the Day of Mutual Meeting.

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: The Exalter of Ranks, the Lord of the Throne. He causeth the Spirit of His command upon whom He will of His slaves, that He may warn of the Day of Meeting,

15.author: Qarai
15.text: Raiser of ranks, Lord of the Throne, He casts the Spirit of His command upon whomever of His servants that He wishes, that he may warn [people] of the Day of Encounter.

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: Exalter in ranks, Owner of the Throne. He lets the spirit (the revelation) descend at His order on those of His worshipers whom He chooses, in order that He warns of the Day of the Meeting;

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: [He is] the Exalted above [all] degrees, Owner of the Throne; He places the inspiration of His command upon whom He wills of His servants to warn of the Day of Meeting.

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: God is the promoter of His servants and the owner of the Throne. He sends His spirit by His command to whichever of His servants He wants to warn them of the Day of Judgment.

15.author: Shakir
15.text: Possessor of the highest rank, Lord of power: He makes the inspiration to light by His command upon whom He pleases of His servants, that he may warn (men) of the day of meeting.

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: Exalted and throned on high, He lets the Spirit descend at His behest upon whichever of His servants He will, so that he may warn of the Day of Meeting,

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: Raised high above ranks (or degrees), (He is) the Lord of the Throne (of Authority): by His Command doth He send the Spirit (of inspiration) to any of His servants he pleases, that it may warn (men) of the Day of Mutual Meeting,-

15.author: Transliteration
15.text: RafeeAAu alddarajati thoo alAAarshi yulqee alrrooha min amrihi AAala man yashao min AAibadihi liyunthira yawma alttalaqi

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: The day when they will come out (of their graves), with nothing of them hidden from God. Whose then will be the kingdom? -- God's, the one, the omnipotent.

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: A day when they will be fully exposed; nothing from their affairs will be hidden from Allah; “For whom is the kingship this day? For Allah, the One, the All Dominant.”

16.author: Arberry
16.text: the day they sally forth, and naught of theirs is hidden from God. 'Whose is' the Kingdom today?' 'God's, the One, the Omnipotent.

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: The Day whereon they will appear: naught of them will be concealed from Allah, Whose is the dominion to-day? It is of Allah, the One, the Subduer.

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: The Day when they will (all) come out, nothing of them will be hidden from Allah. Whose is the kingdom this Day? (Allah Himself will reply to His Question): It is Allah's the One, the Irresistible!

16.author: Itani
16.text: The Day when they will emerge, nothing about them will be concealed from God. “To whom does the sovereignty belong today?” “To God, the One, the Irresistible.”

16.author: Maududi
16.text: the Day when they will emerge and nothing of them shall be hidden from Allah. (On that Day they will be asked): “Whose is the kingdom today?” (The whole world will cry out): “It is Allah's, the One, the Overpowering.”

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: The Day when they will come out, nothing of them will be hidden from Allah. Whose is the kingdom this Day It is Allah's, the One, the Irresistible!

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: The day when they come forth, nothing of them being hidden from Allah. Whose is the Sovereignty this day? It is Allah's, the One, the Almighty.

16.author: Qarai
16.text: The day when they will emerge [from their graves], nothing about them will be hidden from Allah. ‘To whom does the sovereignty belong today?’ ‘To Allah, the One, the All-paramount!’

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: the Day when they shall rise up with nothing hidden from Allah. And who is the Owner of the Kingdom on that Day? Allah, the One, the Conqueror!

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: The Day they come forth nothing concerning them will be concealed from Allah. To whom belongs [all] sovereignty this Day? To Allah, the One, the Prevailing.

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: Nothing will remain hidden from God concerning them on the day when they appear before God (from their graves) (It will be asked), "To whom does the kingdom belong on this Day?" (It will be answered), "The kingdom belongs to the Almighty One God".

16.author: Shakir
16.text: (Of) the day when they shall come forth, nothing concerning them remains hidden to Allah. To whom belongs the kingdom this day? To Allah, the One, the Subduer (of all).

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: the Day when they shall rise up [from their graves] and nothing about them will be hidden from God. "To whom shall the kingdom belong that Day?" It shall belong to God, the One, the All Powerful.

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: The Day whereon they will (all) come forth: not a single thing concerning them is hidden from Allah. Whose will be the dominion that Day?" That of Allah, the One the Irresistible!

16.author: Transliteration
16.text: Yawma hum barizoona la yakhfa AAala Allahi minhum shayon limani almulku alyawma lillahi alwahidi alqahhari

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: Each soul will be recompensed that Day for what it had earned. There will be no depriving on that Day. Surely God is swift at reckoning.

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: This day each soul will be repaid for what it has earned; no one will be wronged this day; indeed Allah is Swift At Taking Account.

17.author: Arberry
17.text: Today each soul shall be recompensed for that it has earned; no wrong today. Surely God is swift at the reckoning.'

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: To-day each soul will be recompensed for that which it hath earned; no wrong-doing to-day; verily Allah is Swift at reckoning.

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: This Day shall every person be recompensed for what he earned. No injustice (shall be done to anybody). Truly, Allah is Swift in reckoning.

17.author: Itani
17.text: On that Day, every soul will be recompensed for what it had earned. There will be no injustice on that Day. God is quick to settle accounts.

17.author: Maududi
17.text: (It will then be said): “Today shall everyone be fully recompensed for his deeds. None shall be wronged today. Surely Allah is Swift in Reckoning.”

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: This Day shall every person be recompensed for what he earned. This Day no injustice. Truly, Allah is swift in reckoning.

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: This day is each soul requited that which it hath earned; no wrong (is done) this day. Lo! Allah is swift at reckoning.

17.author: Qarai
17.text: ‘Today every soul shall be requited for what it has earned. There will be no injustice today. Indeed Allah is swift at reckoning.’

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: On that Day every soul shall be recompensed for its earnings and on that Day none shall be wronged. The reckoning of Allah is swift.

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: This Day every soul will be recompensed for what it earned. No injustice today! Indeed, Allah is swift in account.

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: Every soul will be recompensed for its deeds on this Day. There will be no injustice. Certainly God's reckoning is swift.

17.author: Shakir
17.text: This day every soul shall be rewarded for what it has earned; no injustice (shall be done) this day; surely Allah is quick in reckoning.

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: That Day every soul shall be requited for what it has earned. On that Day none shall be wronged. And God is swift in reckoning.

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: That Day will every soul be requited for what it earned; no injustice will there be that Day, for Allah is Swift in taking account.

17.author: Transliteration
17.text: Alyawma tujza kullu nafsin bima kasabat la thulma alyawma inna Allaha sareeAAu alhisabi

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: Warn them of the coming day inevitable, when hearts would jump to the throats, filling them with anguish. The sinners will have neither friend nor intercessor whose (word) will be heeded.

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: And warn them of the day of impending calamity, when hearts will rise up to the throats filled with grief; and the disbelievers will have neither any friend nor any intercessor who will be obeyed. (Intercession will be accepted only for the Muslims, not for the disbelievers)

18.author: Arberry
18.text: And warn them against the Day of the Imminent when, choking with anguish, the hearts are in the throats and the evildoers have not one loyal friend, no intercessor to be heeded.

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: Wherefore warn them thou of the Day of portending, whereon the hearts will be in the throats, choking: then will be for the wrong- doers no ardent friend nor an intercessor to be given heed to.

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: And warn them (O Muhammad SAW) of the Day that is drawing near (i.e. the Day of Resurrection), when the hearts will be choking the throats, and they can neither return them (hearts) to their chests nor can they throw them out. There will be no friend, nor an intercessor for the Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers, etc.), who could be given heed to.

18.author: Itani
18.text: And warn them of the Day of Imminence, when the hearts are at the throats, choking them. The evildoers will have no intimate friend, and no intercessor to be obeyed.

18.author: Maududi
18.text: (O Prophet), then warn them of the Day that has drawn near, the Day when hearts full of suppressed grief will leap up to the throats and the wrong-doers shall neither have any sincere friend nor intercessor whose word will be heeded.

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: And warn them of the Day that is drawing near, when the hearts will be at the throats Kazimin. There will be no friend, nor an intercessor for the wrongdoers, who could be given heed to.

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: Warn them (O Muhammad) of the Day of the approaching (doom), when the hearts will be choking the throats, (when) there will be no friend for the wrong-doers, nor any intercessor who will be heard.

18.author: Qarai
18.text: Warn them of the Approaching Day, when the hearts will be at the throats, choking with suppressed agony, [and] the wrongdoers will have no sympathizer, nor any intercessor who might be heard.

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: Warn them against the Imminent Day when, choking with anguish, the hearts will be in their throats, the harmdoers will not have a single, loyal friend, and there will be no intercessor to be obeyed.

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: And warn them, [O Muhammad], of the Approaching Day, when hearts are at the throats, filled [with distress]. For the wrongdoers there will be no devoted friend and no intercessor [who is] obeyed.

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: (Muhammad), warn them of the approaching day when because of hardship and frustration their hearts will almost reach up to their throats. The unjust will have no friends nor any intercessor who will be heard.

18.author: Shakir
18.text: And warn them of the day that draws near, when hearts shall rise up to the throats, grieving inwardly; the unjust shall not have any compassionate friend nor any intercessor who should be obeyed.

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: [O Prophet] forewarn them of the approaching Day, when hearts will leap up to the throats and choke them; when the wrongdoers will have no friend, nor any intercessor who will be listened to,

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: Warn them of the Day that is (ever) drawing near, when the hearts will (come) right up to the throats to choke (them); No intimate friend nor intercessor will the wrong-doers have, who could be listened to.

18.author: Transliteration
18.text: Waanthirhum yawma alazifati ithi alquloobu lada alhanajiri kathimeena ma lilththalimeena min hameemin wala shafeeAAin yutaAAu

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: Known to Him is the treachery of the eye, and what the breasts conceal.

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: Allah well knows the covert glance and all what lies hidden in the hearts.

19.author: Arberry
19.text: He knows the treachery of the eyes and what the breasts conceal.

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: He knoweth the fraudulence of the eyes, and that which the breasts conceal.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: Allah knows the fraud of the eyes, and all that the breasts conceal.

19.author: Itani
19.text: He knows the deceptions of the eyes, and what the hearts conceal.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: He knows even the most stealthy glance of the eyes and all the secrets that hearts conceal.

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: Allah knows the fraud of the eyes, and all that the breasts conceal.

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: He knoweth the traitor of the eyes, and that which the bosoms hide.

19.author: Qarai
19.text: He knows the treachery of the eyes, and what the breasts hide.

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: He (Allah) knows the furtive looks of the eyes and what the chests conceal.

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: He knows that which deceives the eyes and what the breasts conceal.

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: God knows the disloyalty of the eyes and what the hearts conceal.

19.author: Shakir
19.text: He knows the stealthy looks and that which the breasts conceal.

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: [for] He is aware of the [most] stealthy glance, and of all that the hearts conceal.

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: (Allah) knows of (the tricks) that deceive with the eyes, and all that the hearts (of men) conceal.

19.author: Transliteration
19.text: YaAAlamu khainata alaAAyuni wama tukhfee alssudooru

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: God decides with justice. But those they call apart from Him can not adjudge in the least. Verily God is all-hearing, all-perceiving.

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: And Allah renders the true judgement; and those whom they worship instead of Him do not judge at all; indeed Allah only, is the All Hearing, the All Seeing.

20.author: Arberry
20.text: God shall decide justly, and those they call on, apart from Him, shall not decide by any means. surely God is the All-hearing, the All-seeing.

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: Allah decreeth with truth, while those whom they call upon beside Him cannot decree aught; verily Allah! He is the Hearer, the Beholder!

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: And Allah judges with truth, while those to whom they invoke besides Him, cannot judge anything. Certainly, Allah! He is the All-Hearer, the All-Seer.

20.author: Itani
20.text: God judges with justice, while those whom they invoke besides Him cannot judge with anything. It is God who is the Hearing, the Seeing.

20.author: Maududi
20.text: Allah will judge with justice, whereas those whom they call upon beside Him cannot judge at all. Surely Allah -- and He alone -- is All- Hearing, All-Seeing.

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: And Allah judges with truth, while those to whom they invoke besides Him, cannot judge anything. Certainly, Allah! He is the All-Hearer, the All-Seer.

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: Allah judgeth with truth, while those to whom they cry instead of Him judge not at all. Lo! Allah, He is the Hearer, the Seer.

20.author: Qarai
20.text: Allah judges with justice, while those whom they invoke besides Him do not judge by anything. Indeed it is Allah who is the All-hearing, the All-seeing.

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: With justice Allah will judge, but those upon whom they call, other than Him, can not judge anything at all! Allah is the Hearer, the Seer.

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: And Allah judges with truth, while those they invoke besides Him judge not with anything. Indeed, Allah - He is the Hearing, the Seeing.

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: God judges with Truth but those whom they worship besides God can have no Judgment. God is certainly All-hearing and All-aware.

20.author: Shakir
20.text: And Allah judges with the truth; and those whom they call upon besides Him cannot judge aught; surely Allah is the Hearing, the Seeing.

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: God will judge with [justice and] truth: but those whom they invoke besides Him, have no power to judge at all. Surely, God is all hearing, all seeing.

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: And Allah will judge with (justice and) Truth: but those whom (men) invoke besides Him, will not (be in a position) to judge at all. Verily it is Allah (alone) Who hears and sees (all things).

20.author: Transliteration
20.text: WaAllahu yaqdee bialhaqqi waallatheena yadAAoona min doonihi la yaqdoona bishayin inna Allaha huwa alssameeAAu albaseeru

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: Have they not travelled on the earth that they could see what happened to those before them? They were greater in strength than they, and have left behind them traces on the earth. Yet they were seized by God for their sins, and had none to protect them against God.

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: So did they not travel in the land in order to see what sort of fate befell those before them? Their strength and the signs they left behind in the earth, exceeded them – so Allah seized them on account of their sins, and they had no one to save them from Allah.

21.author: Arberry
21.text: What, have they not journeyed in the land and beheld how was the end of those before them? They were stronger than themselves in might and left firmer traces in the earth; yet God seized them in their sins, and they had none to defend them from God.

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: Have they not travelled about in the land so that they may behold of what wise hath been the end of those who were before them? They were mightier than these in strength and in the traces in the land. Yet Allah laid hold of them for their sins, and from Allah they had none as protector.

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: Have they not travelled in the land and seen what was the end of those who were before them? They were superior to them in strength, and in the traces (which they left) in the land. But Allah seized them with punishment for their sins. And none had they to protect them from Allah.

21.author: Itani
21.text: Have they not travelled through the earth, and seen the consequences for those before them? They were stronger than them, and they left more impact on earth. But God seized them for their sins, and they had no defender against God.

21.author: Maududi
21.text: Have they not journeyed in the land that they might observe the end of those who came before them? They were even greater in strength than they and left behind more splendid traces in the land. Then Allah seized them because of their sins and they had none who could protect them from Allah.

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: Have they not traveled in the land and seen what was the end of those who were before them They were superior to them in strength, and in the traces in the land. But Allah seized them with punishment for their sins. And none had they to protect them from Allah.

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: Have they not travelled in the land to see the nature of the consequence for those who disbelieved before them? They were mightier than these in power and (in the) traces (which they left behind them) in the earth. Yet Allah seized them for their sins, and they had no protector from Allah.

21.author: Qarai
21.text: Have they not travelled through the land so that they may observe how was the fate of those who were before them? They were greater than them in might and with respect to the effects [they left] in the land. But then Allah seized them for their sins, and they had no defender against Allah [’s punishment].

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: Have they never journeyed through the land and seen what was the end of those who have gone before them? They were stronger than themselves in might and left firmer traces in the earth, but Allah seized them for their sinning, and they had none to protect them from Allah.

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: Have they not traveled through the land and observed how was the end of those who were before them? They were greater than them in strength and in impression on the land, but Allah seized them for their sins. And they had not from Allah any protector.

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: Have they not travelled through the land to see the terrible end of those who lived before them. They had been mightier than them in power and in leaving their traces on earth. God punished them for their sins. They had no one to save them from God's torment.

21.author: Shakir
21.text: Have they not travelled in the earth and seen how was the end of those who were before them? Mightier than these were they in strength-- and in fortifications in the land, but Allah destroyed them for their sins; and there was not for them any defender against Allah.

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: Have they not travelled through the land and seen what was the end of those who have gone before them? They were stronger than them and made a more impressive mark upon the land, yet God destroyed them for their sins, they had no one to defend them against Him,

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: Do they not travel through the earth and see what was the End of those before them? They were even superior to them in strength, and in the traces (they have left) in the land: but Allah did call them to account for their sins, and none had they to defend them against Allah.

21.author: Transliteration
21.text: Awa lam yaseeroo fee alardi fayanthuroo kayfa kana AAaqibatu allatheena kanoo min qablihim kanoo hum ashadda minhum quwwatan waatharan fee alardi faakhathahumu Allahu bithunoobihim wama kana lahum mina Allahi min waqin

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: This was so because their apostles came with clear proofs to them, but they refused to believe. So they were seized by God. Surely God is powerful, unrelenting in retribution.

22.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
22.text: This occurred because their Noble Messengers came to them with clear signs, thereupon they used to disbelieve, so Allah seized them; indeed Allah is All Powerful, Severe in Punishment.

22.author: Arberry
22.text: That was because their Messengers came to them with the clear signs; but they disbelieved, so God seized them. Surely He is All-strong, terrible in retribution.

22.author: Daryabadi
22.text: That was because their apostles were wont to bring them evidence, but they disbelieved; wherefore Allah laid hold of them. Verily He is strong, severe in chastisement.

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: That was because there came to them their Messengers with clear evidences, proofs and signs but they disbelieved (in them). So Allah seized them with punishment. Verily, He is All-Strong, Severe in punishment.

22.author: Itani
22.text: That is because their messengers used to come to them with clear proofs, but they disbelieved, so God seized them. He is Strong, Severe in retribution.

22.author: Maududi
22.text: They came to this end because their Messengers would come to them with Clear Signs and yet they would refuse to believe. So Allah seized them. He is indeed Strong, Terrible in Retribution.

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: That was because there came to them their Messengers with clear evidences but they disbelieved (in them). So Allah seized them. Verily, He is All-Strong, Severe in punishment.

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: That was because their messengers kept bringing them clear proofs (of Allah's Sovereignty) but they disbelieved; so Allah seized them. Lo! He is Strong, severe in punishment.

22.author: Qarai
22.text: That was because their apostles used to bring them manifest proofs, but they defied [them]. So Allah seized them. Indeed He is all-strong, severe in retribution.

22.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
22.text: That was because they disbelieved their Messengers when they came to them with clear signs, and so Allah seized them. Surely, He is Strong, and Stern in retribution.

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: That was because their messengers were coming to them with clear proofs, but they disbelieved, so Allah seized them. Indeed, He is Powerful and severe in punishment.

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: Messengers had come to them with illustrious miracles but they disbelieved and thus God struck them with His torment. He is Mighty and Severe in His retribution.

22.author: Shakir
22.text: That was because there came to them their apostles with clear arguments, but they rejected (them), therefore Allah destroyed them; surely He is Strong, Severe in retribution.

22.author: Wahiduddin Khan
22.text: that was because their messengers came to them with clear signs but they rejected them. So God seized them: He is powerful, severe in punishment.

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: That was because there came to them their messengers with Clear (Signs), but they rejected them: So Allah called them to account: for He is Full of Strength, Strict in Punishment.

22.author: Transliteration
22.text: Thalika biannahum kanat tateehim rusuluhum bialbayyinati fakafaroo faakhathahumu Allahu innahu qawiyyun shadeedu alAAiqabi

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: We sent Moses with Our signs and clear authority

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: And indeed We sent Moosa with Our signs and a clear proof.

23.author: Arberry
23.text: We also sent Moses with Our signs and a clear authority,

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: And assuredly We sent Musa with Our signs and a manifest authority.

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: And indeed We sent Musa (Moses) with Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), and a manifest authority,

23.author: Itani
23.text: We sent Moses with Our signs, and a clear authority.

23.author: Maududi
23.text: Verily We sent Moses with Our Signs and a clear authority

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: And indeed We sent Musa with Our Ayat, and a manifest authority,

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: And verily We sent Moses with Our revelations and a clear warrant

23.author: Qarai
23.text: Certainly We sent Moses with Our signs and a manifest authority

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: We sent Moses with Our signs and with clear authority

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: And We did certainly send Moses with Our signs and a clear authority

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: We sent Moses with Our miracles and clear authority.

23.author: Shakir
23.text: And certainly We sent Musa with Our communications and clear authority,

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: We sent Moses with Our signs and clear authority

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: Of old We sent Moses, with Our Signs and an authority manifest,

23.author: Transliteration
23.text: Walaqad arsalna moosa biayatina wasultanin mubeenin

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: To Pharaoh, Haman and Qarun. But they said: "He is only a deceiving sorcerer."

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: Towards Firaun and Haman and Qaroon – in response they said, “(He is) a magician, a big liar!”

24.author: Arberry
24.text: to Pharaoh, Haman and Korah; they said, 'A lying sorcerer!'

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: Unto Fir'awn and Haman and Qur'an, but they said: a magician, a liar.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: To Fir'aun (Pharaoh), Haman and Qarun (Korah), but they called (him): "A sorcerer, a liar!"

24.author: Itani
24.text: To Pharaoh, Hamaan, and Quaroon. But they said, “A lying sorcerer.”

24.author: Maududi
24.text: to Pharaoh and Haman and Korah. They said: “(He is) a sorcerer, an utter liar.”

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: To Fir`awn, Haman and Qarun, but they called (him): "A sorcerer, a liar!"

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: Unto Pharaoh and Haman and Korah, but they said: A lying sorcerer!

24.author: Qarai
24.text: to Pharaoh, Haman and Korah, but they said, ‘A magician and a mendacious liar.’

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: to Pharaoh, Haman, and Korah. But they said: 'A sorcerer, a liar'

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: To Pharaoh, Haman and Qarun; but they said, "[He is] a magician and a liar."

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: to the Pharaoh, Haman, and Korah, who said, "He is only a lying magician.".

24.author: Shakir
24.text: To Firon and Haman and Qaroun, but they said: A lying magician.

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: to Pharaoh, Haman and Korah. But they said, "A magician, a liar."

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: To Pharaoh, Haman, and Qarun; but they called (him)" a sorcerer telling lies!"...

24.author: Transliteration
24.text: Ila firAAawna wahamana waqaroona faqaloo sahirun kaththabun

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: And when he brought the truth to them from Us, they said: "Slay the sons of those who believe with him, and spare their women." But the unbelievers' stratagem is bound to fail.

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: So when he brought the truth from Our presence to them, they said, “Kill the sons of those who believe in him, and keep their women alive”; and the evil scheme of the disbelievers only keeps wandering astray.

25.author: Arberry
25.text: And when he brought them the truth from Us, they said, 'Slay the sons of those who believe with him, and spare their women.' But the guile of the unbelievers is ever in error.

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: And when he came to them with the truth from before Us, they said: slay the sons of those who have believed with him, and let their women live. And the plot of the infidels was naught but vain.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: Then, when he brought them the Truth from Us, they said: "Kill the sons of those who believe with him and let their women live", but the plots of disbelievers are nothing but errors!

25.author: Itani
25.text: Then, when he came to them with the truth from Us, they said, “Kill the sons of those who have believed with him, and spare their daughters.” But the scheming of the unbelievers can only go astray.

25.author: Maududi
25.text: When Moses brought them the Truth from Us they said: “Kill the sons of all the believers who have joined him, but spare the women.” The guile of the unbelievers always ends in vain.

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: Then, when he brought them the Truth from Us, they said: "Kill with him the sons of those who believe and let their women live;" but the plots of disbelievers are nothing but in vain!

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: And when he brought them the Truth from Our presence, they said: Slay the sons of those who believe with him, and spare their women. But the plot of disbelievers is in naught but error.

25.author: Qarai
25.text: So when he brought them the truth from Us, they said, ‘Kill the sons of the faithful who are with him, and spare their women.’ But the stratagems of the faithless only go awry.

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: And when he brought them the truth from Us they said: 'Kill the sons of those who believe with him, but spare their women' But the scheming of the unbelievers is always in error.

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: And when he brought them the truth from Us, they said, "Kill the sons of those who have believed with him and keep their women alive." But the plan of the disbelievers is not except in error.

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: When We sent him to them for a genuine purpose, they said, "Kill the sons of those who have believed in him but keep their women alive." The plots of the unbelievers can only result in failure.

25.author: Shakir
25.text: So when he brought to them the truth from Us, they said: Slay the sons of those who believe with him and keep their women alive; and the struggle of the unbelievers will only come to a state of perdition.

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: When he came to them with the truth from Us, they said, "Slay the sons of those who believe with him and spare only their daughters" -- the schemes of those who denied the truth were futile.

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: Now, when he came to them in Truth, from Us, they said, "Slay the sons of those who believe with him, and keep alive their females," but the plots of Unbelievers (end) in nothing but errors (and delusions)!...

25.author: Transliteration
25.text: Falamma jaahum bialhaqqi min AAindina qaloo oqtuloo abnaa allatheena amanoo maAAahu waistahyoo nisaahum wama kaydu alkafireena illa fee dalalin

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: "Let me kill Moses," the Pharaoh said, "and let him call to his Lord. I fear that he will change your faith and spread corruption in the land."

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: And said Firaun, “Allow me to kill Moosa and let him pray to his Lord; I fear that he will change your religion or cause chaos in the land!”

26.author: Arberry
26.text: And Pharaoh said, 'Let me slay Moses, and let him call to his Lord. I fear that he may change, your religion, or that he may cause corruption to appear in the land.'
