26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: And Fir'awn said: let me alone, that may slay Musa, and let him call upon his Lord. Verily fear that he may change your religion or that he may cause to appear in the land corruption.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: Fir'aun (Pharaoh) said: "Leave me to kill Musa (Moses), and let him call his Lord (to stop me from killing him)! I fear that he may change your religion, or that he may cause mischief to appear in the land!"

26.author: Itani
26.text: Pharaoh said, “Leave me to kill Moses, and let him appeal to his Lord. I fear he may change your religion, or spread disorder in the land.”

26.author: Maududi
26.text: One day Pharaoh said: “Let me go and kill Moses; then let him invoke his Lord. I fear that he will change your religion or cause disruption in the land.”

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: Fir`awn said: "Leave me to kill Musa, and let him call his Lord! I fear that he may change your religion, or that he may cause mischief to appear in the land!"

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: And Pharaoh said: Suffer me to kill Moses, and let him cry unto his Lord. Lo! I fear that he will alter your religion or that he will cause confusion in the land.

26.author: Qarai
26.text: And Pharaoh said, ‘Let me slay Moses, and let him invoke his Lord. Indeed I fear that he will change your religion, or bring forth corruption in our land.’

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: Pharaoh said: 'Let me kill Moses, then let him call to his Lord! I am fearful that he will change your religion or cause mischief in the land'

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: And Pharaoh said, "Let me kill Moses and let him call upon his Lord. Indeed, I fear that he will change your religion or that he will cause corruption in the land."

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: The Pharaoh said, "Let me kill Moses and let him call for help from his Lord. I am afraid that he will change your religion or spread evil through the land.".

26.author: Shakir
26.text: And Firon said: Let me alone that I may slay Musa and let him call upon his Lord; surely I fear that he will change your religion or that he will make mischief to appear in the land.

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: Pharaoh said, "Let me kill Moses, let him call upon his Lord, I fear that he may cause you to change your religion, or that he may cause disorder in the land."

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: Said Pharaoh: "Leave me to slay Moses; and let him call on his Lord! What I fear is lest he should change your religion, or lest he should cause mischief to appear in the land!"

26.author: Transliteration
26.text: Waqala firAAawnu tharoonee aqtul moosa walyadAAu rabbahu innee akhafu an yubaddila deenakum aw an yuthhira fee alardi alfasada

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: Moses said: "I seek refuge in my Lord and your Lord from every insolent imposter who does not believe in the Day of Reckoning."

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: Said Moosa, “I seek the refuge of mine and your Lord, from every haughty person who does not believe in the Day of Reckoning.”

27.author: Arberry
27.text: And Moses said, 'I take refuge in my Lord and your Lord from every man who is proud, and believes not in the Day of Reckoning.'

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: And Musa said: verily I seek refuge in my Lord and your Lord from every stiff-necked person who believeth not in a Day of Reckoning.

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: Musa (Moses) said: "Verily, I seek refuge in my Lord and your Lord from every arrogant who believes not in the Day of Reckoning!"

27.author: Itani
27.text: Moses said, “I have sought the protection of my Lord and your Lord, from every tyrant who does not believe in the Day of Account.”

27.author: Maududi
27.text: Moses said: “I have taken refuge with my Lord and your Lord from everyone who waxes arrogant and does not believe in the Day of Reckoning.”

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: Musa said: "Verily, I seek refuge in my Lord and your Lord from every arrogant who believes not in the Day of Reckoning!"

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: Moses said: Lo! I seek refuge in my Lord and your Lord from every scorner who believeth not in a Day of Reckoning.

27.author: Qarai
27.text: Moses said, ‘I seek the protection of my Lord and your Lord from every arrogant one who does not believe in the Day of Reckoning.’

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: Moses said: 'I take refuge in my Lord and in your Lord from every proud (person), who does not believe in Day of Reckoning'

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: But Moses said, "Indeed, I have sought refuge in my Lord and your Lord from every arrogant one who does not believe in the Day of Account."

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: Moses said, "I seek protection from your and my Lord against every arrogant person who has no faith in the Day of Judgment."

27.author: Shakir
27.text: And Musa said: Surely I take refuge with my Lord and-- your Lord from every proud one who does not believe in the day of reckoning.

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: Moses replied, "I seek refuge with my Lord and your Lord from every arrogant person who does not believe in the Day of Reckoning."

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: Moses said: "I have indeed called upon my Lord and your Lord (for protection) from every arrogant one who believes not in the Day of Account!"

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: Waqala moosa innee AAuthtu birabbee warabbikum min kulli mutakabbirin la yuminu biyawmi alhisabi

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: A believer from the House of Pharaoh who had kept his faith to himself, said: "Will you kill a man because he says: 'My Lord is God,' when he has brought clear signs from his Lord to you? If he is a liar his lie will recoil back on him; but in case he speaks the truth, then what he predicts will befall you. Surely God does not show the way to the shameful liar.

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: And said a Muslim man from the people of Firaun, who used to hide his faith, “What! You want to kill a man just because he says, ‘Allah is my Lord’ whereas he has indeed brought clear signs to you from your Lord? And supposedly if he is speaking wrongly, then the calamity of wrongful speech is upon him; and if he is truthful, then part of what he promises you will reach you; indeed Allah does not guide any transgressor, excessive liar.”

28.author: Arberry
28.text: Then said a certain man, a believer 'of Pharaoh's folk that kept hidden his belief, 'What, will you slay a man because he says, "My Lord is God," yet he has brought you the clear signs from your Lord? If he is a liar, his lying is upon his own head; but if he is truthful, somewhat of that he promises you will smite you. Surely God guides not him who is prodigal and a liar.

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: And a believing man of Fir'awn 's household, hiding his belief, said: would ye slay a man because he saith: my Lord is God, and hath come upto you with evidences from your Lord? If he is a liar, then upon him will be his lie, and if he is a truth-teller, then there will befall you some of that wherewith he threateneth you; verily God guideth not one who is an extravagant and a liar.

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: And a believing man of Fir'aun's (Pharaoh) family, who hid his faith said: "Would you kill a man because he says: My Lord is Allah, and he has come to you with clear signs (proofs) from your Lord? And if he is a liar, upon him will be (the sin of) his lie; but if he is telling the truth, then some of that (calamity) wherewith he threatens you will befall on you." Verily, Allah guides not one who is a Musrif (a polytheist, or a murderer who shed blood without a right, or those who commit great sins, oppressor, transgressor), a liar!

28.author: Itani
28.text: A believing man from Pharaoh's family, who had concealed his faith, said, “Are you going to kill a man for saying, `My Lord is God,’ and he has brought you clear proofs from your Lord? If he is a liar, his lying will rebound upon him; but if he is truthful, then some of what he promises you will befall you. God does not guide the extravagant imposter.

28.author: Maududi
28.text: Then a man endowed with faith, from Pharaoh's folk, who had kept his faith hidden, said: “Do you kill a person simply because he says: 'My Lord is Allah' even though he brought to you clear Signs from your Lord? If he is a liar, his lying will recoil upon him; but if he is truthful, you will be smitten with some of the awesome consequences of which he warns you. Allah does not guide to the Right Way any who exceeds the limits and is an utter liar.

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: And a believing man of Fir`awn's family, who hid his Faith said: "Would you kill a man because he says: `My Lord is Allah,' and he has come to you with clear signs from your Lord And if he is a liar, upon him will be (the sin of) his lie; but if he is telling the truth, then some of that (calamity) wherewith he threatens you will befall on you." Verily, Allah guides not one who is a transgressor, a liar!

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: And a believing man of Pharaoh's family, who hid his faith, said: Would ye kill a man because he saith: My Lord is Allah, and hath brought you clear proofs from your Lord? If he is lying, then his lie is upon him; and if he is truthful, then some of that wherewith he threateneth you will strike you. Lo! Allah guideth not one who is a prodigal, a liar.

28.author: Qarai
28.text: Said a man of faith from Pharaoh’s clan, who concealed his faith, ‘Will you kill a man for saying, ‘‘My Lord is Allah,’’ while he has already brought you manifest proofs from your Lord? Should he be lying, his falsehood will be to his own detriment; but if he is truthful, there shall visit you some of what he promises you. Indeed Allah does not guide someone who is a transgressor and liar.

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: But one of Pharaoh's people, who was in secret a believer, asked: 'Would you kill a man because he says: "My Lord is Allah?" He has brought you clear signs from your Lord. If he is lying, let his lie be on his head, but, if he is speaking the truth then at least a part of what he promises will befall you. Allah does not guide the lying sinner.

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: And a believing man from the family of Pharaoh who concealed his faith said, "Do you kill a man [merely] because he says, 'My Lord is Allah' while he has brought you clear proofs from your Lord? And if he should be lying, then upon him is [the consequence of] his lie; but if he should be truthful, there will strike you some of what he promises you. Indeed, Allah does not guide one who is a transgressor and a liar.

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: A believing person from the people of the Pharaoh who concealed his faith said, "Would you kill a man just because he says God is my Lord? He has brought you illustrious miracles from your Lord. If he speaks lies, it will only harm him, but if he speaks the Truth, some of his warnings may affect you. God does not guide a transgressing liar.

28.author: Shakir
28.text: And a believing man of Firon's people who hid his faith said: What! will you slay a man because he says: My Lord is Allah, and indeed he has brought to you clear arguments from your Lord? And if he be a liar, on him will be his lie, and if he be truthful, there will befall you some of that which he threatens you (with); surely Allah does not guide him who is extravagant, a liar:

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: A believer, a man from among the people of Pharaoh, who had concealed his faith, said, "Would you slay a man merely because he says, "My Lord is God." He has brought you clear signs from your Lord, and if he is lying, the sin of his will be on his own head; but if he is truthful, a part of that of which he warns you will surely befall you. Certainly, God does not guide one who is a transgressor and a liar.

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: A believer, a man from among the people of Pharaoh, who had concealed his faith, said: "Will ye slay a man because he says, 'My Lord is Allah'?- when he has indeed come to you with Clear (Signs) from your Lord? and if he be a liar, on him is (the sin of) his lie: but, if he is telling the Truth, then will fall on you something of the (calamity) of which he warns you: Truly Allah guides not one who transgresses and lies!

28.author: Transliteration
28.text: Waqala rajulun muminun min ali firAAawna yaktumu eemanahu ataqtuloona rajulan an yaqoola rabbiyya Allahu waqad jaakum bialbayyinati min rabbikum wain yaku kathiban faAAalayhi kathibuhu wain yaku sadiqan yusibkum baAAdu allathee yaAAidukum inna Allaha la yahdee man huwa musrifun kaththabun

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: O my people, authority is yours today being the most powerful in the land; but who will save us from the scourge of God if it fall upon us?" "I show you," said the Pharaoh, "only what I see (is right), and guide you but to the right path."

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: “O my people! You now rule the earth, dominant; so who will save us from Allah’s punishment if it comes upon us?” Said Firaun, “I only explain to you what I think is correct, and I show you only the right path.”

29.author: Arberry
29.text: O my people, today the kingdom is yours, who are masters in the land. But who will help us, against the might of God, if it comes upon us?' Said Pharaoh, 'I only let you see what I see; I only guide you in the way of rectitude.'

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: O My people! yours is the the dominion to-day: ye being overcomers in the land; but who will succour us aginst the scourge of God if it cometh Unto us! Fir'awn said: I shew you only that which see, and I guide you but to the path of rectitude.

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: "O my people! Yours is the kingdom this day, you are uppermost in the land. But who will save us from the Torment of Allah, should it befall us?" Fir'aun (Pharaoh) said: "I show you only that which I see (correct), and I guide you only to the path of right policy!"

29.author: Itani
29.text: O my people! Yours is the dominion today, supreme in the land; but who will help us against God’s might, should it fall upon us?” Pharaoh said, “I do not show you except what I see, and I do not guide you except to the path of prudence.”

29.author: Maududi
29.text: My people, today the kingdom is yours, and you are supreme in the land. But if Allah's chastisement were to come upon you, who will come to our help?” Pharaoh said: “I only counsel what I consider right; I only direct you to the Path of Rectitude.”

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: "O my people! Yours is the kingdom today, you being dominant in the land. But who will save us from the torment of Allah, should it befall us" Fir`awn said: "I show you only that which I see, and I guide you only to the path of right policy!"

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: O my people! Yours is the kingdom to-day, ye being uppermost in the land. But who would save us from the wrath of Allah should it reach us? Pharaoh said: I do but show you what I think, and I do but guide you to wise policy.

29.author: Qarai
29.text: O my people! Today sovereignty belongs to you, and you are dominant in the land. But who will save us from Allah’s punishment should it overtake us?’ Pharaoh said, ‘I just point out to you what I see [to be advisable for you], and I guide you only to the way of rectitude.’

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: O my nation, today the kingdom is yours and you are the masters in the land. But, if the might of Allah should come against us, who will help us' Pharaoh said: 'I only let you see what I see. I guide you to the path of righteousness'

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: O my people, sovereignty is yours today, [your being] dominant in the land. But who would protect us from the punishment of Allah if it came to us?" Pharaoh said, "I do not show you except what I see, and I do not guide you except to the way of right conduct."

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: My people, today you have the kingdom and the power on earth but who will help us against the wrath of God if it will befall us?" The Pharaoh said, "I show you only what I think is proper and guide you only to the right direction".

29.author: Shakir
29.text: O my people! yours is the kingdom this day, being masters in the land, but who will help us against the punishment of Allah if it come to us? Firon said: I do not show you aught but that which I see (myself), and I do not make you follow any but the right way.

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: My people! Yours is the kingdom today, you have dominion in the land; but who will help us against the scourge of God if it befalls us?" But Pharaoh said, "I point out to you only that which I consider right; and I guide you to the right path."

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: "O my People! Yours is the dominion this day: Ye have the upper hand in the land: but who will help us from the Punishment of Allah, should it befall us?" Pharaoh said: "I but point out to you that which I see (myself); Nor do I guide you but to the Path of Right!"

29.author: Transliteration
29.text: Ya qawmi lakumu almulku alyawma thahireena fee alardi faman yansuruna min basi Allahi in jaana qala firAAawnu ma oreekum illa ma ara wama ahdeekum illa sabeela alrrashadi

30.author: Ahmed Ali
30.text: But the man of belief said: "O my people, what I fear for you is the like of what befell the communities (of old).

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: And the believer said, “O my people! I fear for you a day similar to that of the earlier groups!”

30.author: Arberry
30.text: Then said he who believed, 'My people, truly I fear for you the like of the day of the parties,

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: And he who believed said: O my people. verily I fear for you a fate like the day of the confederates.

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: And he who believed said: "O my people! Verily, I fear for you a fate like that day (of disaster) of the Confederates (of old)!

30.author: Itani
30.text: The one who had believed said, “O my people, I fear for you the like of the day of the confederates.

30.author: Maududi
30.text: He who had faith said: “My people, I fear that you will confront a day like that which overtook many parties before you,

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: And he who believed said: "O my people! Verily, I fear for you an end like that day (of disaster) of the groups (of old)!"

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: And he who believed said: O my people! Lo! I fear for you a fate like that of the factions (of old);

30.author: Qarai
30.text: He who had faith said, ‘O my people! Indeed I fear for you [a day] like the day of the [heathen] factions;

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: The one who believed said: 'I fear for you the like of the day of the confederates my nation,

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: And he who believed said, "O my people, indeed I fear for you [a fate] like the day of the companies -

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: The believing man said, "I am afraid that you will face a (terrible) day like that of the groups:

30.author: Shakir
30.text: And he who believed said: O my people! surely I fear for you the like of what befell the parties:

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: The believer said, "My people! I fear for you a fate like that of the people of old:

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: Then said the man who believed: "O my people! Truly I do fear for you something like the Day (of disaster) of the Confederates (in sin)!-

30.author: Transliteration
30.text: Waqala allathee amana ya qawmi innee akhafu AAalaykum mithla yawmi alahzabi

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: Like the people of Noah, 'Ad and Thamud, and those that came after them. God does not want to be unjust to His creatures.

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: “Like the tradition of the people of Nooh, and Aad, and Thamud and others after them; and Allah does not will injustice upon bondmen.”

31.author: Arberry
31.text: the like of the case of Noah's people, Ad, Thamood, and those after them; and God desires not wrong for His servants.'

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: Like the wont of the people of Nuh and A'ad and Thamud and those after them, and God intendeth not any wrong Unto His bondmen.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: "Like the fate of the people of Nuh (Noah), and 'Ad, and Thamud and those who came after them. And Allah wills no injustice for (His) slaves.

31.author: Itani
31.text: Like the fate of the people of Noah, and Aad, and Thamood, and those after them. God wants no injustice for the servants.

31.author: Maududi
31.text: like the day that overtook the people of Noah and Ad and Thamud, and those who came after them. Allah does not wish to subject His servants to any injustice.

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: "Like the end of the people of Nuh, and `Ad, and Thamud and those who came after them. And Allah wills no injustice for (His) servants."

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: A plight like that of Noah's folk, and A'ad and Thamud, and those after them, and Allah willeth no injustice for (His) slaves.

31.author: Qarai
31.text: like the case of the people of Noah, of Ad and Thamud, and those who came after them, and Allah does not desire any wrong for [His] servants.

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: or, something similar to the circumstances of the nations of Noah, Aad, and Thamood, and those who came after them. Allah does not want to wrong His worshipers.

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: Like the custom of the people of Noah and of 'Aad and Thamud and those after them. And Allah wants no injustice for [His] servants.

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: people of Noah, Ad, Thamud, and those after them. God did not want injustice for His servants.

31.author: Shakir
31.text: The like of what befell the people of Nuh and Ad and Samood and those after them, and Allah does not desire injustice for (His) servants;

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: like the fate of the people of Noah, 'Ad, Thamud, and those who came after them -- God never wills injustice on His creatures.

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: "Something like the fate of the People of Noah, the 'Ad, and the Thamud, and those who came after them: but Allah never wishes injustice to his Servants.

31.author: Transliteration
31.text: Mithla dabi qawmi noohin waAAadin wathamooda waallatheena min baAAdihim wama Allahu yureedu thulman lilAAibadi

32.author: Ahmed Ali
32.text: O my people, what I fear for you is the day of gathering, crying and calling,

32.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
32.text: “O my people! I fear for you a day on which will be a great outcry!”

32.author: Arberry
32.text: O my people, I fear for you the Day of Invocation,

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: And, O my people! verily fear for you a Day of Mutual Calling.

32.author: Hilali & Khan
32.text: "And, O my people! Verily! I fear for you the Day when there will be mutual calling (between the people of Hell and of Paradise)."

32.author: Itani
32.text: O my people, I fear for you the Day of Calling Out.

32.author: Maududi
32.text: My people, I fear that you will encounter a day when there will be much wailing and you will cry out to one another for help,

32.author: Mubarakpuri
32.text: "And, O my people! Verily, I fear for you the Day when there will be mutual calling."

32.author: Pickthall
32.text: And, O my people! Lo! I fear for you a Day of Summoning,

32.author: Qarai
32.text: O my people! I fear for you a day of mutual distress calls,

32.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
32.text: And, my nation, I fear for you the Day of Calling,

32.author: Saheeh International
32.text: And O my people, indeed I fear for you the Day of Calling -

32.author: Sarwar
32.text: My people, I am afraid for you on (the Day of Judgment), when people will cry for help.

32.author: Shakir
32.text: And, O my people! I fear for you the day of calling out,

32.author: Wahiduddin Khan
32.text: My people, I fear for you the Day you will cry out to one another,

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: "And O my people! I fear for you a Day when there will be Mutual calling (and wailing),-

32.author: Transliteration
32.text: Waya qawmi innee akhafu AAalaykum yawma alttanadi

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: The day you will turn your backs and flee, with none to defend you against God. Whoever God allows to go astray has none to show him the way.

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: “A day when you will turn back fleeing; none can save you from Allah; and whomever Allah sends astray, there is no guide for him.”

33.author: Arberry
33.text: the day you turn about, retreating, having none to defend you from God; and whomsoever God leads astray, no guide has he.

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: A Day whereon ye shall turn away retreating: for you there will be no protector from God; and whomsoever God sendeth astray for him there is no guide.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: A Day when you will turn your backs and flee having no protector from Allah, And whomsoever Allah sends astray, for him there is no guide.

33.author: Itani
33.text: The Day when you will turn and flee, having no defender against God. Whomever God misguides has no guide.”

33.author: Maududi
33.text: the day when you will turn around to retreat, there will be none to protect you from Allah. He whom Allah lets go astray, none will be able to show him the Right Way.

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: A Day when you will turn your backs and flee having no protector from Allah. And whomsoever Allah sends astray, for him there is no guide.

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: A day when ye will turn to flee, having no preserver from Allah: and he whom Allah sendeth astray, for him there is no guide.

33.author: Qarai
33.text: a day when you will turn back [to flee], not having anyone to protect you from Allah, and whomever Allah leads astray has no guide.

33.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
33.text: the Day when you will turn in retreat with none to protect you from Allah. He whom Allah leads astray has no guide.

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: The Day you will turn your backs fleeing; there is not for you from Allah any protector. And whoever Allah leaves astray - there is not for him any guide.

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: On that day you will run away, but no one will be able to protect you from (God's Judgment). No one can guide one whom God has caused to go astray.

33.author: Shakir
33.text: The day on which you will turn back retreating; there shall be no savior for you from Allah, and whomsoever Allah causes to err, there is no guide for him:

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: the Day when you will [wish to] turn your backs and flee, having none to defend you against God: for he whom God lets go astray can never find any guide.

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: "A Day when ye shall turn your backs and flee: No defender shall ye have from Allah: Any whom Allah leaves to stray, there is none to guide...

33.author: Transliteration
33.text: Yawma tuwalloona mudbireena ma lakum mina Allahi min AAasimin waman yudlili Allahu fama lahu min hadin

34.author: Ahmed Ali
34.text: Joseph had indeed come to you before with clear proofs, but you did not cease to doubt what he had brought until he died, when you said: 'God will not send a prophet after him.' That is how God leads the waster, the sceptic astray.

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: “And indeed Yusuf came to you with clear signs before this, thereupon you remained doubtful concerning what he had brought; to the extent that when he died, you said, ‘Allah will surely not send any Noble Messenger after him’”; this is how Allah sends astray whoever transgresses, is doubtful. –

34.author: Arberry
34.text: Joseph brought you the clear signs before, yet you continued in doubt concerning that he brought you until, when he perished, you said, "God will never send forth a Messenger after him." Even, so God leads astray the prodigal and the doubter.'

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: And assuredly there came Unto you Yusuf aforetime with evidences, yet ye ceased not to be in doubt concerning that which he brought Unto you, until when he died, ye said: God shall by no means raise an apostle after him. Thus Allah keepeth astray one who is extravagant and a doubter.

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: And indeed Yusuf (Joseph) did come to you, in times gone by, with clear signs, but you ceased not to doubt in that which he did bring to you, till when he died you said: "No Messenger will Allah send after him." Thus Allah leaves astray him who is a Musrif (a polytheist, oppressor, a criminal, sinner who commit great sins) and a Murtab (one who doubts Allah's Warning and His Oneness).

34.author: Itani
34.text: Joseph had come to you with clear revelations, but you continued to doubt what he came to you with. Until, when he perished, you said, “God will never send a messenger after him.” Thus God leads astray the outrageous skeptic.

34.author: Maududi
34.text: Verily Joseph came to you with Clear Signs before, yet you continued to doubt his Message. Thereafter when he died, you said: 'Allah shall send no Messenger after him.'” Thus Allah leads astray those who transgress the limits and are given to much doubting;

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: And indeed Yusuf did come to you, in times gone by, with clear signs, but you ceased not to doubt in that which he did bring to you, till when he died, you said: "No Messenger will Allah send after him." Thus Allah leaves astray him who is a transgressor and a skeptic.

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: And verily Joseph brought you of old clear proofs, yet ye ceased not to be in doubt concerning what he brought you till, when he died, ye said: Allah will not send any messenger after him. Thus Allah deceiveth him who is a prodigal, a doubter.

34.author: Qarai
34.text: Certainly Joseph brought you manifest proofs earlier, but you continued to remain in doubt concerning what he had brought you. When he died, you said, ‘‘Allah will never send any apostle after him.’’ That is how Allah leads astray those who are unrestrained and skeptical.

34.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
34.text: Before this, Joseph brought you clear signs, but you continued to be doubtful of that which he brought. When he passed away you said: "Allah will never send another Messenger after him." As such Allah leads astray the sinner, the doubter.

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: And Joseph had already come to you before with clear proofs, but you remained in doubt of that which he brought to you, until when he died, you said, 'Never will Allah send a messenger after him.' Thus does Allah leave astray he who is a transgressor and skeptic."

34.author: Sarwar
34.text: Joseph came to you before with illustrious evidence but you still have doubts about what he brought. When he passed away, you said, "God will never send any Messenger after him." Thus does God causes to go astray the skeptical transgressing people,

34.author: Shakir
34.text: And certainly Yusuf came to you before with clear arguments, but you ever remained in doubt as to what he brought; until when he died, you said: Allah will never raise an apostle after him. Thus does Allah cause him to err who is extravagant, a doubter

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: Joseph came to you before with clear signs, but you never ceased to doubt the message he brought you. When he died, you said, 'God will not send another messenger.'"" In this way God leaves the transgressors and doubters to go astray --

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: "And to you there came Joseph in times gone by, with Clear Signs, but ye ceased not to doubt of the (Mission) for which he had come: At length, when he died, ye said: 'No messenger will Allah send after him.' thus doth Allah leave to stray such as transgress and live in doubt,-

34.author: Transliteration
34.text: Walaqad jaakum yoosufu min qablu bialbayyinati fama ziltum fee shakkin mimma jaakum bihi hatta itha halaka qultum lan yabAAatha Allahu min baAAdihi rasoolan kathalika yudillu Allahu man huwa musrifun murtabun

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: Those who dispute God's revelations, with no authority having come to them, (are) greatly odious in the sight of God, and the sight of those who believe. That is how God seals every proud and perverse heart."

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: Those who dispute regarding the signs of Allah without any proof having come to them; how very disgusting this is, in the sight of Allah and in the sight of the believers! This is how Allah seals the entire heart of every haughty, rebellious person.

35.author: Arberry
35.text: (Those who dispute concerning the signs of God, without any authority come to them, very hateful is that in the sight of God and the believers; 'so God sets a seal on every heart proud', arrogant.)

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: Those who wrangle concerning the signs of Allah without any authority that hath come Unto them. It is greatly abhorrent Unto Allah and Unto those who believe. Thus Allah sealeth up the heart of any stiff-necked, high-handed, person.

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: Those who dispute about the Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) of Allah, without any authority that has come to them, it is greatly hateful and disgusting to Allah and to those who believe. Thus does Allah seal up the heart of every arrogant, tyrant. (So they cannot guide themselves to the Right Path).

35.author: Itani
35.text: Those who argue against God’s revelations, without any proof having come to them—a heinous sin in the sight of God, and of those who believe. Thus God seals the heart of every proud bully.

35.author: Maududi
35.text: those who contend regarding Allah's Signs without any evidence that might have come to them. That is exceedingly loathsome to Allah and to those that believe. Thus does Allah seal the heart of everyone who is proud and high-handed.

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: Those who dispute about the Ayat of Allah, without any authority that has come to them, it is greatly hateful and disgusting to Allah and to those who believe. Thus does Allah seal up the heart of every arrogant, tyrant.

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: Those who wrangle concerning the revelations of Allah without any warrant that hath come unto them, it is greatly hateful in the sight of Allah and in the sight of those who believe. Thus doth Allah print on every arrogant, disdainful heart.

35.author: Qarai
35.text: Those who dispute the signs of Allah without any authority that may have come to them—[that is] greatly outrageous to Allah and to those who have faith. That is how Allah seals the heart of every arrogant tyrant.’

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: Those who dispute the signs of Allah without any authority having reached them are very hateful before Allah and the believers. As such Allah sets a seal on every heart that is proud and arrogant.

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: Those who dispute concerning the signs of Allah without an authority having come to them - great is hatred [of them] in the sight of Allah and in the sight of those who have believed. Thus does Allah seal over every heart [belonging to] an arrogant tyrant.

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: those who dispute the revelations of God without having received clear authority. This act greatly angers God and the believers. Thus does God seal the hearts of every arrogant oppressor."

35.author: Shakir
35.text: Those who dispute concerning the communications of Allah without any authority that He has given them; greatly hated is it by Allah and by-those who believe. Thus does Allah set a seal over the heart of every proud, haughty one.

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: those who dispute God's revelations without any authority are doing something that is greatly abhorrent to God and to the believers. That is how God seals up the heart of every arrogant oppressor.

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: "(Such) as dispute about the Signs of Allah, without any authority that hath reached them, grievous and odious (is such conduct) in the sight of Allah and of the Believers. Thus doth Allah, seal up every heart - of arrogant and obstinate Transgressors."

35.author: Transliteration
35.text: Allatheena yujadiloona fee ayati Allahi bighayri sultanin atahum kabura maqtan AAinda Allahi waAAinda allatheena amanoo kathalika yatbaAAu Allahu AAala kulli qalbi mutakabbirin jabbarin

36.author: Ahmed Ali
36.text: The Pharaoh said: "O Haman, build me a lofty tower that I may perhaps find the means

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: And said Firaun, “O Haman! Build a high palace for me, in order that I may reach the routes.” –

36.author: Arberry
36.text: Pharaoh said, 'Haman, build for me a tower, that haply so I may reach the cords,

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: And Fir'awn said: O Haman! build for me a tower that haply I may reach the ways of access

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: And Fir'aun (Pharaoh) said: "O Haman! Build me a tower that I may arrive at the ways,

36.author: Itani
36.text: And Pharaoh said, “O Hamaan, build me a tower, that I may reach the pathways.

36.author: Maududi
36.text: Pharaoh said: “Haman, build for me a lofty tower that I may scale the highways --

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: And Fir`awn said: "O Haman! Build me a lofty tower that I may arrive at the ways --

36.author: Pickthall
36.text: And Pharaoh said: O Haman! Build for me a tower that haply I may reach the roads,

36.author: Qarai
36.text: Pharaoh said, ‘O Haman! Build me a tower so that I may reach the routes

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: Pharaoh said: 'Haman, build me a tower that I can reach the ways,

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: And Pharaoh said, "O Haman, construct for me a tower that I might reach the ways -

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: Pharaoh said, "Haman, build a tower of baked bricks for me so that I shall have access.

36.author: Shakir
36.text: And Firon said: O Haman! build for me a tower that I may attain the means of access,

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: Pharaoh said, "O Haman, build for me a lofty building so that I may gain access

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: Pharaoh said: "O Haman! Build me a lofty palace, that I may attain the ways and means-

36.author: Transliteration
36.text: Waqala firAAawnu ya hamanu ibni lee sarhan laAAallee ablughu alasbaba

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: Of reaching the tracts of heaven and look at the god of Moses, though I think that he is a liar." Thus were the evil deeds of Pharaoh made to look attractive to him, and he was hindered from the path. So the stratagem of Pharaoh was bound to perish.

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: “The routes of the heavens, in order to glance at the God of Moosa – and indeed I think he is a liar”; this is how the evil deeds of Firaun were made seeming good to him, and he was stopped from the path; and the evil scheme of Firaun was destined to be ruined.

37.author: Arberry
37.text: the cords of the heavens, and look upon Moses' God; for I think that he is a liar.' So the evil of his deeds was decked out fair to Pharaoh, and he was barred from the way, and Pharaoh's guile came only to ruin.

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: The way of access to the heavens so that may be apprised of the God of Musa, and verily I believe him to be a liar. And thus fair- seeming was made Unto FIr'awn the evil of his work, and he was hindered from the path. And the plot of Fir'awn ended only in ruin.

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: "The ways of the heavens, and I may look upon the Ilah (God) of Musa (Moses) but verily, I think him to be a liar." Thus it was made fair-seeming, in Fir'aun's (Pharaoh) eyes, the evil of his deeds, and he was hindered from the (Right) Path, and the plot of Fir'aun (Pharaoh) led to nothing but loss and destruction (for him).

37.author: Itani
37.text: The pathways of the heavens, so that I may glance at the God of Moses; though I think he is lying.” Thus Pharaoh’s evil deeds were made to appear good to him, and he was averted from the path. Pharaoh's guile was only in defeat.

37.author: Maududi
37.text: the highways to the heavens -- and have a look at the God of Moses, although I am certain that Moses is a liar.” Thus Pharaoh's evil deed was made to seem fair to him, and he was barred from the Right Path. Pharaoh's guile only led him to his own perdition.

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: The ways of the heavens, and I may look upon the God of Musa, but verily, I think him to be a liar." Thus it was made fair seeming, in Fir`awn's eyes, the evil of his deeds, and he was hindered from the path; and the plot of Fir`awn led to nothing but loss and destruction.

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: The roads of the heavens, and may look upon the god of Moses, though verily I think him a liar. Thus was the evil that he did made fairseeming unto Pharaoh, and he was debarred from the (right) way. The plot of Pharaoh ended but in ruin.

37.author: Qarai
37.text: —the routes of the heavens—and take a look at the God of Moses, and indeed I consider him a liar.’ The evil of his conduct was thus presented as decorous to Pharaoh, and he was kept from the way [of Allah]. Pharaoh’s stratagems only led him into ruin.

37.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
37.text: the ways of heaven so that I may look upon the God of Moses, because I think that he is a liar' And so Pharaoh's evil deeds were made to seem fair to him, and he was barred from the Way. And Pharaoh's guile came to nothing except ruin.

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: The ways into the heavens - so that I may look at the deity of Moses; but indeed, I think he is a liar." And thus was made attractive to Pharaoh the evil of his deed, and he was averted from the [right] way. And the plan of Pharaoh was not except in ruin.

37.author: Sarwar
37.text: to the heavens and be able to climb up to the Lord of Moses. I think that Moses is lying." Thus, Pharaoh's evil deeds seemed attractive to him and prevented him from the right path. The Pharaoh's plots only led him to his own destructor.

37.author: Shakir
37.text: The means of access to the heavens, then reach the god of Musa, and I surely think him to be a liar. And thus the evil of his deed was made fairseeming to Firon, and he was turned away from the way; and the struggle of Firon was not (to end) in aught but destruction.

37.author: Wahiduddin Khan
37.text: to the heavens, so that I may look upon the God of Moses: I am convinced that he is a liar!" That is how Pharaoh's evil actions were made to look fair in the eyes of Pharoah, and he was turned away from the path [of truth]. Pharaoh's scheming led to nothing but ruin.

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: "The ways and means of (reaching) the heavens, and that I may mount up to the god of Moses: But as far as I am concerned, I think (Moses) is a liar!" Thus was made alluring, in Pharaoh's eyes, the evil of his deeds, and he was hindered from the Path; and the plot of Pharaoh led to nothing but perdition (for him).

37.author: Transliteration
37.text: Asbaba alssamawati faattaliAAa ila ilahi moosa wainnee laathunnuhu kathiban wakathalika zuyyina lifirAAawna sooo AAamalihi wasudda AAani alssabeeli wama kaydu firAAawna illa fee tababin

38.author: Ahmed Ali
38.text: The man who believed said: "O my people, follow me; I will guide you to the right path.

38.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
38.text: And the believer said, “O my people! Follow me, I shall show you the way of righteousness.”

38.author: Arberry
38.text: Then said he who believed, 'My people, follow me, and I will guide you in the way of rectitude.

38.author: Daryabadi
38.text: And he who believed said: O my people! follow me, I shall guide you to the path of rectitude,

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: And the man who believed said: "O my people! Follow me, I will guide you to the way of right conduct [i.e. guide you to Allah's religion of Islamic Monotheism with which Musa (Moses) has been sent].

38.author: Itani
38.text: The one who had believed said, “O my people, follow me, and I will guide you to the path of rectitude.”

38.author: Maududi
38.text: The person endowed with faith said: “My people, follow me; I shall direct you to the Path of Rectitude.

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: And the man who believed said: "O my people! Follow me, I will guide you to the way of right conduct."

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: And he who believed said: O my people! Follow me. I will show you the way of right conduct.

38.author: Qarai
38.text: And he who had faith said, ‘O my people! Follow me; I will guide you to the way of rectitude.

38.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
38.text: He who was a believer said: 'Follow me, my nation, so that I may guide you to the Right Path.

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: And he who believed said, "O my people, follow me, I will guide you to the way of right conduct.

38.author: Sarwar
38.text: The believing man said, "My people, follow me and I shall show you the right guidance.

38.author: Shakir
38.text: And he who believed said: O my people! follow me, I will guide you to the right course;

38.author: Wahiduddin Khan
38.text: The believer said, "My people, follow me! I will guide you to the right path.

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: The man who believed said further: "O my people! Follow me: I will lead you to the Path of Right.

38.author: Transliteration
38.text: Waqala allathee amana ya qawmi ittabiAAooni ahdikum sabeela alrrashadi

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: O people, the life of this world is ephemeral; but enduring is the abode of the Hereafter.

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: “O my people! The life of this world is just a brief usage, and indeed the next abode is one of everlasting stay.”

39.author: Arberry
39.text: O my people, surely this present life is but a passing enjoyment; surely the world to come is the abode of stability.

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: O My people! this life of the world Is but a possi enjoyment, and verily the Hereafter! that is the Abode of rest.

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: "O my people! Truly, this life of the world is nothing but a (quick passing) enjoyment, and verily, the Hereafter that is the home that will remain forever."

39.author: Itani
39.text: “O my people, the life of this world is nothing but fleeting enjoyment, but the Hereafter is the Home of Permanence.

39.author: Maududi
39.text: My people, the life of this world is ephemeral, whereas the Hereafter, that is the permanent abode.

39.author: Mubarakpuri
39.text: "O my people! Truly, this life of the world is nothing but an enjoyment, and verily, the Hereafter that is the home that will remain forever."

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: O my people! Lo! this life of the world is but a passing comfort, and lo! the Hereafter, that is the enduring home.

39.author: Qarai
39.text: O my people! This life of the world is only a [passing] enjoyment, and indeed the Hereafter is the abiding home.

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: O my nation, the life of this world is nothing but an enjoyment, but surely, the life of the Everlasting Life is the stable abode.

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: O my people, this worldly life is only [temporary] enjoyment, and indeed, the Hereafter - that is the home of [permanent] settlement.

39.author: Sarwar
39.text: My people, this worldly life is only the means (to an end), but the life hereafter will be the everlasting abode.

39.author: Shakir
39.text: O my people! this life of the world is only a (passing) enjoyment, and surely the hereafter is the abode to settle;

39.author: Wahiduddin Khan
39.text: O my people, the life of this world is only a temporary provision; and the Hereafter is the permanent abode.

39.author: Yusuf Ali
39.text: "O my people! This life of the present is nothing but (temporary) convenience: It is the Hereafter that is the Home that will last.

39.author: Transliteration
39.text: Ya qawmi innama hathihi alhayatu alddunya mataAAun wainna alakhirata hiya daru alqarari

40.author: Ahmed Ali
40.text: Whoever does evil will be requited in accordance with it; but whoever does right, whether man or woman, and is a believer, will enter Paradise, where they will have provision in abundance.

40.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
40.text: “Whoever commits an evil deed will not be repaid except to the same extent; and whoever does good deeds, whether a man or a woman, and is a Muslim, will be admitted into Paradise, in which they will receive sustenance without account.”

40.author: Arberry
40.text: Whosoever does an evil deed shall be recompensed only with the like of it, but whosoever does a righteous deed, be it male or female, believing -- those shall enter Paradise, therein provided without reckoning.

40.author: Daryabadi
40.text: Whosoever worketh an evil, he shallnotberequited except the like thereof; and whosoever, male or female, worketh righteously, and is a believer-they will enter the Garden wherein they will be pro vided for without measure.

40.author: Hilali & Khan
40.text: "Whosoever does an evil deed, will not be requited except the like thereof, and whosoever does a righteous deed, whether male or female and is a true believer (in the Oneness of Allah), such will enter Paradise, where they will be provided therein (with all things in abundance) without limit.

40.author: Itani
40.text: Whoever commits a sin will be repaid only with its like. But whoever works righteousness, whether male or female, and is a believer—these will enter Paradise, where they will be provided for without account.

40.author: Maududi
40.text: Whosoever does an evil deed will be requited only with the like of it; and whosoever acts righteously and has attained to faith -- be he a male or a female -- they shall enter Paradise and be provided sustenance beyond all reckoning.

40.author: Mubarakpuri
40.text: "Whoever does an evil deed, will not be requited except the like thereof; and whosoever does a righteous deed, whether male or female and is a true believer, such will enter Paradise, where they will be provided therein without limit."

40.author: Pickthall
40.text: Whoso doeth an ill-deed, he will be repaid the like thereof, while whoso doeth right, whether male or female, and is a believer, (all) such will enter the Garden, where they will be nourished without stint.

40.author: Qarai
40.text: Whoever commits a misdeed shall not be requited except with its like, but whoever acts righteously, whether male or female, should he be faithful—such shall enter paradise, and they will be provided therein without any reckoning.

40.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
40.text: Those who do an evil deed shall only be rewarded with its like, but those who believe and do good works, either men and women, shall enter the Gardens of Paradise and are provided for without reckoning.

40.author: Saheeh International
40.text: Whoever does an evil deed will not be recompensed except by the like thereof; but whoever does righteousness, whether male or female, while he is a believer - those will enter Paradise, being given provision therein without account.

40.author: Sarwar
40.text: Whoever commits evil deeds will be recompensed to the same degree. The righteously striving believer, male or female, will enter Paradise wherein they will receive their sustenance without any account being kept.

40.author: Shakir
40.text: Whoever does an evil, he shall not be recompensed (with aught) but the like of it, and whoever does good, whether male or female, and he is a believer, these shall enter the garden, in which they shall be given sustenance without measure.

40.author: Wahiduddin Khan
40.text: Whoever does evil will be requited with evil; but whoever does good, whether male or female, and is a believer, will enter the Garden; where they will be provided for without measure.

40.author: Yusuf Ali
40.text: "He that works evil will not be requited but by the like thereof: and he that works a righteous deed - whether man or woman - and is a Believer- such will enter the Garden (of Bliss): Therein will they have abundance without measure.

40.author: Transliteration
40.text: Man AAamila sayyiatan fala yujza illa mithlaha waman AAamila salihan min thakarin aw ontha wahuwa muminun faolaika yadkhuloona aljannata yurzaqoona feeha bighayri hisabin

41.author: Ahmed Ali
41.text: O my people, what is wrong with me that I am calling you to preservation, while you invite me to the Fire!

41.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
41.text: “And O my people! What is the matter with me that I call you towards salvation whereas you call me towards hell?”

41.author: Arberry
41.text: O my people, how is it with me, that I call you to salvation, and you call me to the Fire?

41.author: Daryabadi
41.text: And, O my people! what aileth me that call you Unto deliverance, while ye call me Unto the Fire!

41.author: Hilali & Khan
41.text: "And O my people! How is it that I call you to salvation while you call me to the Fire!

41.author: Itani
41.text: O my people, how is it that I call you to salvation, and you call me to the Fire?

41.author: Maududi
41.text: My people, how is it that I call you to salvation while you call me to the Fire;

41.author: Mubarakpuri
41.text: "And O my people! How is it that I call you to salvation while you call me to the Fire!"

41.author: Pickthall
41.text: And, O my people! What aileth me that I call you unto deliverance when ye call me unto the Fire?

41.author: Qarai
41.text: O my people! [Think,] what makes me invite you to deliverance, while you invite me toward the Fire?

41.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
41.text: My nation, how is it that I call you to salvation and you call me to the Fire?

41.author: Saheeh International
41.text: And O my people, how is it that I invite you to salvation while you invite me to the Fire?

41.author: Sarwar
41.text: My people, "How strange is it that I invite you to salvation when you invite me to the fire.

41.author: Shakir
41.text: And, O my people! how is it that I call you to salvation and you call me to the fire?

41.author: Wahiduddin Khan
41.text: My people! How is it that I call you to salvation, while you call me to the Fire?

41.author: Yusuf Ali
41.text: "And O my people! How (strange) it is for me to call you to Salvation while ye call me to the Fire!

41.author: Transliteration
41.text: Waya qawmi ma lee adAAookum ila alnnajati watadAAoonanee ila alnnari

42.author: Ahmed Ali
42.text: You are asking me to disbelieve in God, and to associate with Him that of which I have no knowledge, yet I invite you to the all-mighty, all-forgiving.

42.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
42.text: “You call me to disbelieve in Allah and ascribe such as partners to Him, regarding whom I do not have any knowledge – whereas I call you towards the Most Honourable, the Oft Forgiving!”

42.author: Arberry
42.text: You call me to disbelieve in God, and to associate with Him that whereof I have no knowledge, while I call you to the All-mighty, the All-forgiving.

42.author: Daryabadi
42.text: Ye call me for this, that should O blaspheme against God and associate with Him that whereof have no knowledge, call you onto the Mighty, the

42.author: Hilali & Khan
42.text: "You invite me to disbelieve in Allah (and in His Oneness), and to join partners in worship with Him; of which I have no knowledge, and I invite you to the All-Mighty, the Oft-Forgiving!

42.author: Itani
42.text: You call me to reject God, and to associate with Him what I have no knowledge of, while I call you to the Mighty Forgiver.

42.author: Maududi
42.text: you call me to deny Allah and to associate with Him as His partners those regarding whom I have no knowledge (that they are Allah's partners in His Divinity), whereas I call you to the Most Mighty, the Most Forgiving?

42.author: Mubarakpuri
42.text: "You invite me to disbelieve in Allah, and to join partners in worship with Him of which I have no knowledge; and I invite you to the Almighty, the Oft-Forgiving!"

42.author: Pickthall
42.text: Ye call me to disbelieve in Allah and ascribe unto Him as partners that whereof I have no knowledge, while I call you unto the Mighty, the Forgiver.

42.author: Qarai
42.text: You invite me to defy Allah and to ascribe to Him partners of which I have no knowledge, while I call you to the All-mighty, the All-forgiver.

42.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
42.text: You call me to disbelieve in Allah and to associate with Him that of which I know nothing; while I call you to the Almighty, the Forgiving.

42.author: Saheeh International
42.text: You invite me to disbelieve in Allah and associate with Him that of which I have no knowledge, and I invite you to the Exalted in Might, the Perpetual Forgiver.

42.author: Sarwar
42.text: You call me to disbelieve in God and to believe other things equal to Him about which I have no knowledge. I call you to the Majestic and All-forgiving One.

42.author: Shakir
42.text: You call on me that I should disbelieve in Allah and associate with Him that of which I have no knowledge, and I call you to the Mighty, the most Forgiving;

42.author: Wahiduddin Khan
42.text: You call upon me to deny God and to serve other deities about which I have no knowledge, while I call you to the Almighty, the Forgiver.

42.author: Yusuf Ali
42.text: "Ye do call upon me to blaspheme against Allah, and to join with Him partners of whom I have no knowledge; and I call you to the Exalted in Power, Who forgives again and again!"

42.author: Transliteration
42.text: TadAAoonanee liakfura biAllahi waoshrika bihi ma laysa lee bihi AAilmun waana adAAookum ila alAAazeezi alghaffari

43.author: Ahmed Ali
43.text: What you are calling me to is surely not worth the calling in this world or in the next, because our returning is to God; and because the transgressors will be inmates of Hell.

43.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
43.text: “So it is self evident that what you call me towards has no benefit being prayed to, either in this world or in the Hereafter, and that our return is towards Allah, and that the transgressors only are the people of the fire.”

43.author: Arberry
43.text: No doubt that what you call me to has no call heard, in this world or in the world to come, that to God we return, and that the prodigal are the inhabitants of the Fire.

43.author: Daryabadi
43.text: Without doubt ye only call me Unto that which is not to be invoked in the world nor in the Hereafter; and verily our return shall be Unto God, and the extravagant! they shall be fellows of the Fire.

43.author: Hilali & Khan
43.text: "No doubt you call me to (worship) one who cannot grant (me) my request (or respond to my invocation) in this world or in the Hereafter. And our return will be to Allah, and Al-Musrifun (i.e. polytheists and arrogants, those who commit great sins, the transgressors of Allah's set limits)! They shall be the dwellers of the Fire!

43.author: Itani
43.text: Without a doubt, what you call me to has no say in this world, or in the Hereafter; and our turning back is to God; and the transgressors are the inmates of the Fire.

43.author: Maududi
43.text: There is no doubt that those whom you call me to have no claim to be called upon in this world and in the Hereafter. Certainly to Allah shall be our return, and those who exceed the limits are destined to the Fire.

43.author: Mubarakpuri
43.text: "La Jarama, you call me to one that does not have a claim in this world or in the Hereafter. And our return will be to Allah, and the transgressors, they shall be the dwellers of the Fire!"

43.author: Pickthall
43.text: Assuredly that whereunto ye call me hath no claim in the world or in the Hereafter, and our return will be unto Allah, and the prodigals will be owners of the Fire.

43.author: Qarai
43.text: Undoubtedly, that to which you invite me has no invitation in the world nor in the Hereafter, and indeed our return will be to Allah, and indeed it is the transgressors who will be inmates of the Fire.

43.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
43.text: There is no doubt that what you call me to has neither a call in this world, nor in the Everlasting Life. To Allah we shall return, and the excessive sinners are the inhabitants of the Fire.

43.author: Saheeh International
43.text: Assuredly, that to which you invite me has no [response to a] supplication in this world or in the Hereafter; and indeed, our return is to Allah, and indeed, the transgressors will be companions of the Fire.

43.author: Sarwar
43.text: The idols to which you invite me certainly have no claim to be deities in this world or in the life to come. Our return is to God and the transgressors will be the dwellers of hell fire.

43.author: Shakir
43.text: No doubt that what you call me to has no title to be called to in this world, nor in the hereafter, and that our turning back is to Allah, and that the extravagant are the inmates of the fire;

43.author: Wahiduddin Khan
43.text: Surely that to which you call me has no say in this world or in the life to come, that our return is to God alone, and that the transgressors shall be the inmates of the Fire.

43.author: Yusuf Ali
43.text: "Without doubt ye do call me to one who is not fit to be called to, whether in this world, or in the Hereafter; our return will be to Allah; and the Transgressors will be Companions of the Fire!

43.author: Transliteration
43.text: La jarama annama tadAAoonanee ilayhi laysa lahu daAAwatun fee alddunya wala fee alakhirati waanna maraddana ila Allahi waanna almusrifeena hum ashabu alnnari

44.author: Ahmed Ali
44.text: You will remember what I say in time to come; I submit my case to the judgement of God. Surely God keeps an eye on His creatures."

44.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
44.text: “And soon the time will come when you will remember what I now say to you; and I entrust my tasks to Allah; indeed Allah sees the bondmen.”

44.author: Arberry
44.text: You will remember what I say to you. I commit my affair to God; surely God sees His servants.'

44.author: Daryabadi
44.text: And anon ye shall remember that which am telling you. Confide my affair Unto God; verily God is the Beholder of His bondmen.

44.author: Hilali & Khan
44.text: "And you will remember what I am telling you, and my affair I leave it to Allah. Verily, Allah is the All-Seer of (His) slaves."

44.author: Itani
44.text: You will remember what I am telling you, so I commit my case to God. God is Observant of the servants.”

44.author: Maududi
44.text: Soon you shall remember what I say to you. I entrust my affairs to Allah. Surely Allah is watchful over His servants.”

44.author: Mubarakpuri
44.text: "And you will remember what I am telling you, and my affair I leave it to Allah. Verily, Allah is the All-Seer of (His) servants."

44.author: Pickthall
44.text: And ye will remember what I say unto you. I confide my cause unto Allah. Lo! Allah is Seer of (His) slaves.

44.author: Qarai
44.text: Soon you will remember what I tell you, and I entrust my affair to Allah. Indeed Allah watches His servants.’

44.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
44.text: You will remember what I say to you. To Allah I commit my affair, surely, Allah sees His worshipers'

44.author: Saheeh International
44.text: And you will remember what I [now] say to you, and I entrust my affair to Allah. Indeed, Allah is Seeing of [His] servants."

44.author: Sarwar
44.text: You will soon recall what I have told you. I entrust God with my affairs. God is Well Aware of His servants."

44.author: Shakir
44.text: So you shall remember what I say to you, and I entrust my affair to Allah, Surely Allah sees the servants.

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: Soon you will remember what I say to you! I shall entrust my affair to God, for God is observant of all [His] servants."

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: "Soon will ye remember what I say to you (now), My (own) affair I commit to Allah: for Allah (ever) watches over His Servants."

44.author: Transliteration
44.text: Fasatathkuroona ma aqoolu lakum waofawwidu amree ila Allahi inna Allaha baseerun bialAAibadi

45.author: Ahmed Ali
45.text: So God preserved him from the evil they were planning; and a dreadful doom encompassed the people of Pharaoh:

45.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
45.text: Therefore Allah saved him from the evils of their scheming, and an evil punishment enveloped the people of Firaun. –

45.author: Arberry
45.text: So God guarded him against the evil things of their devising, and there encompassed the folk of Pharaoh the evil chastisement,

45.author: Daryabadi
45.text: Wherefore Allah protected him from ills which they plotted, and there surrounded the family of Fir'awn the evil of torment.

45.author: Hilali & Khan
45.text: So Allah saved him from the evils that they plotted (against him), while an evil torment encompassed Fir'aun's (Pharaoh) people.

45.author: Itani
45.text: So God protected him from the evils of their scheming, while a terrible torment besieged Pharaoh’s clan.

45.author: Maududi
45.text: Eventually Allah saved the person endowed with faith from all the evils of their guile, and a woeful chastisement encompassed the Pharaonites.

45.author: Mubarakpuri
45.text: So, Allah saved him from the evils that they plotted (against him), while an evil torment encompassed Fir`awn's people.

45.author: Pickthall
45.text: So Allah warded off from him the evils which they plotted, while a dreadful doom encompassed Pharaoh's folk,

45.author: Qarai
45.text: Then Allah saved him from their evil schemes, while a terrible punishment besieged Pharaoh’s clan:

45.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
45.text: Allah saved him from the evils that they devised, and an evil punishment encompassed Pharaoh's people.

45.author: Saheeh International
45.text: So Allah protected him from the evils they plotted, and the people of Pharaoh were enveloped by the worst of punishment -

45.author: Sarwar
45.text: God protected him against their evil plans and the people of the Pharaoh were struck by the most horrible torment.

45.author: Shakir
45.text: So Allah protected him from the evil (consequences) of what they planned, and the most evil punishment overtook Firon's people:

45.author: Wahiduddin Khan
45.text: Thus, God delivered him from the evils which they plotted, and the companions of Pharaoh themselves were encompassed by a dreadful scourge;

45.author: Yusuf Ali
45.text: Then Allah saved him from (every) ill that they plotted (against him), but the burnt of the Penalty encompassed on all sides the People of Pharaoh.

45.author: Transliteration
45.text: Fawaqahu Allahu sayyiati ma makaroo wahaqa biali firAAawna sooo alAAathabi

46.author: Ahmed Ali
46.text: Fire, to which they are exposed morning and evening. The day the Hour is proclaimed (it will be said:) "Admit the people of Pharaoh to the severest punishment,"

46.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
46.text: The fire – upon which they are presented morning and evening; and when the Last Day is established – “Put the people of Firaun into the most severe punishment.” (Punishment in the grave is proven by this verse.)

46.author: Arberry
46.text: the Fire, to which they shall be exposed morning and evening; and on the day when the Hour is come: 'Admit the folk of Pharaoh into the most terrible chastisement!'

46.author: Daryabadi
46.text: The Fire! They are exposed thereto morning and evening. And on the Day whereon the Hour will uprise, it will be said: cause the family of Fir'awn to enter the most grievous torment.

46.author: Hilali & Khan
46.text: The Fire; they are exposed to it, morning and afternoon, and on the Day when the Hour will be established (it will be said to the angels): "Cause Fir'aun's (Pharaoh) people to enter the severest torment!"

46.author: Itani
46.text: The Fire. They will be exposed to it morning and evening. And on the Day the Hour takes place: “Admit the clan of Pharaoh to the most intense agony.”

46.author: Maududi
46.text: They are exposed to the Fire every morning and evening; and when the Last Hour will come to pass, a command shall be given: “Admit the Pharaonites to an even more severe chastisement.”

46.author: Mubarakpuri
46.text: The Fire, they are exposed to it, morning and afternoon. And on the Day when the Hour will be established (it will be said to the angels): "Admit Fir`awn's people into the severest torment!"

46.author: Pickthall
46.text: The Fire; they are exposed to it morning and evening; and on the day when the Hour upriseth (it is said): Cause Pharaoh's folk to enter the most awful doom.

46.author: Qarai
46.text: the Fire, to which they are exposed morning and evening. On the day when the Hour sets in, Pharaoh’s clan will enter the severest punishment.

46.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
46.text: (Before) the Fire they shall be exposed morning and evening, and on the Day when the Hour comes, (it will be said): 'Admit the family of Pharaoh into the most terrible punishment'

46.author: Saheeh International
46.text: The Fire, they are exposed to it morning and evening. And the Day the Hour appears [it will be said], "Make the people of Pharaoh enter the severest punishment."

46.author: Sarwar
46.text: They will be exposed to the fire in the mornings and the evenings and on the Day of Judgment, they will be told, "People of the Pharaoh, suffer the most severe torment."

46.author: Shakir
46.text: The fire; they shall be brought before it (every) morning and evening and on the day when the hour shall come to pass: Make Firon's people enter the severest chastisement.

46.author: Wahiduddin Khan
46.text: they will be brought before the Fire morning and evening. On the Day the Hour comes, [a voice will cry], "Mete out to Pharaoh's people the harshest punishment!"

46.author: Yusuf Ali
46.text: In front of the Fire will they be brought, morning and evening: And (the sentence will be) on the Day that Judgment will be established: "Cast ye the People of Pharaoh into the severest Penalty!"

46.author: Transliteration
46.text: Alnnaru yuAAradoona AAalayha ghuduwwan waAAashiyyan wayawma taqoomu alssaAAatu adkhiloo ala firAAawna ashadda alAAathabi

47.author: Ahmed Ali
47.text: As they will noisily argue in the Fire, the weaker ones will say to the arrogant: "We were your followers, so will you take over some of our share of the fire?"

47.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
47.text: And when they will quarrel amongst themselves in the fire, those who were weak will say to those who sought greatness, “We were your followers, so will you reduce from us some of the punishment of the fire?”

47.author: Arberry
47.text: And when they argue one with the other in the Fire, and the weak say unto those who waxed proud, 'Why, we were your followers; will you avail us now against any part of the Fire?'

47.author: Daryabadi
47.text: And consider what time they will wrangle together in the Fire, and the oppressed will say Unto those who were stiff-necked: verily we have been Unto you a following, are ye going to avail us against a portion of the Fire?

47.author: Hilali & Khan
47.text: And, when they will dispute in the Fire, the weak will say to those who were arrogant; "Verily! We followed you, can you then take from us some portion of the Fire?"

47.author: Itani
47.text: As they quarrel in the Fire, the weak will say to those who were arrogant, “We were followers of yours; will you then spare us a portion of the Fire?”

47.author: Maududi
47.text: Just imagine when they will remonstrate with one another in Hell. The weak ones will say to those who waxed proud: “We were your followers. Will you, then, lighten for us a part of our suffering of the Fire?”

47.author: Mubarakpuri
47.text: And, when they will dispute in the Fire, the weak will say to those who were arrogant: "Verily, we followed you, can you then take from us some portion of the Fire"

47.author: Pickthall
47.text: And when they wrangle in the Fire, the weak say unto those who were proud: Lo! we were a following unto you; will ye therefor rid us of a portion of the Fire?

47.author: Qarai
47.text: When they argue in the Fire, the oppressed will say to the oppressors, ‘We used to follow you; will you avail us against any portion of the Fire?’

47.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
47.text: And when they argue with one another in Hell, the weak will say to the proud: 'We were your followers, will you help us against any share of the Fire'

47.author: Saheeh International
47.text: And [mention] when they will argue within the Fire, and the weak will say to those who had been arrogant, "Indeed, we were [only] your followers, so will you relieve us of a share of the Fire?"

47.author: Sarwar
47.text: During a dispute in the fire, the suppressed ones will say to those who had dominated them, "We were your followers. Can you now relieve us of our suffering in the fire?"

47.author: Shakir
47.text: And when they shall contend one with another in the fire, then the weak shall say to those who were proud: Surely we were your followers; will you then avert from us a portion of the fire?

47.author: Wahiduddin Khan
47.text: When they dispute with one another in the Fire, the weak will say to those who deemed themselves mighty, "We were your followers; will you then relieve us of some of the Fire?"

47.author: Yusuf Ali
47.text: Behold, they will dispute with each other in the Fire! The weak ones (who followed) will say to those who had been arrogant, "We but followed you: Can ye then take (on yourselves) from us some share of the Fire?

47.author: Transliteration
47.text: Waith yatahajjoona fee alnnari fayaqoolu aldduAAafao lillatheena istakbaroo inna kunna lakum tabaAAan fahal antum mughnoona AAanna naseeban mina alnnari

48.author: Ahmed Ali
48.text: The arrogant will answer: "All of us are in it. Surely God has judged between His creatures."

48.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
48.text: Those who were proud will say, “We are all in the fire – indeed Allah has already passed the judgement among the bondmen.”

48.author: Arberry
48.text: Then those who waxed proud shall say, 'Every one of us is in it; indeed, God already has passed judgment between His servants.'

48.author: Daryabadi
48.text: Those who were stiff-necked will say: verily we are all in it; verily Allah hath judged between His bondmen.

48.author: Hilali & Khan
48.text: Those who were arrogant will say: "We are all (together) in this (Fire)! Verily Allah has judged between (His) slaves!"

48.author: Itani
48.text: Those who were arrogant will say, “We are all in it; God has judged between the servants.”

48.author: Maududi
48.text: Those who had waxed proud will reply: “All of us are in it. Allah has already passed His judgement among His servants.”

48.author: Mubarakpuri
48.text: Those who were arrogant will say: "We are all (together) in this (Fire)! Verily, Allah has judged between (His) servants!"

48.author: Pickthall
48.text: Those who were proud say: Lo! we are all (together) herein. Lo! Allah hath judged between (His) slaves.

48.author: Qarai
48.text: The oppressors will say, ‘We are all in it [together]; Allah has judged between [His] servants.’

48.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
48.text: But those who were proud will reply: 'All of us are in it (Hell). Allah has judged between His worshipers'

48.author: Saheeh International
48.text: Those who had been arrogant will say, "Indeed, all [of us] are in it. Indeed, Allah has judged between the servants."

48.author: Sarwar
48.text: The ones who had dominated them will say, "All of us are now in hell. God has already issued His Judgment of His servants (and no one can change this)."

48.author: Shakir
48.text: Those who were proud shall say: Surely we are all in it: surely Allah has judged between the servants.

48.author: Wahiduddin Khan
48.text: But those who had been arrogant will say, "We are all in this together. God has judged between His servants."

48.author: Yusuf Ali
48.text: Those who had been arrogant will say: "We are all in this (Fire)! Truly, Allah has judged between (his) Servants!"

48.author: Transliteration
48.text: Qala allatheena istakbaroo inna kullun feeha inna Allaha qad hakama bayna alAAibadi

49.author: Ahmed Ali
49.text: Those in the Fire will say to the warders of Hell: "Ask your Lord to reduce the punishment by a day for us."

49.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
49.text: And those who are in the fire said to its guards, “Pray to your Lord to decrease the punishment upon us for one day.”

49.author: Arberry
49.text: And those who are in the Fire will say to the keepers of Gehenna, 'Call on your Lord, to lighten for us one day of the chastisement!'

49.author: Daryabadi
49.text: And those in the Fire will say Unto the keepers of Hell: supplicate your Lord that He may lighten for us a day of the torment.

49.author: Hilali & Khan
49.text: And those in the Fire will say to the keepers (angels) of Hell: "Call upon your Lord to lighten for us the torment for a day!"

49.author: Itani
49.text: And those in the Fire will say to the keepers of Hell, “Call to your Lord to lessen our suffering for one day.”

49.author: Maududi
49.text: Those suffering in the Fire will say to the keepers of Hell: “Call upon your Lord to lighten the chastisement for us just for a day.”

49.author: Mubarakpuri
49.text: And those in the Fire will say to the keepers (angels) of Hell: "Call upon your Lord to lighten for us the torment for a day!"

49.author: Pickthall
49.text: And those in the Fire say unto the guards of hell: Entreat your Lord that He relieve us of a day of the torment.

49.author: Qarai
49.text: Those who are in the Fire will say to the keepers of hell, ‘Invoke your Lord to lighten for us [at least] a day’s punishment.’

49.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
49.text: And those in the Fire will say to the keepers of Gehenna (Hell): 'Call your Lord to lessen our punishment for one day'

49.author: Saheeh International
49.text: And those in the Fire will say to the keepers of Hell, "Supplicate your Lord to lighten for us a day from the punishment."

49.author: Sarwar
49.text: The dwellers of hell fire will ask its keepers, "Pray to your Lord to relieve us from the torment at least for one day".

49.author: Shakir
49.text: And those who are in the fire shall say to the keepers of hell: Call upon your Lord that He may lighten to us one day of the punishment.

49.author: Wahiduddin Khan
49.text: Those in the Fire will say to its keepers, "Implore your Lord to relieve our torment for one day,"

49.author: Yusuf Ali
49.text: Those in the Fire will say to the Keepers of Hell: "Pray to your Lord to lighten us the Penalty for a day (at least)!"

49.author: Transliteration
49.text: Waqala allatheena fee alnnari likhazanati jahannama odAAoo rabbakum yukhaffif AAanna yawman mina alAAathabi

50.author: Ahmed Ali
50.text: They will say: "Did not your apostles come to you with clear proofs?" They will answer: "Indeed, they did." "Then pray," will (the warders) say. But the praying of unbelievers will be all in vain.

50.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
50.text: They said, “Is it not that your Noble Messengers used to come to you with clear signs?” They said, “Why not, surely yes!” They said, “Then you yourselves pray”; and the prayer of the disbelievers is nothing but astray.

50.author: Arberry
50.text: They shall say, 'Did not your Messengers bring you the clear signs?' They shall say, 'Yes indeed.' They shall say, 'Then do you call!' But the calling of the unbelievers is only in error.

50.author: Daryabadi
50.text: They will say: came there not your apostles Unto you with evidences? They will say: Yea? They will say: supplicate then yourselves. And supplication of the infidels will be but in wandering.

50.author: Hilali & Khan
