50.text: They will say: "Did there not come to you, your Messengers with (clear) evidences and signs? They will say: "Yes." They will reply: "Then call (as you like)! And the invocation of the disbelievers is nothing but in error!"

50.author: Itani
50.text: They will say, “Did not your messengers come to you with clear signs?” They will say, “Yes.” They will say, “Then pray, but the prayers of the disbelievers will always be in vain.”

50.author: Maududi
50.text: The keepers of Hell will ask: “Did your Messengers not come to you with Clear Signs?” They will say: “Yes (they did).” The keepers of Hell will say: “Then you yourselves should call (upon the Lord). And the call of the unbelievers will end in vain.”

50.author: Mubarakpuri
50.text: They will say: "Did there not come to you, your Messengers with (clear) evidences" They will say: "Yes." They will reply: "Then call (as you like)! And the invocation of the disbelievers is nothing but in vain!"

50.author: Pickthall
50.text: They say: Came not your messengers unto you with clear proofs? They say: Yea, verily. They say: Then do ye pray, although the prayer of disbelievers is in vain.

50.author: Qarai
50.text: They will say, ‘Had not your apostles brought you manifest proofs?’ They will say, ‘Yes.’ They will say, ‘Then invoke [Him] yourselves.’ But the invocations of the faithless only go awry.

50.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
50.text: But they will say: 'Did your Messengers not come to you with clear signs' 'Yes indeed' they will answer. And they will reply: 'Then you call' But the calling of the unbelievers is only in error.

50.author: Saheeh International
50.text: They will say, "Did there not come to you your messengers with clear proofs?" They will say, "Yes." They will reply, "Then supplicate [yourselves], but the supplication of the disbelievers is not except in error."

50.author: Sarwar
50.text: The keepers will ask them, "Did your Messengers not come to you with illustrious evidence (of the Truth)? They will reply, "Yes, they did." The keepers will then say, "You may pray but the prayer of the disbelievers will not be answered".

50.author: Shakir
50.text: They shall say: Did not your apostles come to you with clear arguments? They shall say: Yea. They shall say: Then call. And the call of the unbelievers is only in error.

50.author: Wahiduddin Khan
50.text: but they will say, "Did not your messengers come to you with clear signs?" They will say, "Yes." The keepers will say, "Then pray [for help] yourselves." But the prayer of those who deny the truth is of no avail.

50.author: Yusuf Ali
50.text: They will say: "Did there not come to you your messengers with Clear Signs?" They will say, "Yes". They will reply, "Then pray (as ye like)! But the prayer of those without Faith is nothing but (futile wandering) in (mazes of) error!"

50.author: Transliteration
50.text: Qaloo awalam taku tateekum rusulukum bialbayyinati qaloo bala qaloo faodAAoo wama duAAao alkafireena illa fee dalalin

51.author: Ahmed Ali
51.text: We will certainly help Our messengers and those who believe, in this world, and on the day the witnesses take their stand,

51.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
51.text: Indeed We will surely help Our Noble Messengers, and the believers, in the life of this world and on the day when the witnesses will be standing.

51.author: Arberry
51.text: Surely We shall help Our Messengers and those who have believed, in the present life, and upon the day when the witnesses arise,

51.author: Daryabadi
51.text: Verily We! We shall surely succour Our apostles and those who believe, both in the life of the world and on a Day whereon the witnesses will stand forth.

51.author: Hilali & Khan
51.text: Verily, We will indeed make victorious Our Messengers and those who believe (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism) in this world's life and on the Day when the witnesses will stand forth, (i.e. Day of Resurrection),

51.author: Itani
51.text: Most surely We will support Our messengers and those who believe, in this life, and on the Day the witnesses arise.

51.author: Maududi
51.text: Surely We shall help Our Messengers and the believers in the life of this world and on the Day when witnesses will rise to testify,

51.author: Mubarakpuri
51.text: Verily, We will indeed make victorious Our Messengers and those who believe in this world's life and on the Day when the witnesses will stand forth, --

51.author: Pickthall
51.text: Lo! We verily do help Our messengers, and those who believe, in the life of the world and on the day when the witnesses arise,

51.author: Qarai
51.text: Indeed We shall help Our apostles and those who have faith in the life of the world and on the day when the witnesses rise up,

51.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
51.text: Surely, We shall help Our Messengers and the believers both in this world and on the Day when the witnesses rise.

51.author: Saheeh International
51.text: Indeed, We will support Our messengers and those who believe during the life of this world and on the Day when the witnesses will stand -

51.author: Sarwar
51.text: We shall help Our Messengers and the believers, in this life and on the day when witness will come forward.

51.author: Shakir
51.text: Most surely We help Our apostles, and those who believe, in this world's life and on the day when the witnesses shall stand

51.author: Wahiduddin Khan
51.text: Most surely We help our messengers, and those who believe, in the life of this world and on the Day when all the witnesses will stand up.

51.author: Yusuf Ali
51.text: We will, without doubt, help our messengers and those who believe, (both) in this world's life and on the Day when the Witnesses will stand forth,-

51.author: Transliteration
51.text: Inna lanansuru rusulana waallatheena amanoo fee alhayati alddunya wayawma yaqoomu alashhadu

52.author: Ahmed Ali
52.text: The day upon which their excuses will not benefit the evil-doers, and the condemnation and evil abode will be theirs.

52.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
52.text: The day on which the unjust will not gain any benefit from their excuses, and for them is the curse, and for them is the wretched home.

52.author: Arberry
52.text: upon the day when their excuses shall not profit the evildoers, and theirs shall be the curse, and theirs the evil abode.

52.author: Daryabadi
52.text: A Day whereon their excuse will not Profit the wrong- doers; and theirs Will be the curse and theirs the evil Abode.

52.author: Hilali & Khan
52.text: The Day when their excuses will be of no profit to Zalimun (polytheists, wrong-doers and disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah). Theirs will be the curse, and theirs will be the evil abode (i.e. painful torment in Hell-fire).

52.author: Itani
52.text: The Day when their excuses will not profit the wrongdoers, and the curse will be upon them, and they will have the Home of Misery.

52.author: Maududi
52.text: the Day when the excuses offered by the wrong-doers shall not avail them. They shall be victims of the curse and a woeful abode.

52.author: Mubarakpuri
52.text: The Day when their excuses will be of no profit to wrongdoers. Theirs will be the curse, and theirs will be the evil abode.

52.author: Pickthall
52.text: The day when their excuse availeth not the evil-doers, and theirs is the curse, and theirs the ill abode.

52.author: Qarai
52.text: the day when the excuses of the wrongdoers will not benefit them, the curse will lie on them, and for them will be the ills of the [ultimate] abode.

52.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
52.text: On that Day no excuse will benefit the harmdoers. Theirs shall be the curse, and an evil lodging.

52.author: Saheeh International
52.text: The Day their excuse will not benefit the wrongdoers, and they will have the curse, and they will have the worst home.

52.author: Sarwar
52.text: The excuses of the unjust will be of no benefit to them on that day. They will be condemned to live in a most terrible abode.

52.author: Shakir
52.text: The day on which their excuse shall not benefit the unjust, and for them is curse and for them is the evil abode.

52.author: Wahiduddin Khan
52.text: The Day when their excuses will be of no avail to the wrongdoers, the curse shall be their lot and they will have the most evil abode.

52.author: Yusuf Ali
52.text: The Day when no profit will it be to Wrong-doers to present their excuses, but they will (only) have the Curse and the Home of Misery.

52.author: Transliteration
52.text: Yawma la yanfaAAu alththalimeena maAAthiratuhum walahumu allaAAnatu walahum sooo alddari

53.author: Ahmed Ali
53.text: Verily We showed Moses the way, and bequeathed the Book to the children of Israel,

53.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
53.text: And indeed We gave Moosa the guidance, and bequeathed the Book to the Descendants of Israel.

53.author: Arberry
53.text: We also gave Moses the guidance, and We bequeathed upon the Children of Israel the Book

53.author: Daryabadi
53.text: And assuredly We vouchsafed Unto Musa the guidance, and We caused the Children of Isra'il to inherit the Book.

53.author: Hilali & Khan
53.text: And, indeed We gave Musa (Moses) the guidance, and We caused the Children of Israel to inherit the Scripture [i.e. the Taurat (Torah)],

53.author: Itani
53.text: We gave Moses guidance, and made the Children of Israel inherit the Scripture.

53.author: Maududi
53.text: We surely guided Moses and made the Children of Israel the heirs of the Book

53.author: Mubarakpuri
53.text: And, indeed We gave Musa the guidance, and We caused the Children of Israel to inherit the Scripture --

53.author: Pickthall
53.text: And We verily gave Moses the guidance, and We caused the Children of Israel to inherit the Scripture,

53.author: Qarai
53.text: Certainly We gave guidance to Moses and We gave the Children of Israel the Book as a legacy,

53.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
53.text: We gave Moses the Guidance and bequeathed upon the Children of Israel the Book

53.author: Saheeh International
53.text: And We had certainly given Moses guidance, and We caused the Children of Israel to inherit the Scripture

53.author: Sarwar
53.text: To Moses We had given guidance and to the children of Israel We had given the Book

53.author: Shakir
53.text: And certainly We gave Musa the guidance, and We made the children of Israel inherit the Book,

53.author: Wahiduddin Khan
53.text: We gave Moses Our guidance, and made the Children of Israel the inheritors of the Book --

53.author: Yusuf Ali
53.text: We did aforetime give Moses the (Book of) Guidance, and We gave the book in inheritance to the Children of Israel,-

53.author: Transliteration
53.text: Walaqad atayna moosa alhuda waawrathna banee israeela alkitaba

54.author: Ahmed Ali
54.text: A guidance and reminder for men of wisdom.

54.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
54.text: A guidance and an advice for the people of intellect.

54.author: Arberry
54.text: for a guidance and for a reminder to men possessed of minds.

54.author: Daryabadi
54.text: A guidance and an admonition Unto the men of understanding,

54.author: Hilali & Khan
54.text: A guide and a reminder for men of understanding.

54.author: Itani
54.text: A guide and a reminder for those endowed with reason.

54.author: Maududi
54.text: which was a guidance and good counsel to people endowed with understanding and wisdom.

54.author: Mubarakpuri
54.text: A guide and a reminder for men of understanding.

54.author: Pickthall
54.text: A guide and a reminder for men of understanding.

54.author: Qarai
54.text: as guidance and admonition for those who possess intellect.

54.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
54.text: for guidance and a reminder for those of understanding.

54.author: Saheeh International
54.text: As guidance and a reminder for those of understanding.

54.author: Sarwar
54.text: as their inheritance and as a guide and a reminder to the people of understanding.

54.author: Shakir
54.text: A guidance and a reminder to the men of understanding.

54.author: Wahiduddin Khan
54.text: a guide and an admonition to men of understanding.

54.author: Yusuf Ali
54.text: A Guide and a Message to men of Understanding.

55.author: Ahmed Ali
55.text: So persevere; the promise of God is true; and seek forgiveness for your sins, and chant the praises of your Lord evening and morning.

55.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
55.text: Therefore be patient (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), undoubtedly Allah’s promise is true, and seek forgiveness for the sins of your own people, and praising your Lord, proclaim His Purity morning and evening.

55.author: Arberry
55.text: So be thou patient; surely God's promise is true. And ask forgiveness for thy sin, and proclaim the praise of thy Lord at evening and dawn.

55.author: Daryabadi
55.text: Wherefore persevere thou. Verily the Promise of Allah is true and ask forgiveness for thy fault, and hallow the praise of thy Lord in the evening and in the morning.

55.author: Hilali & Khan
55.text: So be patient (O Muhammad SAW). Verily, the Promise of Allah is true, and ask forgiveness for your fault, and glorify the praises of your Lord in the Ashi (i.e. the time period after the midnoon till sunset) and in the Ibkar (i.e. the time period from early morning or sunrise till before midnoon) [it is said that, that means the five compulsory congregational Salat (prayers) or the 'Asr and Fajr prayers].

55.author: Itani
55.text: So be patient. The promise of God is true. And ask forgiveness for your sin, and proclaim the praise of your Lord evening and morning.

55.author: Maududi
55.text: Be steadfast, then, (O Prophet), Allah's promise is true. Seek forgiveness for your shortcomings, and celebrate the praise of your Lord, evening and morning.

55.author: Mubarakpuri
55.text: So be patient. Verily, the promise of Allah is true, and ask forgiveness for your fault, and glorify the praises of your Lord in the `Ashi and in the Ibkar.

55.author: Pickthall
55.text: Then have patience (O Muhammad). Lo! the promise of Allah is true. And ask forgiveness of thy sin, and hymn the praise of thy Lord at fall of night and in the early hours.

55.author: Qarai
55.text: So be patient! Allah’s promise is indeed true. Plead [Allah] for forgiveness of your sin, and celebrate the praise of your Lord morning and evening.

55.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
55.text: Therefore have patience; the promise of Allah is true, and ask forgiveness of your sins, and exalt with the praise of your Lord in the evening and at dawn.

55.author: Saheeh International
55.text: So be patient, [O Muhammad]. Indeed, the promise of Allah is truth. And ask forgiveness for your sin and exalt [Allah] with praise of your Lord in the evening and the morning.

55.author: Sarwar
55.text: (Muhammad), exercise patience. The promise of God is true. Seek forgiveness for your sins and glorify your Lord with His praise in the evenings and in the early mornings.

55.author: Shakir
55.text: Therefore be patient; surely the promise of Allah is true; and ask protection for your fault and sing the praise of your Lord in the evening and the morning.

55.author: Wahiduddin Khan
55.text: So be patient, for what God has promised is sure to come. Ask forgiveness for your sins; praise your Lord morning and evening.

55.author: Yusuf Ali
55.text: Patiently, then, persevere: for the Promise of Allah is true: and ask forgiveness for thy fault, and celebrate the Praises of thy Lord in the evening and in the morning.

55.author: Transliteration
55.text: Faisbir inna waAAda Allahi haqqun waistaghfir lithanbika wasabbih bihamdi rabbika bialAAashiyyi waalibkari

56.author: Ahmed Ali
56.text: Verily those who argue in the matter of God's revelations, without authority having reached them, have nothing but pride in their hearts, and they will not achieve their end. So take refuge in God: Surely He is all-hearing and all-seeing.

56.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
56.text: Those who dispute concerning the signs of Allah without any proof having come to them – in their hearts is nothing but a craving for greatness which they shall never achieve; therefore seek the refuge of Allah; indeed He only is the All Hearing, the All Seeing.

56.author: Arberry
56.text: Those who dispute concerning the signs of God, without any authority come to them, in their breasts is only pride, that they shall never attain. So seek thou refuge in God; surely He is the All-hearing, the All-seeing.

56.author: Daryabadi
56.text: Verily those who wrangle concerning the revelations of Allah without an authority having come Unto them there is aught in their breasts save greatness Which they shall not reach. Seek refuge thou then in Allah verily He! He is the Hearer, the Beholder.

56.author: Hilali & Khan
56.text: Verily, those who dispute about the Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) of Allah, without any authority having come to them, there is nothing else in their breasts except pride [to accept you (Muhammad SAW) as a Messenger of Allah and to obey you]. They will never have it (i.e. Prophethood which Allah has bestowed upon you). So seek refuge in Allah (O Muhammad SAW from the arrogants). Verily, it is He Who is the All-Hearer, the All-Seer.

56.author: Itani
56.text: Those who dispute regarding God’s revelations without any authority having come to them—there is nothing in their hearts but the feeling of greatness, which they will never attain. So seek refuge in God; for He is the All-Hearing, the All-Seeing.

56.author: Maududi
56.text: Verily those who dispute regarding the Signs of Allah without any evidence that might have come to them, nothing but vain pride fills their hearts. Yet they shall never be able to satisfy the pride with which they are puffed up. So seek refuge with Allah. Verily He is All-Hearing, All-Seeing.

56.author: Mubarakpuri
56.text: Verily, those who dispute about the Ayat of Allah, without any authority having come to them, there is nothing else in their breasts except pride. They will never have it. So seek refuge in Allah. Verily, it is He Who is the All-Hearer, the All-Seer.

56.author: Pickthall
56.text: Lo! those who wrangle concerning the revelations of Allah without a warrant having come unto them, there is naught else in their breasts save pride which they will never attain. So take thou refuge in Allah. Lo! He, only He, is the Hearer, the Seer.

56.author: Qarai
56.text: Indeed those who dispute the signs of Allah without any authority that may have come to them—there is [an ambition for] greatness in their breasts, which they will never attain. So seek the protection of Allah; indeed, He is the All-hearing, the All-seeing.

56.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
56.text: (As for) those who dispute the verses of Allah without authority having been given to them, there is nothing in their chests but pride; that, they shall never attain. Therefore seek refuge in Allah, surely, He is the Hearer, the Seer.

56.author: Saheeh International
56.text: Indeed, those who dispute concerning the signs of Allah without [any] authority having come to them - there is not within their breasts except pride, [the extent of] which they cannot reach. So seek refuge in Allah. Indeed, it is He who is the Hearing, the Seeing.

56.author: Sarwar
56.text: Those who dispute the revelations of God without having received any authority do so because of their arrogance, but their arrogance cannot bring them any success. Seek protection from God for He is All-hearing and All-aware.

56.author: Shakir
56.text: Surely (as for) those who dispute about the communications of Allah without any authority that has come to them, there is naught in their breasts but (a desire) to become great which they shall never attain to; Therefore seek refuge in Allah, surely He is the Hearing, the Seeing.

56.author: Wahiduddin Khan
56.text: As for those who, with no authority to do so, dispute God's messages, there is nothing in their hearts but a feeling of greatness which they will never attain. Seek refuge in God, for He is the All Hearing, the All Seeing.

56.author: Yusuf Ali
56.text: Those who dispute about the signs of Allah without any authority bestowed on them,- there is nothing in their breasts but (the quest of) greatness, which they shall never attain to: seek refuge, then, in Allah: It is He Who hears and sees (all things).

56.author: Transliteration
56.text: Inna allatheena yujadiloona fee ayati Allahi bighayri sultanin atahum in fee sudoorihim illa kibrun ma hum bibaligheehi faistaAAith biAllahi innahu huwa alssameeAAu albaseeru

57.author: Ahmed Ali
57.text: The creation of the heavens and the earth is indeed of greater magnitude than the creation of mankind; but most men do not understand.

57.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
57.text: Certainly the creation of the heavens and the earth is far greater than the creation of men, but most people do not know.

57.author: Arberry
57.text: Certainly the creation of the heavens and earth is greater than the creation of men; but most men know it not.

57.author: Daryabadi
57.text: The creation of the heavens and the earth is indeed greater than the creation of mankind; but most of man kind know not.

57.author: Hilali & Khan
57.text: The creation of the heavens and the earth is indeed greater than the creation of mankind, yet most of mankind know not.

57.author: Itani
57.text: Certainly the creation of the heavens and the earth is greater than the creation of humanity, but most people do not know.

57.author: Maududi
57.text: Surely the creation of the heavens and the earth is a greater act than the creation of human beings. But most people do not know.

57.author: Mubarakpuri
57.text: The creation of the heavens and the earth is indeed greater than the creation of mankind; yet, most of the people do not know.

57.author: Pickthall
57.text: Assuredly the creation of the heavens and the earth is greater than the creation of mankind; but most of mankind know not.

57.author: Qarai
57.text: The creation of the heavens and the earth is surely more prodigious than the creation of mankind, but most people do not know.

57.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
57.text: Indeed, the creation of heavens and earth is greater than the creation of mankind, yet most people do not know.

57.author: Saheeh International
57.text: The creation of the heavens and earth is greater than the creation of mankind, but most of the people do not know.

57.author: Sarwar
57.text: The creation of the heavens and the earth is certainly greater than the creation of mankind, but most people do not know.

57.author: Shakir
57.text: Certainly the creation of the heavens and the earth is greater than the creation of the men, but most people do not know

57.author: Wahiduddin Khan
57.text: Certainly, the creation of the heavens and the earth is greater than the creation of mankind; but most people do not know this.

57.author: Yusuf Ali
57.text: Assuredly the creation of the heavens and the earth is a greater (matter) than the creation of men: Yet most men understand not.

57.author: Transliteration
57.text: Lakhalqu alssamawati waalardi akbaru min khalqi alnnasi walakinna akthara alnnasi la yaAAlamoona

58.author: Ahmed Ali
58.text: The blind and the seeing are surely not alike, nor those who believe and act rightly and those who do evil. Little do you reflect!

58.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
58.text: And the blind and the sighted are not equal – and neither are the believers who perform good deeds and the wicked equal; how very little do you ponder!

58.author: Arberry
58.text: Not equal are the blind and the seeing man, those who believe and do deeds of righteousness, and the wrong-doer. Little do you reflect.

58.author: Daryabadi
58.text: Not equal are the blind and the seeing; nor those who believed and Work righteous works and the evil-doer. Little are ye admonished!

58.author: Hilali & Khan
58.text: And not equal are the blind and those who see, nor are (equal) those who believe (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism), and do righteous good deeds, and those who do evil. Little do you remember!

58.author: Itani
58.text: Not equal are the blind and the seeing. Nor are those who believe and work righteousness equal to the sinners. How little you reflect.

58.author: Maududi
58.text: Never can the blind and the seeing be equal; nor those that believe and act righteously and those that do evil. Little do you understand.

58.author: Mubarakpuri
58.text: And not equal are the blind and those who see; nor those who believe, and do righteous good deeds, and those who do evil. Little do you remember!

58.author: Pickthall
58.text: And the blind man and the seer are not equal, neither are those who believe and do good works (equal with) the evil-doer. Little do ye reflect!

58.author: Qarai
58.text: The blind one and the seer are not equal, neither are the evildoers and those who have faith and do righteous deeds. Little is the admonition that you take!

58.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
58.text: The blind and the seeing are not equal, nor are those who believe and do good works, and the wrongdoer yet you seldom reflect.

58.author: Saheeh International
58.text: And not equal are the blind and the seeing, nor are those who believe and do righteous deeds and the evildoer. Little do you remember.

58.author: Sarwar
58.text: Just as the blind and the seeing are not equal, so are the righteously striving believers and the sinners are not equal. How little to this you pay attention.

58.author: Shakir
58.text: And the blind and the seeing are not alike, nor those who believe and do good and the evil-doer; little is it that you are mindful.

58.author: Wahiduddin Khan
58.text: The blind and the sighted are not equal, just as those who believe and do good works and those who do evil are not equal: how seldom you reflect!

58.author: Yusuf Ali
58.text: Not equal are the blind and those who (clearly) see: Nor are (equal) those who believe and work deeds of righteousness, and those who do evil. Little do ye learn by admonition!

58.author: Transliteration
58.text: Wama yastawee alaAAma waalbaseeru waallatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati wala almuseeo qaleelan ma tatathakkaroona

59.author: Ahmed Ali
59.text: The Hour will certainly come; there is no mystery about it; but most men do not believe.

59.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
59.text: Indeed the Last Day will surely come, there is no doubt in it – but most people do not accept faith.

59.author: Arberry
59.text: The Hour is coming, no doubt of it, but most men do not believe.

59.author: Daryabadi
59.text: Verily the Hour is coming: there is no doubt thereof; yet most of mankind believe not.

59.author: Hilali & Khan
59.text: Verily, the Hour (Day of Judgement) is surely coming, therein is no doubt, yet most men believe not.

59.author: Itani
59.text: Indeed, the Hour is coming; there is no doubt about it; but most people do not believe.

59.author: Maududi
59.text: The Hour will indeed come; there is no doubt about that. Yet most people do not believe.

59.author: Mubarakpuri
59.text: Verily, the Hour is surely coming, there is no doubt about it, yet most men believe not.

59.author: Pickthall
59.text: Lo! the Hour is surely coming, there is no doubt thereof; yet most of mankind believe not.

59.author: Qarai
59.text: Indeed the Hour is bound to come; there is no doubt in it. But most people do not believe.

59.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
59.text: There is no doubt that the Hour is coming, yet most people do not believe.

59.author: Saheeh International
59.text: Indeed, the Hour is coming - no doubt about it - but most of the people do not believe.

59.author: Sarwar
59.text: The Hour of Doom will inevitably come, but most people do not have faith.

59.author: Shakir
59.text: Most surely the hour is coming, there is no doubt therein, but most people do not believe.

59.author: Wahiduddin Khan
59.text: The Final Hour is sure to come, without doubt, but most people do not believe.

59.author: Yusuf Ali
59.text: The Hour will certainly come: Therein is no doubt: Yet most men believe not.

59.author: Transliteration
59.text: Inna alssaAAata laatiyatun la rayba feeha walakinna akthara alnnasi la yuminoona

60.author: Ahmed Ali
60.text: Your Lord has said: "Call to Me that I may answer your call. Surely those who disdain worshipping Me will enter Hell, disgraced."

60.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
60.text: And your Lord proclaimed, “Pray to Me, I will accept; indeed those who stay conceited towards worshipping Me, will enter hell in disgrace."

60.author: Arberry
60.text: Your Lord has said, 'Call upon Me and I will answer you. Surely those who wax too proud to do Me service shall enter Gehenna utterly abject.'

60.author: Daryabadi
60.text: And your Lord hath said: call Unto Me, and I shall answer your prayer. Verily those who are stiff-necked against My worship, anon they will enter Hell abject.

60.author: Hilali & Khan
60.text: And your Lord said: "Invoke Me, [i.e. believe in My Oneness (Islamic Monotheism)] (and ask Me for anything) I will respond to your (invocation). Verily! Those who scorn My worship [i.e. do not invoke Me, and do not believe in My Oneness, (Islamic Monotheism)] they will surely enter Hell in humiliation!"

60.author: Itani
60.text: Your Lord has said, “Pray to Me, and I will respond to you. But those who are too proud to worship Me will enter Hell forcibly.”

60.author: Maududi
60.text: Your Lord said: “Pray to Me, and I will accept your prayers. Surely those who wax too proud to worship Me shall enter Hell, utterly abased.”

60.author: Mubarakpuri
60.text: And your Lord said: "Call upon Me, I will answer you. Verily, those who scorn My worship they will surely enter Hell in humiliation!"

60.author: Pickthall
60.text: And your Lord hath said: Pray unto Me and I will hear your prayer. Lo! those who scorn My service, they will enter hell, disgraced.

60.author: Qarai
60.text: Your Lord has said, ‘Call Me, and I will hear you!’ Indeed those who are disdainful of My worship will enter hell in utter humiliation.

60.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
60.text: Your Lord has said: 'Call on Me and I will answer you. Those who are too proud to worship Me shall enter Gehenna utterly abject'

60.author: Saheeh International
60.text: And your Lord says, "Call upon Me; I will respond to you." Indeed, those who disdain My worship will enter Hell [rendered] contemptible.

60.author: Sarwar
60.text: Your Lord has said, "Pray to Me for I shall answer you prayers. Those who are too proud to worship Me will soon go to hell in disgrace".

60.author: Shakir
60.text: And your Lord says: Call upon Me, I will answer you; surely those who are too proud for My service shall soon enter hell abased.

60.author: Wahiduddin Khan
60.text: Your Lord has said, "Call on Me, and I will answer your prayers." But those who are too arrogant to worship Me will certainly enter Hell, in disgrace.

60.author: Yusuf Ali
60.text: And your Lord says: "Call on Me; I will answer your (Prayer): but those who are too arrogant to serve Me will surely find themselves in Hell - in humiliation!"

60.author: Transliteration
60.text: Waqala rabbukumu odAAoonee astajib lakum inna allatheena yastakbiroona AAan AAibadatee sayadkhuloona jahannama dakhireena

61.author: Ahmed Ali
61.text: It is God who made the night for you to rest, the day to make things visible. Indeed God is gracious to men, but most men are not grateful.

61.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
61.text: It is Allah Who created night for you so that you may gain rest in it, and the day giving sight; indeed Allah is Most Munificent towards mankind, but most people do not give thanks.

61.author: Arberry
61.text: It is God who made for you the night, to repose in it, and the day, to see. Surely God is bountiful to men, but most men are not thankful.

61.author: Daryabadi
61.text: Allah it is who hath appointed for you the night that ye may repose therein, and day enlightening. Verily Allah is the Lord of Grace for mankind; bur most of mankind return not thanks.

61.author: Hilali & Khan
61.text: Allah, it is He Who has made the night for you that you may rest therein and the day for you to see. Truly, Allah is full of Bounty to mankind, yet most of mankind give no thanks.

61.author: Itani
61.text: It is God Who made the night for you, that you may rest therein; and the day allowing sight. God is gracious towards the people, but most people do not give thanks.

61.author: Maududi
61.text: Allah it is Who made the night so that you may seek repose in it, and made the day radiant. Surely Allah is Most Bounteous to people; but most people do not give thanks.

61.author: Mubarakpuri
61.text: Allah, it is He Who has made the night for you that you may rest therein and the day for you to see. Truly, Allah is full of bounty to mankind; yet, most of mankind give no thanks.

61.author: Pickthall
61.text: Allah it is Who hath appointed for you night that ye may rest therein, and day for seeing. Lo! Allah is a Lord of bounty for mankind, yet most of mankind give not thanks.

61.author: Qarai
61.text: It is Allah who made the night for you, that you may rest in it, and the day to provide visibility. Indeed Allah is gracious to mankind, but most people do not give thanks.

61.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
61.text: It is Allah who made for you the night in which to rest and the day to see. Allah is bountiful to the people, yet most people do not give thanks.

61.author: Saheeh International
61.text: It is Allah who made for you the night that you may rest therein and the day giving sight. Indeed, Allah is full of bounty to the people, but most of the people are not grateful.

61.author: Sarwar
61.text: It is God Who has made the night for you to rest and the day for you to see. God is Benevolent to the human being, but most people do not give Him due thanks.

61.author: Shakir
61.text: Allah is He Who made for you the night that you may rest therein and the day to see; most surely Allah is Gracious to men, but most men do not give thanks.

61.author: Wahiduddin Khan
61.text: It is God who has given you the night in which to rest and the day in which to see. God is truly bountiful to people, but most people do not give thanks.

61.author: Yusuf Ali
61.text: It is Allah Who has made the Night for you, that ye may rest therein, and the days as that which helps (you) to see. Verily Allah is full of Grace and Bounty to men: yet most men give no thanks.

61.author: Transliteration
61.text: Allahu allathee jaAAala lakumu allayla litaskunoo feehi waalnnahara mubsiran inna Allaha lathoo fadlin AAala alnnasi walakinna akthara alnnasi la yashkuroona

62.author: Ahmed Ali
62.text: He is God your Lord, creator of everything. There is no god but He. How then do you turn away (from Him)?

62.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
62.text: Such is Allah, your Lord, the Creator of all things; there is no God except Him; so where are you reverting?

62.author: Arberry
62.text: That then is God, your Lord, the Creator of everything; there is no god but He. How then are you perverted?

62.author: Daryabadi
62.text: Such is Allah. your Lord, the Creator of everything there is no God but He. Wither then are ye straying away?

62.author: Hilali & Khan
62.text: That is Allah, your Lord, the Creator of all things, La ilaha illa Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He), where then you are turning away (from Allah, by worshipping others instead of Him)!

62.author: Itani
62.text: Such is God, your Lord, Creator of all things. There is no god except Him; so how could you turn away?

62.author: Maududi
62.text: Allah (Who bestowed all these favours upon you) is your Lord, the Creator of everything. There is no god but He. Whence are you, then, being led astray?

62.author: Mubarakpuri
62.text: That is Allah, your Lord, the Creator of all things, La ilaha ila Huwa. How then are you turning away

62.author: Pickthall
62.text: Such is Allah, your Lord, the Creator of all things, There is no Allah save Him. How then are ye perverted?

62.author: Qarai
62.text: That is Allah, your Lord, the creator of all things, there is no god except Him. Then where do you stray?

62.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
62.text: Such is Allah, your Lord, the Creator of all things. There is no god except He. How then can you turn away from Him?

62.author: Saheeh International
62.text: That is Allah, your Lord, Creator of all things; there is no deity except Him, so how are you deluded?

62.author: Sarwar
62.text: It is God, your Lord, Who has created all things. He is the only Lord. Why then do you turn away from His worship to the worshipping of idols?

62.author: Shakir
62.text: That is Allah, your Lord, the Creator of everything; there is no Allah but He; whence are you then turned away?

62.author: Wahiduddin Khan
62.text: Such is God, your Lord, the Creator of all things. There is no god but He. How then are you being turned away [from Him]?

62.author: Yusuf Ali
62.text: Such is Allah, your Lord, the Creator of all things, there is no god but He: Then how ye are deluded away from the Truth!

62.author: Transliteration
62.text: Thalikumu Allahu rabbukum khaliqu kulli shayin la ilaha illa huwa faanna tufakoona

63.author: Ahmed Ali
63.text: Only they are turned away thus who deny the signs of God.

63.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
63.text: This is how those who deny the signs of Allah go reverting.

63.author: Arberry
63.text: Even so perverted are they who deny the signs of God.

63.author: Daryabadi
63.text: In this wise have those strayed who the signs of Allah were away want to gainsay.

63.author: Hilali & Khan
63.text: Thus were turned away those who used to deny the Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) of Allah.

63.author: Itani
63.text: Thus are turned away those who dispute the signs of God.

63.author: Maududi
63.text: Thus it is only those who had denied Allah's Signs that were led astray.

63.author: Mubarakpuri
63.text: Thus were turned away those who used to deny the Ayat of Allah.

63.author: Pickthall
63.text: Thus are they perverted who deny the revelations of Allah.

63.author: Qarai
63.text: Those who were used to impugning the signs of Allah are thus made to go astray.

63.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
63.text: As such those who disbelieve the signs of Allah turn away.

63.author: Saheeh International
63.text: Thus were those [before you] deluded who were rejecting the signs of Allah.

63.author: Sarwar
63.text: Thus are indeed those who have rejected the revelations of God.

63.author: Shakir
63.text: Thus were turned away those who denied the communications of Allah.

63.author: Wahiduddin Khan
63.text: Thus, indeed, those who deny the signs of God turn away from Him.

63.author: Yusuf Ali
63.text: Thus are deluded those who are wont to reject the Signs of Allah.

63.author: Transliteration
63.text: Kathalika yufaku allatheena kanoo biayati Allahi yajhadoona

64.author: Ahmed Ali
64.text: It is God who made the earth a dwelling for you, and the sky a vaulted roof, who fashioned you and gave you excellent form and provided you with clean and wholesome things. He is God, your Lord. So blessed be God, the Lord of all the worlds.

64.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
64.text: It is Allah Who made for you the earth your resting place and the sky a canopy, and moulded you so gave you the best shape, and gave you pure things for sustenance; such is Allah, your Lord; so Most Auspicious is Allah, the Lord Of The Creation.

64.author: Arberry
64.text: It is God who made for you the earth a fixed place and heaven for an edifice; And He shaped you, and shaped you well, and provided you with the good things. That then is God, your Lord, so blessed be God, the Lord of all Being.

64.author: Daryabadi
64.text: Allah it is Who hath appointed for you the earth for a resting-place and the heaven for a structure, and fashioned you and fashioned you well, and provided you with goodly things. Such is Allah, your Lord. So blessed be Allah, the Lord of the worlds!

64.author: Hilali & Khan
64.text: Allah, it is He Who has made for you the earth as a dwelling place and the sky as a canopy, and has given you shape and made your shapes good (looking) and has provided you with good things. That is Allah, your Lord, then blessed be Allah, the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists).

64.author: Itani
64.text: It is God who made the earth a habitat for you, and the sky a structure. And He designed you, and designed you well; and He provided you with the good things. Such is God, your Lord; so Blessed is God, Lord of the Worlds.

64.author: Maududi
64.text: Allah it is Who made the earth a dwelling place for you and made the sky a canopy, Who shaped you -- and shaped you exceedingly well -- and gave you good things as sustenance. That is Allah, your Lord; blessed be Allah, the Lord of the Universe.

64.author: Mubarakpuri
64.text: Allah, it is He Who has made for you the earth as a dwelling place and the sky as a canopy, and has given you shape and made your shapes good and pure (looking) and has provided you with good things. That is Allah, your Lord, so Blessed be Allah, the Lord of all that exists.

64.author: Pickthall
64.text: Allah it is Who appointed for you the earth for a dwelling-place and the sky for a canopy, and fashioned you and perfected your shapes, and hath provided you with good things. Such is Allah, your Lord. Then blessed be Allah, the Lord of the Worlds!

64.author: Qarai
64.text: It is Allah who made for you the earth an abode and the sky a canopy. He formed you and perfected your forms, and provided you with all the good things. That is Allah, your Lord! Blessed is Allah, Lord of all the worlds!

64.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
64.text: It is Allah who has given you the earth as a fixed place, and the heaven for a canopy. He has shaped you and made you fine images, and provided you with goodness. Such is Allah, your Lord. Blessed be Allah, Lord of all the Worlds.

64.author: Saheeh International
64.text: It is Allah who made for you the earth a place of settlement and the sky a ceiling and formed you and perfected your forms and provided you with good things. That is Allah, your Lord; then blessed is Allah, Lord of the worlds.

64.author: Sarwar
64.text: It is God who has created the earth as a place for you to live and the sky as a dome above you. He has shaped you in the best form and has provided you with pure sustenance. This is God, your Lord. Blessed is God, the Lord of the Universe.

64.author: Shakir
64.text: Allah is He Who made the earth a resting-place for you and the heaven a canopy, and He formed you, then made goodly your forms, and He provided you with goodly things; that is Allah, your Lord; blessed then is Allah, the Lord of the worlds.

64.author: Wahiduddin Khan
64.text: It is God who has given you the earth for a resting place and the heavens for a canopy. He shaped you, formed you well, and provided you with good things. Such is God, your Lord, so glory be to Him, the Lord of the Universe.

64.author: Yusuf Ali
64.text: It is Allah Who has made for you the earth as a resting place, and the sky as a canopy, and has given you shape- and made your shapes beautiful,- and has provided for you Sustenance, of things pure and good;- such is Allah your Lord. So Glory to Allah, the Lord of the Worlds!

64.author: Transliteration
64.text: Allahu allathee jaAAala lakumu alarda qararan waalssamaa binaan wasawwarakum faahsana suwarakum warazaqakum mina alttayyibati thalikumu Allahu rabbukum fatabaraka Allahu rabbu alAAalameena

65.author: Ahmed Ali
65.text: He is the living. There is no god but He. Therefore pray to Him with obedience all-exclusive. Praise be to God, the Lord of all the worlds.

65.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
65.text: It is He, the Alive – there is no God except Him; therefore worship Him as His sincere bondmen; all praise is to Allah, the Lord Of The Creation.

65.author: Arberry
65.text: He is the Living One; there is no god but He. So call upon Him, making your religion His sincerely. Praise belongs to God, the Lord of all Being.

65.author: Daryabadi
65.text: He is the Living: there is no god but He so call upon Him, making religion pure for Him. All praise Unto Allah, the Lord of the worlds!

65.author: Hilali & Khan
65.text: He is the Ever Living, La ilaha illa Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He), so invoke Him making your worship pure for Him Alone (by worshipping Him Alone, and none else, and by doing righteous deeds sincerely for Allah's sake only, and not to show off, and not to set up rivals with Him in worship). All the praises and thanks be to Allah, the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists).

65.author: Itani
65.text: He is the Living One. There is no god except He. So pray to Him, devoting your religion to Him. Praise be to God, the Lord of the Worlds.

65.author: Maududi
65.text: He is the Ever-Living: there is no god but He. So call upon Him, consecrating to Him all your devotion. All praise and thanks be to Allah, the Lord of the whole Universe.

65.author: Mubarakpuri
65.text: He is the Ever Living, La ilaha illa Huwa; so invoke Him making the religion for Him Alone. All the praises and thanks be to Allah, the Lord of all that exits.

65.author: Pickthall
65.text: He is the Living One. There is no Allah save Him. So pray unto Him, making religion pure for Him (only). Praise be to Allah, the Lord of the Worlds!

65.author: Qarai
65.text: He is the Living One, there is no god except Him. So supplicate Him, putting exclusive faith in Him. All praise belongs to Allah, Lord of all the worlds.

65.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
65.text: He is the Living; there is no god except He. Supplicate to Him and make the religion sincerely His. Praise belongs to Allah, Lord of the Worlds!

65.author: Saheeh International
65.text: He is the Ever-Living; there is no deity except Him, so call upon Him, [being] sincere to Him in religion. [All] praise is [due] to Allah, Lord of the worlds.

65.author: Sarwar
65.text: He is the Everlasting and the only Lord. So worship Him and be devoted to His religion. It is only God, the Lord of the Universe who deserves all praise.

65.author: Shakir
65.text: He is the Living, there is no god but He, therefore call on Him, being sincere to Him in obedience; (all) praise is due to Allah, the Lord of the worlds.

65.author: Wahiduddin Khan
65.text: He is the Living One. There is no deity save Him. So pray to Him, making religion pure for Him [only]. Praise be to God, the Lord of the Universe!

65.author: Yusuf Ali
65.text: He is the Living (One): There is no god but He: Call upon Him, giving Him sincere devotion. Praise be to Allah, Lord of the Worlds!

65.author: Transliteration
65.text: Huwa alhayyu la ilaha illa huwa faodAAoohu mukhliseena lahu alddeena alhamdu lillahi rabbi alAAalameena

66.author: Ahmed Ali
66.text: Say: "I am forbidden to worship those you call apart from God, since clear signs have come to me from my Lord, and I am commanded to submit to the Lord of all the worlds."

66.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
66.text: Proclaim (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “I have been forbidden to worship those whom you worship besides Allah whilst clear proofs have come to me from my Lord; and I have been commanded to submit to the Lord Of The Creation.”

66.author: Arberry
66.text: Say: 'I am forbidden to serve those you call on apart from God once the clear signs came to me from my Lord; and I am commanded to surrender to the Lord of All Being.'

66.author: Daryabadi
66.text: Say thou: verily I am forbidden that should worship these whom ye call upon beside Allah when evidences have come Unto me from my Lord, and I am commanded that I should submit to the Lord of the Worlds.

66.author: Hilali & Khan
66.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "I have been forbidden to worship those whom you worship besides Allah, since there have come to me evidences from my Lord, and I am commanded to submit (in Islam) to the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists).

66.author: Itani
66.text: Say, “I was prohibited from worshiping those you invoke besides God, now that clear revelations have come to me from my Lord; and I was commanded to submit to the Lord of the Worlds.”

66.author: Maududi
66.text: Say, (O Prophet): “I have been forbidden to worship those beside Allah whom you call upon. (How can I worship any beside Allah) when clear Signs have come to me from my Lord and I have been commanded to surrender to Allah, the Lord of the Universe?”

66.author: Mubarakpuri
66.text: Say: "I have been forbidden to worship those whom you worship besides Allah, since there have come to me evidences from my Lord; and I am commanded to submit to the Lord of all that exists.

66.author: Pickthall
66.text: Say (O Muhammad): I am forbidden to worship those unto whom ye cry beside Allah since there have come unto me clear proofs from my Lord, and I am commanded to surrender to the Lord of the Worlds.

66.author: Qarai
66.text: Say, ‘I have been forbidden to worship those whom you invoke besides Allah, as manifest proofs have come to me from my Lord, and I have been commanded to submit to the Lord of all the worlds.’

66.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
66.text: Say: 'I am forbidden to worship those whom you worship, other than Allah. Clear verses have come to me from my Lord and I am commanded to surrender to the Lord of the Worlds'

66.author: Saheeh International
66.text: Say, [O Muhammad], "Indeed, I have been forbidden to worship those you call upon besides Allah once the clear proofs have come to me from my Lord, and I have been commanded to submit to the Lord of the worlds."

66.author: Sarwar
66.text: (Muhammad), say, "I have been forbidden to worship whatever you worship besides God after receiving clear evidence from my Lord. I have been commanded to submit myself to the will of the Lord of the Universe".

66.author: Shakir
66.text: Say: I am forbidden to serve those whom you call upon besides Allah when clear arguments have come to me from my Lord, and I am commanded that I should submit to the Lord of the worlds.

66.author: Wahiduddin Khan
66.text: Say, "I have been forbidden to invoke those whom you invoke besides God -- seeing that clear signs have come to me from my Lord; and I have been commanded to submit to the Lord of the Universe."

66.author: Yusuf Ali
66.text: Say: "I have been forbidden to invoke those whom ye invoke besides Allah,- seeing that the Clear Signs have come to me from my Lord; and I have been commanded to bow (in Islam) to the Lord of the Worlds."

66.author: Transliteration
66.text: Qul innee nuheetu an aAAbuda allatheena tadAAoona min dooni Allahi lamma jaaniya albayyinatu min rabbee waomirtu an oslima lirabbi alAAalameena

67.author: Ahmed Ali
67.text: It is He who created you from dust, then a drop of semen, then the embryo; afterwards He brings you forth as a child; then you attain the age of manhood, and then reach old age. But some of you die before you reach the appointed term that you may haply understand.

67.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
67.text: It is He Who created you from clay, then from a drop of liquid, then from a clot of blood, and then brings you forth as a child, then keeps you alive for you to reach adulthood and then to become old; and some among you pass away earlier, and for you to reach an appointed term, and so that you may understand.

67.author: Arberry
67.text: It is He who created you of dust then of a sperm-drop, then of a blood-clot, then He delivers you as infants, then that you may come of age, then that you may be old men -- though some of you there are who die before it -- and that you may reach a stated term; haply you will understand.

67.author: Daryabadi
67.text: He it is Who created you of dust, and thereafter of a drop, and thereafter of a clot, and thereafter He bringeth you forth as an infant, and thereafter He ordaineth that ye attain your full strength and thereafter that ye become old men-though some of you die before-and that ye attain the appointed term, and that haply ye may reflect.

67.author: Hilali & Khan
67.text: He, it is Who has created you (Adam) from dust, then from a Nutfah [mixed semen drops of male and female discharge (i.e. Adam's offspring)] then from a clot (a piece of coagulated blood), then brings you forth as children, then (makes you grow) to reach the age of full strength, and afterwards to be old (men and women), though some among you die before, and that you reach an appointed term, in order that you may understand.

67.author: Itani
67.text: It is He who created you from dust, then from a seed, then from an embryo, then He brings you out as an infant, then He lets you reach your maturity, then you become elderly—although some of you die sooner—so that you may reach a predetermined age, so that you may understand.

67.author: Maududi
67.text: He it is Who created you from dust, then from a sperm-drop, then from a clot; then He brings you out as an infant, then causes you to grow into full maturity, and then causes you to grow further so that you may reach old age, while some of you He recalls earlier. All this is in order that you may reach an appointed term and that you may understand (the Truth).

67.author: Mubarakpuri
67.text: It is He, Who has created you from dust, then from a Nutfah then from a clot (a piece of coagulated blood), then brings you forth as an infant, then (makes you grow) to reach the age of full strength, and afterwards to be old -- though some among you die before -- and that you reach an appointed term in order that you may understand.

67.author: Pickthall
67.text: He it is Who created you from dust, then from a drop (of seed) then from a clot, then bringeth you forth as a child, then (ordaineth) that ye attain full strength and afterward that ye become old men - though some among you die before - and that ye reach an appointed term, that haply ye may understand.

67.author: Qarai
67.text: It is He who created you from dust, then from a drop of [seminal] fluid, then from a clinging mass, then He brings you forth as infants, then [He nurtures you] so that you may come of age, and then that you may become aged—though there are some of you who die earlier—and complete a specified term, and so that you may exercise your reason.

67.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
67.text: It is He who created you from dust, then from a (sperm) drop, and then a (blood) clot. He then brings you forth as an infant, then you reach your strength, after which you come of age though some of you die before it and that you reach an appointed term, in order that you understand.

67.author: Saheeh International
67.text: It is He who created you from dust, then from a sperm-drop, then from a clinging clot; then He brings you out as a child; then [He develops you] that you reach your [time of] maturity, then [further] that you become elders. And among you is he who is taken in death before [that], so that you reach a specified term; and perhaps you will use reason.

67.author: Sarwar
67.text: It is He Who created you from clay, turning it into a living germ, then into a clot of blood, and then brings you forth as a child. He then made you grow into manhood and become old. He causes some of you to live for the appointed time and some of you to die before so that perhaps you may have understanding.

67.author: Shakir
67.text: He it is Who created you from dust, then from a small lifegerm, then from a clot, then He brings you forth as a child, then that you may attain your maturity, then that you may be old-- and of you there are some who are caused to die before-- and that you may reach an appointed term, and that you may understand.

67.author: Wahiduddin Khan
67.text: It is He who created you from dust, then from a drop of fluid, then from a tiny, clinging form, then He brought you forth as infants, then He allowed you to reach maturity, then He let you grow old, though some of you die sooner, and reach your appointed term so that you may reflect.

67.author: Yusuf Ali
67.text: It is He Who has created you from dust then from a sperm-drop, then from a leech-like clot; then does he get you out (into the light) as a child: then lets you (grow and) reach your age of full strength; then lets you become old,- though of you there are some who die before;- and lets you reach a Term appointed; in order that ye may learn wisdom.

67.author: Transliteration
67.text: Huwa allathee khalaqakum min turabin thumma min nutfatin thumma min AAalaqatin thumma yukhrijukum tiflan thumma litablughoo ashuddakum thumma litakoonoo shuyookhan waminkum man yutawaffa min qablu walitablughoo ajalan musamman walaAAallakum taAAqiloona

68.author: Ahmed Ali
68.text: It is He who gives you life and death. When He creates a thing, He has only to say: "Be," and it is.

68.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
68.text: It is He Who gives life and death; so whenever He wills a thing, He only says to it “Be” – it thereupon happens!

68.author: Arberry
68.text: It is He who gives life, and makes to die; and when He decrees a thing, He but says to it 'Be,' and it is.

68.author: Daryabadi
68.text: He it is Who causeth life and death. Then whensoever He decreeth an affair He only saith Unto it: be, and it becometh.

68.author: Hilali & Khan
68.text: He it is Who gives life and causes death. And when He decides upon a thing He says to it only: "Be!" and it is.

68.author: Itani
68.text: It is He who gives life and death; and when He decides on a thing, He just says to it, “Be,” and it comes to be.

68.author: Maududi
68.text: He it is Who gives life and causes death. Whenever He decrees a thing, He only commands to it “Be”, and it is.

68.author: Mubarakpuri
68.text: It is He Who gives life and causes death. And when He decides upon a thing He says to it only: "Be!" - and it is.

68.author: Pickthall
68.text: He it is Who quickeneth and giveth death. When He ordaineth a thing, He saith unto it only: Be! and it is.

68.author: Qarai
68.text: It is He who gives life and brings death. When He decides on a matter, He just says to it, ‘Be!’ and it is.

68.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
68.text: It is He who gives life and makes to die, and when He decrees a thing, He says to it: 'Be' and it is!

68.author: Saheeh International
68.text: He it is who gives life and causes death; and when He decrees a matter, He but says to it, "Be," and it is.

68.author: Sarwar
68.text: It is He Who gives life and causes things to die. When He decides to do something, He only says, "Exist," and it comes into existence.

68.author: Shakir
68.text: He it is Who gives life and brings death, so when He decrees an affair, He only says to it: Be, and it is.

68.author: Wahiduddin Khan
68.text: It is He who gives life and death, and when He ordains a thing, He says only, "Be!" and it is.

68.author: Yusuf Ali
68.text: It is He Who gives Life and Death; and when He decides upon an affair, He says to it, "Be", and it is.

68.author: Transliteration
68.text: Huwa allathee yuhyee wayumeetu faitha qada amran fainnama yaqoolu lahu kun fayakoonu

69.author: Ahmed Ali
69.text: Have you not seen how those who dispute the signs of God are turned away?

69.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
69.text: Did you not see those who dispute concerning the signs of Allah? Where are they being diverted?

69.author: Arberry
69.text: Hast thou not regarded those who dispute concerning the signs of God, how they are turned about?

69.author: Daryabadi
69.text: Observest thou not those who wrangle concerning the revelations of Allah: whither are they turning away?

69.author: Hilali & Khan
69.text: See you not those who dispute about the Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) of Allah? How are they turning away (from the truth, i.e. Islamic Monotheism to the falsehood of polytheism)?

69.author: Itani
69.text: Have you not observed those who dispute regarding God's revelations, how they have deviated?

69.author: Maududi
69.text: Did you not see those who dispute concerning Allah's Signs? Whence are they, then, being turned astray?

69.author: Mubarakpuri
69.text: See you not those who dispute about the Ayat of Allah How are they turning away

69.author: Pickthall
69.text: Hast thou not seen those who wrangle concerning the revelations of Allah, how they are turned away? -

69.author: Qarai
69.text: Have you not regarded those who dispute the signs of Allah, where they are being led away [from Allah’s way]?

69.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
69.text: Do you not see how those who dispute about the verses of Allah how they are turned about?

69.author: Saheeh International
69.text: Do you not consider those who dispute concerning the signs of Allah - how are they averted?

69.author: Sarwar
69.text: Have you not seen how those who dispute the revelations of God, turn away from Truth to falsehood?

69.author: Shakir
69.text: Have you not seen those who dispute with respect to the communications of Allah: how are they turned away?

69.author: Wahiduddin Khan
69.text: Do you not see how those who dispute God's signs, are turned away from the right path --

69.author: Yusuf Ali
69.text: Seest thou not those that dispute concerning the Signs of Allah? How are they turned away (from Reality)?-

69.author: Transliteration
69.text: Alam tara ila allatheena yujadiloona fee ayati Allahi anna yusrafoona

70.author: Ahmed Ali
70.text: Those who deny the Book and what We have sent down with Our apostles, will soon come to know

70.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
70.text: Those who denied the Book and what We sent with Our Noble Messengers; so they will soon come to know. –

70.author: Arberry
70.text: Those who cry lies to the Book and that wherewith We sent Our Messengers -- soon they will know!

70.author: Daryabadi
70.text: Those who belie the Book and that message wherewith We sent Our apostles, presently they shall come to know.

70.author: Hilali & Khan
70.text: Those who deny the Book (this Quran), and that with which We sent Our Messengers (i.e. to worship none but Allah Alone sincerely, and to reject all false deities and to confess resurrection after the death for recompense) they will come to know (when they will be cast into the Fire of Hell).

70.author: Itani
70.text: Those who call the Book a lie, and what We sent Our messengers with—they will surely know.

70.author: Maududi
70.text: Those who gave the lie to this Book and all the Books which We had sent with Our Messengers shall soon come to know the Truth

70.author: Mubarakpuri
70.text: Those who deny the Book, and that with which We sent Our Messengers they will come to know.

70.author: Pickthall
70.text: Those who deny the Scripture and that wherewith We send Our messengers. But they will come to know,

70.author: Qarai
70.text: —Those who deny the Book and what we have sent with Our apostles. Soon they will know

70.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
70.text: Those who belie the Book and that with which We sent Our Messengers shall soon know.

70.author: Saheeh International
70.text: Those who deny the Book and that with which We sent Our messengers - they are going to know,

70.author: Sarwar
70.text: Those who rejected the Book and the message which was given to Our Messenger will soon know (the consequences of their evil deeds).

70.author: Shakir
70.text: Those who reject the Book and that with which We have sent Our Apostle; but they shall soon come to know,

70.author: Wahiduddin Khan
70.text: those who reject the Book and that with which We sent Our messengers shall soon know --

70.author: Yusuf Ali
70.text: Those who reject the Book and the (revelations) with which We sent our messengers: but soon shall they know,-

70.author: Transliteration
70.text: Allatheena kaththaboo bialkitabi wabima arsalna bihi rusulana fasawfa yaAAlamoona

71.author: Ahmed Ali
71.text: When, with (iron) collars and chains around their necks, they will be dragged

71.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
71.text: When around their necks will be shackles and chains; they will be dragged. –

71.author: Arberry
71.text: When the fetters and chains are on their necks, and they dragged

71.author: Daryabadi
71.text: When shackles will be on their necks and also chains; they will be dragged.

71.author: Hilali & Khan
71.text: When iron collars will be rounded over their necks, and the chains, they shall be dragged along.

71.author: Itani
71.text: When the yokes are around their necks, and they will be dragged by the chains.

71.author: Maududi
71.text: when fetters and chains shall be on their necks, and they shall be dragged into

71.author: Mubarakpuri
71.text: When iron collars will be rounded over their necks, and the chains, they shall be dragged along,

71.author: Pickthall
71.text: When carcans are about their necks and chains. They are dragged

71.author: Qarai
71.text: when they are dragged [with] iron collars and chains around their necks

71.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
71.text: When the fetters and chains are round their necks they shall be dragged

71.author: Saheeh International
71.text: When the shackles are around their necks and the chains; they will be dragged

71.author: Sarwar
71.text: when fetters will be placed around their necks and chains will drag them

71.author: Shakir
71.text: When the fetters and the chains shall be on their necks; they shall be dragged

71.author: Wahiduddin Khan
71.text: when, with iron collars and chains around their necks, they are dragged

71.author: Yusuf Ali
71.text: When the yokes (shall be) round their necks, and the chains; they shall be dragged along-

71.author: Transliteration
71.text: Ithi alaghlalu fee aAAnaqihim waalssalasilu yushaboona

72.author: Ahmed Ali
72.text: Through boiling water, and then burnt in the Fire.

72.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
72.text: In boiling water; they will then be ignited* in the fire. (Like fuel – see verse 2:24)

72.author: Arberry
72.text: into the boiling water, then into the Fire they are poured;

72.author: Daryabadi
72.text: Into the balling water; then into the Fire they will be stocked.

72.author: Hilali & Khan
72.text: In the boiling water, then they will be burned in the Fire.

72.author: Itani
72.text: Into the boiling water, then in the Fire they will be consumed.

72.author: Maududi
72.text: boiling water, and cast into the Fire.

72.author: Mubarakpuri
72.text: In the boiling water, then they will be burned in the Fire.

72.author: Pickthall
72.text: Through boiling waters; then they are thrust into the Fire.

72.author: Qarai
72.text: into scalding waters and then set aflame in the Fire.

72.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
72.text: into boiling water, then, into the Fire they shall be poured.

72.author: Saheeh International
72.text: In boiling water; then in the Fire they will be filled [with flame].

72.author: Sarwar
72.text: into boiling water and then they will be burned in the fire.

72.author: Shakir
72.text: Into boiling water, then in the fire shall they be burned;

72.author: Wahiduddin Khan
72.text: into the boiling water and then are thrown into the Fire,

72.author: Yusuf Ali
72.text: In the boiling fetid fluid: then in the Fire shall they be burned;

72.author: Transliteration
72.text: Fee alhameemi thumma fee alnnari yusjaroona

73.author: Ahmed Ali
73.text: They will then be asked: "Where are they you took as partners

73.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
73.text: It will then be said to them, “Where are the partners you used to appoint?” –

73.author: Arberry
73.text: then it is said to them, 'Where are those you associated, apart from God?'

73.author: Daryabadi
73.text: Then it will be said Unto them: where are those whom ye have been associating

73.author: Hilali & Khan
73.text: Then it will be said to them: "Where are (all) those whom you used to join in worship as partners

73.author: Itani
73.text: Then it will be said to them, “Where are those you used to deify?

73.author: Maududi
73.text: It will then be said to them: “Where are those whom you

73.author: Mubarakpuri
73.text: Then it will be said to them: "Where are (all) those whom you used to consider partners"

73.author: Pickthall
73.text: Then it is said unto them: Where are (all) that ye used to make partners (in the Sovereignty)

73.author: Qarai
73.text: Then they will be told, ‘Where are they whom you used to worship as deities

73.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
73.text: It will then be asked of them: 'Where are those whom you associated,

73.author: Saheeh International
73.text: Then it will be said to them, "Where is that which you used to associate [with Him in worship]

73.author: Sarwar
73.text: Then they will be asked, "Where are the idols which you worshipped besides God?"

73.author: Shakir
73.text: Then shall it be said to them: Where is that which you used to set up

73.author: Wahiduddin Khan
73.text: and then they will be asked, "Where are those whom you associated [with God]?"

73.author: Yusuf Ali
73.text: Then shall it be said to them: "Where are the (deities) to which ye gave part-worship-

73.author: Transliteration
73.text: Thumma qeela lahum ayna ma kuntum tushrikoona

74.author: Ahmed Ali
74.text: Apart from God?" They will answer: "They have left us in the lurch and fled. In fact it was nothing that we prayed to before." That is how God sends the unbelievers astray.

74.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
74.text: “As rivals to Allah?”; they will say, “We have lost them – in fact we never used to worship anything before!”; this is how Allah sends the disbelievers astray.

74.author: Arberry
74.text: They shall say, 'They have gone astray from us; nay, but it was nothing at all that we called upon aforetime.' Even so God leads astray the unbelievers.

74.author: Daryabadi
74.text: Beside Allah? They will say: they have failed us: aye! we have not been calling upon aught afore. Thus doth Allah lead the infidels astray.

74.author: Hilali & Khan
74.text: "Besides Allah" They will say: "They have vanished from us: Nay, we did not invoke (worship) anything before." Thus Allah leads astray the disbelievers.

74.author: Itani
74.text: Instead of God?” They will say, “They have abandoned us. In fact, we were praying to nothing before.” Thus God sends the disbelievers astray.

74.author: Maududi
74.text: associated with Allah in His Divinity?” They will say: “We have lost them; rather, we never used to call upon anyone before.” Thus will Allah cause them to stumble in error.

74.author: Mubarakpuri
74.text: "Besides Allah" They will say: "They have vanished from us. Nay, we did not call upon anything before." Thus Allah leads astray the disbelievers.

74.author: Pickthall
74.text: Beside Allah? They say: They have failed us; but we used not to pray to anything before. Thus doth Allah send astray the disbelievers (in His guidance).

74.author: Qarai
74.text: besides Allah?’ They will say, ‘They have forsaken us. No, we did not invoke anything before.’ That is how Allah leads the faithless astray.

74.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
74.text: other than Allah' They will reply: 'They have gone astray from us. Indeed what we used to call upon before is nothing' Accordingly, Allah leads the unbelievers astray.

74.author: Saheeh International
74.text: Other than Allah?" They will say, "They have departed from us; rather, we did not used to invoke previously anything." Thus does Allah put astray the disbelievers.

74.author: Sarwar
74.text: They will reply, "They have abandoned us. In fact, we had worshipped nothing" Thus does God cause the disbelievers to go astray.

74.author: Shakir
74.text: Besides Allah? They shall say: They are gone away from us, nay, we used not to call upon anything before. Thus does Allah confound the unbelievers.

74.author: Wahiduddin Khan
74.text: They will say, "They have been lost to us; nay, we did not invoke anything before [that had real existence]." Thus God leaves the deniers of the truth to stray;

74.author: Yusuf Ali
74.text: "In derogation of Allah?" They will reply: "They have left us in the lurch: Nay, we invoked not, of old, anything (that had real existence)." Thus does Allah leave the Unbelievers to stray.

74.author: Transliteration
74.text: Min dooni Allahi qaloo dalloo AAanna bal lam nakun nadAAoo min qablu shayan kathalika yudillu Allahu alkafireena

75.author: Ahmed Ali
75.text: "This is so because you went about exulting wrongfully in the land," (will they be told), "and you were insolent.

75.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
75.text: “This is the recompense of your being happy upon falsehood in the earth, and the recompense of your conceit.”

75.author: Arberry
75.text: 'That is because you rejoiced in the earth without right, and were exultant.

75.author: Daryabadi
75.text: That is because ye had been exulting in the earth without right, and because ye had been strutting.

75.author: Hilali & Khan
75.text: That was because you had been exulting in the earth without any right (by worshipping others instead of Allah and by committing crimes), and that you used to rejoice extremely (in your error).

75.author: Itani
75.text: That is because you used to rejoice on earth in other than the truth, and because you used to behave with vanity.

75.author: Maududi
75.text: (They will be told): “This is because while you were on earth you took delight in untruth and exulted in it.

75.author: Mubarakpuri
75.text: That was because you had been exulting in the earth without any right, and that you used to rejoice extremely.

75.author: Pickthall
75.text: (And it is said unto them): This is because ye exulted in the earth without right, and because ye were petulant.

75.author: Qarai
75.text: ‘That [punishment] is because you used to boast unduly on the earth and because you used to strut.

75.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
75.text: (And it will be said): 'That is because you rejoiced on earth in things of which you had no right, and were very happy.

75.author: Saheeh International
75.text: [The angels will say], "That was because you used to exult upon the earth without right and you used to behave insolently.

75.author: Sarwar
75.text: They will be told, "This (torment) is the result of your unreasonable happiness on the earth and of your propagation of falsehood.

75.author: Shakir
75.text: That is because you exulted in the land unjustly and because you behaved insolently.

75.author: Wahiduddin Khan
75.text: that is because you exulted in the land without justification and because you behaved insolently.

75.author: Yusuf Ali
75.text: "That was because ye were wont to rejoice on the earth in things other than the Truth, and that ye were wont to be insolent.

75.author: Transliteration
75.text: Thalikum bima kuntum tafrahoona fee alardi bighayri alhaqqi wabima kuntum tamrahoona

76.author: Ahmed Ali
76.text: So enter the gates of Hell to abide in it for ever." How evil the abode of the arrogant!

76.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
76.text: “Enter the gates of hell, to remain in it forever”; so what a wretched destination for the haughty!

76.author: Arberry
76.text: Enter the gates of Gehenna, to dwell therein forever.' How evil is the lodging of those that are proud!

76.author: Daryabadi
76.text: Enter ye the gates of Hell as abiders therein. Hapless is the abode of the stiff-necked.

76.author: Hilali & Khan
76.text: Enter the gates of Hell to abide therein, and (indeed) what an evil abode of the arrogant!

76.author: Itani
76.text: Enter the gates of Hell, to remain therein forever. What a terrible dwelling for the arrogant.

76.author: Maududi
76.text: Enter Hell now to abide in it. How woeful is the abode of those who wax proud!

76.author: Mubarakpuri
76.text: Enter the gates of Hell to abide therein, and (indeed) what an evil abode of the arrogant!

76.author: Pickthall
76.text: Enter ye the gates of hell, to dwell therein. Evil is the habitation of the scornful.

76.author: Qarai
76.text: Enter the gates of hell, to remain in it [forever].’ Evil is the [final] abode of the arrogant.

76.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
76.text: Enter the gates of Gehenna and live there for ever. Evil is the lodging of the proud.

76.author: Saheeh International
76.text: Enter the gates of Hell to abide eternally therein, and wretched is the residence of the arrogant."

76.author: Sarwar
76.text: Enter the gates of hell to live therein forever. How terrible is the dwelling of the arrogant ones.

76.author: Shakir
76.text: Enter the gates of hell to abide therein, evil then is the abode of the proud.

76.author: Wahiduddin Khan
76.text: Enter the gates of Hell to stay therein forever. The abode of the arrogant is evil.

76.author: Yusuf Ali
76.text: "Enter ye the gates of Hell, to dwell therein: and evil is (this) abode of the arrogant!"

76.author: Transliteration
76.text: Odkhuloo abwaba jahannama khalideena feeha fabisa mathwa almutakabbireena

77.author: Ahmed Ali
77.text: So you persevere with patience (in your mission). The promise of God is true. Whether We show you some of the punishment We have promised them, or gather you up in death, they have to come back to Us.

77.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
77.text: Therefore be patient (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), undoubtedly Allah’s promise is true; and whether We show some of what We promise them, or cause you to pass away before it – in any case they will return to Us.

77.author: Arberry
77.text: So be thou patient; surely God's promise is true. Whether We show thee a part of that We promise them, or We call thee unto Us, to Us they shall be returned.

77.author: Daryabadi
77.text: Wherefore persevere thou; verily the promise of Allah is true; then whether We let thee see a part of that wherewith We have promised them, or whether We cause thee to die, Unto us they all will be caused to retvtn.

77.author: Hilali & Khan
77.text: So be patient (O Muhammad SAW), verily, the Promise of Allah is true, and whether We show you (O Muhammad SAW in this world) some part of what We have promised them, or We cause you to die, then it is to Us they all shall be returned.

77.author: Itani
77.text: So be patient. The promise of God is true. Whether We show you some of what We have promised them, or take you to Us, to Us they will be returned.

77.author: Maududi
77.text: So be patient, (O Prophet). Surely Allah's promise is true. Whether We show them a part of the woeful consequences against which We warn them (while you are still in their midst) or We recall you (from this world) before that, eventually it is to Us that they shall be brought back.

77.author: Mubarakpuri
77.text: So be patient, verily, the promise of Allah is true and whether We show you some part of what We have promised them, or We cause you to die, then still it is to Us they all shall be returned.

77.author: Pickthall
77.text: Then have patience (O Muhammad). Lo! the promise of Allah is true. And whether we let thee see a part of that which We promise them, or (whether) We cause thee to die, still unto Us they will be brought back.

77.author: Qarai
77.text: So be patient! Allah’s promise is indeed true. Whether We show you a part of what We promise them, or take you away [before that], [in any case] they will be brought back to Us.

77.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
77.text: Therefore have patience, the Promise of Allah is true. Whether We show you something of that We promise them or call you to Us, to Us they shall all return.

77.author: Saheeh International
77.text: So be patient, [O Muhammad]; indeed, the promise of Allah is truth. And whether We show you some of what We have promised them or We take you in death, it is to Us they will be returned.

77.author: Sarwar
