77.text: (Muhammad), exercise patience. The promise of God is true. Whether We let you witness the suffering with which they were threatened or because of your death (you do not see them suffering,), We shall still punish them when they return to Us (on the Day of Judgment).

77.author: Shakir
77.text: So be patient, surely the promise of Allah is true. So should We make you see part of what We threaten them with, or should We cause you to die, to Us shall they be returned.

77.author: Wahiduddin Khan
77.text: So be patient [Prophet], for God's promise is true: whether We show you part of what We have promised them in this life, or cause you to die first, it is to Us that they will be recalled.

77.author: Yusuf Ali
77.text: So persevere in patience; for the Promise of Allah is true: and whether We show thee (in this life) some part of what We promise them,- or We take thy soul (to Our Mercy) (before that),-(in any case) it is to Us that they shall (all) return.

77.author: Transliteration
77.text: Faisbir inna waAAda Allahi haqqun faimma nuriyannaka baAAda allathee naAAiduhum aw natawaffayannaka failayna yurjaAAoona

78.author: Ahmed Ali
78.text: Surely We have sent apostles before you, some of whose account We have related to you, and that of some We have not told you. But no apostle was given a miracle unless God dispensed. But when the decree of God comes the sentence is passed with justice; and the lovers of vice and vanity will then come to grief.

78.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
78.text: Indeed We sent many Noble Messengers before you, so We have related to you the affairs of some among them, and not related the affairs of some; and no Noble Messenger has the right to bring any sign except with the command of Allah; so the time when the command of Allah comes, the true judgement will be delivered and there will the people of falsehood be ruined.

78.author: Arberry
78.text: We sent Messengers before thee; of some We have related to thee, and some We have not related to thee. It was not for any Messenger to bring a sign, save by God's leave. When God's command comes, justly the issue shall be decided; then the vain-doers shall be lost.

78.author: Daryabadi
78.text: Assuredly We have sent apostlesy before thee, of them are these whose store We have recounted Unto thee and these whose story We have net recounted Unto thee. And it was not possible for anv apostle that he should bring a sign san by Allah's leave. So when the commade of Allah cometh, judgment will be given with truth, and then will lose the followers of falsehood.

78.author: Hilali & Khan
78.text: And, indeed We have sent Messengers before you (O Muhammad SAW); of some of them We have related to you their story and of some We have not related to you their story, and it was not given to any Messenger that he should bring a sign except by the Leave of Allah. So, when comes the Commandment of Allah, the matter will be decided with truth, and the followers of falsehood will then be lost.

78.author: Itani
78.text: We sent messengers before you. Some of them We told you about, and some We did not tell you about. No messenger can bring a miracle except by leave of God. Then, when the command of God is issued, fair judgment will be passed, and there and then the seekers of vanity will lose.

78.author: Maududi
78.text: Indeed We sent many Messengers before you: of them there are some whose account We have narrated to you and there are others whose account We have not narrated to you. It did not lie in any Messenger's power to bring any Sign except with Allah's leave. So when Allah's decree came, the matter was decided with justice, and those steeped in error courted utter loss, then and there.

78.author: Mubarakpuri
78.text: And, indeed We have sent Messengers before you, of some of them We have related to you their story. And of some We have not related to you their story, and it was not given to any Messenger that he should bring a sign except by the leave of Allah. But, when comes the commandment of Allah, the matter will be decided with truth, and the followers of falsehood will then be lost.

78.author: Pickthall
78.text: Verily We sent messengers before thee, among them those of whom We have told thee, and some of whom We have not told thee; and it was not given to any messenger that he should bring a portent save by Allah's leave, but when Allah's commandment cometh (the cause) is judged aright, and the followers of vanity will then be lost.

78.author: Qarai
78.text: Certainly We have sent apostles before you. Of them are those We have recounted to you, and of them are those We have not recounted to you. An apostle may not bring any sign except by Allah’s permission. Hence, when Allah’s edict comes, judgment is made with justice, and it is thence that the falsifiers become losers.

78.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
78.text: We sent other Messengers before you; of some, We have already told you, of others We have not told you. Yet it was not for any Messenger to bring a verse except by the permission of Allah. And when the command of Allah comes the matter will be justly decided; and the vaindoers will be lost.

78.author: Saheeh International
78.text: And We have already sent messengers before you. Among them are those [whose stories] We have related to you, and among them are those [whose stories] We have not related to you. And it was not for any messenger to bring a sign [or verse] except by permission of Allah. So when the command of Allah comes, it will be concluded in truth, and the falsifiers will thereupon lose [all].

78.author: Sarwar
78.text: We have told you the stories of some of Our Messengers whom We had sent before you and We have not told you the stories of some others. A Messenger is not supposed to show a miracle without the permission of God. When God's decree of punishment comes to pass, He will judge truthfully and the supporters of the falsehood will perish when God's decree of punishemnt comes to pass.

78.author: Shakir
78.text: And certainly We sent apostles before you: there are some of them that We have mentioned to you and there are others whom We have not mentioned to you, and it was not meet for an apostle that he should bring a sign except with Allah's permission, but when the command of Allah came, judgment was given with truth, and those who treated (it) as a lie were lost.

78.author: Wahiduddin Khan
78.text: Before your time We sent other messengers: of them there are some whose story We have related to you, and some whose story We have not related to you. It was not [possible] for any messenger to bring a sign except by the leave of God: but when the command of God was issued, the matter was decided in truth and justice. There and then, those who stood on falsehoods perished.

78.author: Yusuf Ali
78.text: We did aforetime send messengers before thee: of them there are some whose story We have related to thee, and some whose story We have not related to thee. It was not (possible) for any messenger to bring a sign except by the leave of Allah: but when the Command of Allah issued, the matter was decided in truth and justice, and there perished, there and then those who stood on Falsehoods.

78.author: Transliteration
78.text: Walaqad arsalna rusulan min qablika minhum man qasasna AAalayka waminhum man lam naqsus AAalayka wama kana lirasoolin an yatiya biayatin illa biithni Allahi faitha jaa amru Allahi qudiya bialhaqqi wakhasira hunalika almubtiloona

79.author: Ahmed Ali
79.text: It is God who made the cattle for you so that some you ride and some you eat.

79.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
79.text: It is Allah Who created the animals for you, in order for you to ride some of them, and some to eat.

79.author: Arberry
79.text: It is God who appointed for you the cattle, some of them to ride and of some you eat;

79.author: Daryabadi
79.text: Allah it is Who hath appointed for you cattle, that ye may ride on some of them, and eat of others

79.author: Hilali & Khan
79.text: Allah, it is He Who has made cattle for you, that you may ride on some of them and of some you eat.

79.author: Itani
79.text: God is He who created the domestic animals for you—some for you to ride, and some you eat.

79.author: Maududi
79.text: Allah it is Who has made cattle for you so that you ride some of them and from some of them you derive food.

79.author: Mubarakpuri
79.text: Allah, it is He Who has made cattle for you, that you may ride on some of them, and of some you eat.

79.author: Pickthall
79.text: Allah it is Who hath appointed for you cattle, that ye may ride on some of them, and eat of some -

79.author: Qarai
79.text: It is Allah who created the cattle for you that you may ride some of them, and some of them you eat;

79.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
79.text: It is Allah who has provided you with cattle, some that you ride and some that you eat,

79.author: Saheeh International
79.text: It is Allah who made for you the grazing animals upon which you ride, and some of them you eat.

79.author: Sarwar
79.text: It is God Who has created cattle for you to ride and to consume as food.

79.author: Shakir
79.text: Allah is He Who made the cattle for you that you may ride on some of them, and some of them you eat.

79.author: Wahiduddin Khan
79.text: It is God who provides livestock for you, some for riding and some for your food:

79.author: Yusuf Ali
79.text: It is Allah Who made cattle for you, that ye may use some for riding and some for food;

79.author: Transliteration
79.text: Allahu allathee jaAAala lakumu alanAAama litarkaboo minha waminha takuloona

80.author: Ahmed Ali
80.text: There are advantages for you in them, so that you may satisfy your needs through them, and may be borne upon them and on ships.

80.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
80.text: And in them are numerous benefits for you, and for you to reach your hearts’ desires while riding them – and you ride upon them and upon the ships.

80.author: Arberry
80.text: other uses also you have in them; and that on them you may attain a need in your breasts, and upon them and on the ships you are carried.

80.author: Daryabadi
80.text: And there are other benefits in them for you and that ye may attain thereby to any need that is in Your breasts and upon them and upon ships ye are borne.

80.author: Hilali & Khan
80.text: And you have (many other) benefits from them, and that you may reach by their means a desire that is in your breasts (i.e. carry your goods, loads, etc.), and on them and on ships you are carried.

80.author: Itani
80.text: And in them you have other benefits as well, and through them you satisfy your needs. And on them, and on the ships, you are transported.

80.author: Maududi
80.text: In them there are also other benefits for you, and through them you fulfil your heartfelt need (to reach places), and you are borne along upon them as upon the ships.

80.author: Mubarakpuri
80.text: And you have (many other) benefits from them, and that you may reach by their means a desire that is in your breasts, and on them and on ships you are carried.

80.author: Pickthall
80.text: (Many) benefits ye have from them - and that ye may satisfy by their means a need that is in your breasts, and may be borne upon them as upon the ship.

80.author: Qarai
80.text: and there are [numerous] uses in them for you, and that over them you may satisfy any need that is in your breasts, and you are carried on them and on ships.

80.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
80.text: and for you there are other uses in them, and that upon them you may reach a need in your chests, and upon them and on ships you are carried.

80.author: Saheeh International
80.text: And for you therein are [other] benefits and that you may realize upon them a need which is in your breasts; and upon them and upon ships you are carried.

80.author: Sarwar
80.text: You may also obtain other benefits from them. You may ride them to seek whatever you need or be carried by them as ships carry you by sea.

80.author: Shakir
80.text: And there are advantages for you in them, and that you may attain thereon a want which is in your breasts, and upon them and upon the ships you are borne.

80.author: Wahiduddin Khan
80.text: you have other benefits in them too. You can reach any destination you wish on them: they carry you by land, as ships carry you on the sea.

80.author: Yusuf Ali
80.text: And there are (other) advantages in them for you (besides); that ye may through them attain to any need (there may be) in your hearts; and on them and on ships ye are carried.

80.author: Transliteration
80.text: Walakum feeha manafiAAu walitablughoo AAalayha hajatan fee sudoorikum waAAalayha waAAala alfulki tuhmaloona

81.author: Ahmed Ali
81.text: He shows His signs. How many of God's signs will you then deny?

81.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
81.text: And He shows you His signs; so which sign of Allah will you deny?

81.author: Arberry
81.text: And He shows you His signs; then which of God's signs do you reject?

81.author: Daryabadi
81.text: And He sheweth you His signs. Which, then, of the signs of Allah shall ya deny?

81.author: Hilali & Khan
81.text: And He shows you His Signs and Proofs (of His Oneness in all the above mentioned things). Which, then of the Signs and Proofs of Allah do you deny?

81.author: Itani
81.text: And He shows you His signs. So which of God’s signs will you deny?

81.author: Maududi
81.text: Allah shows His Signs to you; then which of Allah's Signs will you deny?

81.author: Mubarakpuri
81.text: And He shows you His Ayat. Which, then of the Ayat of Allah do you deny

81.author: Pickthall
81.text: And He showeth you His tokens. Which, then, of the tokens of Allah do ye deny?

81.author: Qarai
81.text: He shows you His signs. So which of the signs of Allah do you deny?

81.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
81.text: He shows you His signs. Now which of the signs of Allah do you disbelieve?

81.author: Saheeh International
81.text: And He shows you His signs. So which of the signs of Allah do you deny?

81.author: Sarwar
81.text: God shows you the evidence (of His existence). How can you then deny such evidence?

81.author: Shakir
81.text: And He shows you His signs: which then of Allah's signs will you deny?

81.author: Wahiduddin Khan
81.text: He shows you His signs; which then of the signs of God will you deny?

81.author: Yusuf Ali
81.text: And He shows you (always) His Signs: then which of the Signs of Allah will ye deny?

81.author: Transliteration
81.text: Wayureekum ayatihi faayya ayati Allahi tunkiroona

82.author: Ahmed Ali
82.text: Have they not travelled in the land and seen how was the end of those before them who were far more numerous than they and more strong, and have left behind them traces on the earth. And yet nothing of what they did profited them.

82.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
82.text: Did they not travel in the land to see what sort of fate befell those before them? They were more than these in number, and they exceeded them in strength and the signs they left behind in the earth – so what benefit did they get from what they earned?

82.author: Arberry
82.text: What, have they not journeyed in the land and beheld how was the end of those before them? They were stronger than themselves in might and left firmer traces in the earth; yet that they earned did not avail them.

82.author: Daryabadi
82.text: Have they not travelled in the earth so that they may behold what wise hath been the end of those before them. They were more numerous than these, and mightier in strength and the traces in the land. But naught availed them of that which they had been earning.

82.author: Hilali & Khan
82.text: Have they not travelled through the earth and seen what was the end of those before them? They were more numerous than them and mightier in strength, and in the traces (they have left behind them) in the land, yet all that they used to earn availed them not.

82.author: Itani
82.text: Have they not journeyed through the land, and seen the outcome for those before them? They were more numerous than they, and had greater power and influence in the land. But what they had achieved availed them nothing.

82.author: Maududi
82.text: Did they not journey in the land that they may behold the end of those who had gone before them? They were more numerous and greater in strength and left behind more splendid traces in the land. Yet their attainments did not avail them.

82.author: Mubarakpuri
82.text: Have they not traveled through the earth and seen what was the end of those before them They were more in number than them and mightier in strength, and in the traces in the land; yet all that they used to earn availed them not.

82.author: Pickthall
82.text: Have they not travelled in the land to see the nature of the consequence for those before them? They were more numerous than these, and mightier in power and (in the) traces (which they left behind them) in the earth. But all that they used to earn availed them not.

82.author: Qarai
82.text: Have they not travelled over the land so that they may observe how was the fate of those who were before them? They were more numerous than them and were greater [than them] in power and with respect to the effects [they left] in the land. But what they used to earn did not avail them.

82.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
82.text: Have they never journeyed through the land and seen what was the end of those who have gone before them? They were stronger than themselves in might and left firmer traces upon the earth; yet, whatsoever they earned did not help them.

82.author: Saheeh International
82.text: Have they not traveled through the land and observed how was the end of those before them? They were more numerous than themselves and greater in strength and in impression on the land, but they were not availed by what they used to earn.

82.author: Sarwar
82.text: Have they not travelled through the land to see the terrible end of those who lived before them? They were far mightier in both number and power and in what they had established. Their (worldly) gains were of no benefit to them.

82.author: Shakir
82.text: Have they not then journeyed in the land and seen how was the end of those before them? They were more (in numbers) than these and greater in strength and in fortifications in the land, but what they earned did not avail them.

82.author: Wahiduddin Khan
82.text: Have they not travelled in the land to see the fate of those who went before them? They were more numerous and mightier and left greater traces of their power on the earth; yet all that they accomplished was of no avail to them.

82.author: Yusuf Ali
82.text: Do they not travel through the earth and see what was the End of those before them? They were more numerous than these and superior in strength and in the traces (they have left) in the land: Yet all that they accomplished was of no profit to them.

82.author: Transliteration
82.text: Afalam yaseeroo fee alardi fayanthuroo kayfa kana AAaqibatu allatheena min qablihim kanoo akthara minhum waashadda quwwatan waatharan fee alardi fama aghna AAanhum ma kanoo yaksiboona

83.author: Ahmed Ali
83.text: For when Our apostles came to them with clear proofs, they boasted and exulted at the knowledge they possessed; but what they used to mock recoiled back on them,

83.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
83.text: So when their Noble Messengers came to them with clear signs, they remained happy over the worldly knowledge they possessed, and upon them only reverted what they used to mock at!

83.author: Arberry
83.text: So, when their Messengers brought them the clear signs, they rejoiced in what knowledge they had, and were encompassed by that they mocked at.

83.author: Daryabadi
83.text: And when their apostles came Unto them with evidences, they exulted in the knowledge which was with them, and there surrounded them that which they had been mocking.

83.author: Hilali & Khan
83.text: Then when their Messengers came to them with clear proofs, they were glad (and proud) with that which they had of the knowledge (of worldly things): And that at which they used to mock, surrounded them (i.e. the punishment).

83.author: Itani
83.text: When their messengers came to them with clear proofs, they rejoiced in the knowledge they had, and the very things they used to ridicule besieged them.

83.author: Maududi
83.text: When their Messengers came to them with Clear Signs, they arrogantly exulted in whatever knowledge they had. They were then encompassed by what they had mocked.

83.author: Mubarakpuri
83.text: Then when their Messengers came to them with clear proofs, they were glad (and proud) with that which they had of the knowledge (of worldly things). And that at which they used to mock, surrounded them.

83.author: Pickthall
83.text: And when their messengers brought them clear proofs (of Allah's Sovereignty) they exulted in the knowledge they (themselves) possessed. And that which they were wont to mock befell them.

83.author: Qarai
83.text: When their apostles brought them manifest proofs, they boasted about the knowledge they possessed, but they were besieged by what they used to deride.

83.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
83.text: When their Messengers brought them clear verses they rejoiced in such knowledge as they had; but they were encompassed by that which they mocked.

83.author: Saheeh International
83.text: And when their messengers came to them with clear proofs, they [merely] rejoiced in what they had of knowledge, but they were enveloped by what they used to ridicule.

83.author: Sarwar
83.text: They were far too content with their own knowledge (to pay attention to the Messengers) when Our Messengers came to them with illustrious evidence. They were encompassed by the torment for mocking Our guidance.

83.author: Shakir
83.text: Then when their apostles came to them with clear arguments, they exulted in what they had with them of knowledge, and there beset them that which they used to mock.

83.author: Wahiduddin Khan
83.text: When messengers came to them with clear signs, they revelled in whatever knowledge they had, and so they were engulfed by the very punishment they mocked:

83.author: Yusuf Ali
83.text: For when their messengers came to them with Clear Signs, they exulted in such knowledge (and skill) as they had; but that very (Wrath) at which they were wont to scoff hemmed them in.

83.author: Transliteration
83.text: Falamma jaathum rusuluhum bialbayyinati farihoo bima AAindahum mina alAAilmi wahaqa bihim ma kanoo bihi yastahzioona

84.author: Ahmed Ali
84.text: So that when they saw Our might, they said: "We believe in God the one and single, and reject those we associated (with Him)." --

84.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
84.text: So when they saw Our punishment, they said, “We accept faith in the One Allah, and reject those whom we ascribed with Him.”

84.author: Arberry
84.text: Then, when they saw Our might, they said, 'We believe in God alone, and we disbelieve in that we were associating with Him.'

84.author: Daryabadi
84.text: Then when they behold Our prowess, they said: we believe in Allah alone, and we disbelieve in that which with Him we have been associating.

84.author: Hilali & Khan
84.text: So when they saw Our punishment, they said: "We believe in Allah Alone and reject (all) that we used to associate with Him as (His) partners.

84.author: Itani
84.text: Then, when they witnessed Our might, they said, “We believe in God alone, and we reject what we used to associate with Him.”

84.author: Maududi
84.text: When they saw Our chastisement, they said: “We have come to believe in Allah, the Only One, and we reject all what we had associated (with Allah in His Divinity).”

84.author: Mubarakpuri
84.text: So when they saw Our punishment, they said: "We believe in Allah Alone and reject (all) that we used to associate with Him as (His) partners."

84.author: Pickthall
84.text: Then, when they saw Our doom, they said: We believe in Allah only and reject (all) that we used to associate (with Him).

84.author: Qarai
84.text: Then, when they sighted Our punishment, they said, ‘We believe in Allah alone, and disavow what we used to take as His partners.’

84.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
84.text: And when they saw Our Might, they said: 'We believe in Allah alone, and we disbelieve in that we used to associate with Him'

84.author: Saheeh International
84.text: And when they saw Our punishment, they said," We believe in Allah alone and disbelieve in that which we used to associate with Him."

84.author: Sarwar
84.text: They said, "We believe in One God and disbelieve in whatever we had considered equal to Him," when they experienced Our wrath.

84.author: Shakir
84.text: But when they saw Our punishment, they said: We believe in Allah alone and we deny what we used to associate with Him.

84.author: Wahiduddin Khan
84.text: but when they saw Our punishment, they said, "We believe in God, the One God, and we reject the partners we used to associate with Him,"

84.author: Yusuf Ali
84.text: But when they saw Our Punishment, they said: "We believe in Allah,- the one Allah - and we reject the partners we used to join with Him."

84.author: Transliteration
84.text: Falamma raaw basana qaloo amanna biAllahi wahdahu wakafarna bima kunna bihi mushrikeena

85.author: Ahmed Ali
85.text: But then their affirming served them nothing after they had seen Our torment. This is the law of God that has prevailed among His creatures, Then the unbelievers went to rack and ruin.

85.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
85.text: So their accepting of faith did not benefit them when they saw Our punishment; the tradition of Allah which has passed among His bondmen; and there were the disbelievers ruined.

85.author: Arberry
85.text: But their belief when they saw Our might did not profit them -- the wont of God, as in the past, touching His servants; then the unbelievers shall be lost.

85.author: Daryabadi
85.text: But their belief profited them naught when they saw our prowess. This hath been Allah's dispensation that hath- been in regard to His bondmen. And then there were lost the infidels.

85.author: Hilali & Khan
85.text: Then their Faith (in Islamic Monotheism) could not avail them when they saw Our punishment. (Like) this has been the way of Allah in dealing with His slaves. And there the disbelievers lost utterly (when Our Torment covered them).

85.author: Itani
85.text: But their faith could not help them once they witnessed Our might. This has been God’s way of dealing with His servants. And there and then the disbelievers lost.

85.author: Maududi
85.text: But their believing after they had seen Our chastisement did not avail them. That has been Allah's Way concerning His servants. And the unbelievers courted utter loss, then and there.

85.author: Mubarakpuri
85.text: Then their Faith could not avail them when they saw Our punishment. (Like) this has been the way of Allah in dealing with His servants. And there the disbelievers lost utterly.

85.author: Pickthall
85.text: But their faith could not avail them when they saw Our doom. This is Allah's law which hath ever taken course for His bondmen. And then the disbelievers will be ruined.

85.author: Qarai
85.text: But their faith was of no benefit to them when they sighted Our punishment—Allah’s precedent, which has passed among His servants, and it is thence that the faithless will be losers.

85.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
85.text: But when they saw Our Might their belief did not benefit them! It is the way of Allah that has passed concerning His worshipers. There, the unbelievers shall be lost.

85.author: Saheeh International
85.text: But never did their faith benefit them once they saw Our punishment. [It is] the established way of Allah which has preceded among His servants. And the disbelievers thereupon lost [all].

85.author: Sarwar
85.text: Their faith proved to be of no benefit to them when they became subject to Our torment. Such was God's prevailing tradition among His servants in the past. Thus were the unbelievers destroyed.

85.author: Shakir
85.text: But their belief was not going to profit them when they had seen Our punishment; (this is) Allah's law, which has indeed obtained in the matter of His servants, and there the unbelievers are lost.

85.author: Wahiduddin Khan
85.text: but believing after seeing Our punishment did not benefit them at all: this is the law of God to deal with His creatures, and thus the disbelievers were the losers.

85.author: Yusuf Ali
85.text: But their professing the Faith when they (actually) saw Our Punishment was not going to profit them. (Such has been) Allah's Way of dealing with His Servants (from the most ancient times). And even thus did the Rejecters of Allah perish (utterly)!

85.author: Transliteration
85.text: Falam yaku yanfaAAuhum eemanuhum lamma raaw basana sunnata Allahi allatee qad khalat fee AAibadihi wakhasira hunalika alkafiroona
